校.equation: tree 木 (10a) + pay 交 (380) = school 校
校.mnemonic:
In the village there is a special tree, and the villagers make people pay to see it, using
the money to build a school. / Two dwarves go round the local area like town criers,
shouting out news about the tree and drumming up business.

住.equation: person 亻 (13a) + lord 主 (297) = live in 住
住.mnemonic:
Harry is made a lord and given an official house to live in. / The dwarf supervises
the placing of the lordly jewel above the front door.

在.equation: talent 才 (55a) + earth 土 (54a) = at 在
在.mnemonic:
(An announcement at the flower show) “the expert gardeners, who have a special
talent for growing things in the earth, will be at the main marquee to answer
questions when the big “@” sign is displayed above it.” / The dwarf (who is
organizing things) plays a couple of notes on his xylophone to end the
announcement.

里.equation: field 田 (156a) + earth 土 (54a) = in 里
里.mnemonic:
“See that field with small round humps of earth? — that’s the potato field.” “But I
don’t see any potatoes!” “That’s because they’re in the humps!” / Teddy runs to leap
on the humps (crying, “Let’
s make mashed potatoes!”)

面.equation: thumb tack (40a) + enclosed 囗 (24a) + eye 目 (93) = face 面
面.mnemonic:
(The children had been throwing flour at each other and some went in the girl’s eye)
they used a thumb tack to secure the bandages enclosing the eye, which made her
face look very odd! / The two dwarves grumbled to each other,
“These children are
a right menace,” as they cleaned up the mess.

又.mnemonic:
/ Two friends on unicycles meet in the street — each extends his right hand while
balancing on a single wheel, and they shake hands again and again. The dwarf
comes rushing past (nearly sending them flying) — he’
s late for his yoga class and
in no mood to be impressed.

方.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + bow (51a) = direction 方
方.mnemonic:
(The vet stops to ask the way from a strange old man who is sitting outside his house)
The old man has a lid on his head (instead of a hat) and silently bows to show which
direction the vet must go. / The vet is off to meet the giant to attend to his dog,
Fang. [Fang will appear in the next few stories: picture him as a huge soppy dog].

便.equation: 1. person 亻 (13a) + even more 更 (399) = advantageous 便
便.mnemonic:
In the saloon Harry had had a lot to drink, but his opponent had drunk even more—
harry would find this advantageous if it came to a gunfight. / The two fairies wanted
to avoid bloodshed so each conjured up a bullet-proof vest so that any bullets
wouldn’t penetrate.

更.mnemonic:
/ (At the tire shop) Even more wheels than yesterday are being returned by the
customers. The dwarf orders them to be coated with gunk and resold …

房.equation: door 户 (269) + direction 方 (291) = house 房
房.mnemonic:
You knock on the door to ask directions to your friend’s house. / The fairy opens the
door and casts a spell on Fang the dog to enable him to show you the way.

租.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + bookcase 且 (443) = rent 租
租.mnemonic:
Some grain is left on the bookcase every month to pay the rent. / The giant landlord
accepts this from his Zulu lodger (but would rather have money).

贵.equation: shopping basket + sea shell 贝 (477a) = expensive 贵
贵.mnemonic:
On the beach the girl collects sea shells in her shopping basket— they’d be
expensive if bought in the shop. / The ghostly dwarf who guards the beach sits on
the gate (on the path to the beach) watching to make sure she doesn’t take too many.

些.equation: this 此 (314a) + two 二 (2) = few 些
些.mnemonic:
(Air show commentary) “This biplane is one of the few of its type remaining. / The
two giants kindly carried it all the way from their shed so it can be here today.”

贝.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + person 人 (12) = sea shell 贝
贝.mnemonic:
Harry journeyed to the outer limits of the kingdom carrying the magic sea shell (for
protection against the bandits). / [No pronunciation needed]

过.equation: road 辶 (26a) + inch 寸 (104a) = cross 过
过.mnemonic:
The snail on the road can only travel an inch every hour so it takes all day to cross the
road. / The ghostly dwarf, who’s been assigned to protect him, urges him on,
“Go,
go, go!” (he wants to get home for his tea).

凶.equation: shears 乂 (25a) + pit 凵 (626a) = terrible 凶
凶.mnemonic:
If you’ve dropped your shears down the pit, that’s terrible. (You’ll have to use
scissors to cut the grass now!) / [No pronunciation needed]

离.mnemonic:
“The brain and rump of a giraffe are very distant from each other.” / To demonstrate,
the fairy pokes the giraffe in the rump with her wand. “Now let’
s see how long he
takes to leap up in surprise.”

市.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + towel 巾 (427a) = market 市
市.mnemonic:
He puts a lid on the box of towels (before loading it onto the cart) to take it to market.
/ Last time the towels had got wet and the dwarf had hit him with a shillelagh!

斤.mnemonic:
/ The giant is sharpening his axe on the grinding wheel, and has a swig of gin every
so often to keep up his strength!

近.equation: road 辶 (26a) + axe 斤 (240) = close 近
近.mnemonic:
An axe was lying in the road and he swerved dramatically to avoid it.
close!” / In the passenger seat the dwarf had a swift swig of his gin to recover!
“that was close !” / In the passenger seat the dwarf had a swift swig of his gin to recover!

很.equation: step forward 彳 (81a) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = very 很
很.mnemonic:
(V olunteers are sought for a dangerous mission to capture the dragon) Sir Galahad
stepped forward stubbornly; he was very sure he wanted to do it. / Teddy jumps
around excitedly singing, “A dragon hunt, a dragon hunt!”

般.equation: boat 舟 (680a) + strike 殳 (169a) = sort 般
般.mnemonic:
At the launching ceremony they strike the boat with a bottle, but what sort of bottle
depends on what sort of boat it is. / The giant strikes up the band as the boat is
launched.

搬.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + sort 般 (680) = move 搬
搬.mnemonic:
“We need a hand.” “What sort of hand?” “any sort we can get, to help us move
house.” / The giant brings his band of helpers (and they finish the move in no time).

京.equation: tall (239a) + small 小 (50) = capital 京
京.mnemonic: There were both tall and small buildings in the capital. / [No pronunciation needed]

就.equation: capital 京 (239b) + especially 尤 (238) = right away 就
就.mnemonic:
In the capital it is especially important to get on the train right away. / The two dwarf
attendants don’t like people joking around (as everyones in a rush).

尤.mnemonic: The fairy found the wheel useful, especially in her yoga class.

犬.equation: big 大 (47) + a drop 丶 (22a) = dog 犬
犬.mnemonic:
The big fisherman felt a drop of water on his arm (but there was no cloud in the sky).
the dog was shaking itself after having been in the river. / [No pronunciation needed]

免.equation: bow (51a) + kiss (671a) + boy 儿 (21) = avoid 免
免.mnemonic:
The aunt bows down to kiss the boy— but he ducks away to avoid her (as he hates
sloppy kisses from relatives). / [No pronunciation needed]

晚.equation: sun 日 (6) + avoid 免 (673a) = evening 晚
晚.mnemonic:
The albino animal has to avoid the sun, so only comes out in the evening. / [DIY
pronunciation]

象.equation: bow (51a) + kiss (671a) + pig 豕 (109a) = elephant 象
象.mnemonic:
The princess bows down to kiss the pig— who promptly turns into a handsome …
elephant! / [No pronunciation needed]

音.equation: stand 立 (177) + sun 日 (6) = sound 音
音.mnemonic:
“If you stand this in the sun for a while it will make a deep, resonant sound. / But the
giant won’t like it because it upsets his yin!”

立.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + feet (90a) = stand 立
立.mnemonic:
He was trying to close the lid of his bulging suitcase, but in the end he had to use his
feet and stand on it. / When he got home, the dwarf realized he’d crushed his sun-
cream, which had leaked out (and ruined his hat).

意.equation: sound 音 (257) + heart 心 (161) = idea 意
意.mnemonic:
If we listen to the sound of your heart, this will give us a better idea of what is going
on,” the surgeon said. / The dwarf stands by with ether for anesthetic.

见.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + boy 儿 (21) = see 见
见.mnemonic:
(On a bus tour of the outer limits) In the outer limits the boy wants to go to the loo but
won’t go behind a bush.
“Someone might see me!”
. [Notice the boy’s legs are held
together in the character!] / The two dwarf passengers decide that gentlemen don’t
go in the bushes, so they will try to wait until the toilets at the next stop.

斥.equation: axe 斤 (240) + splinter (695a) = drive out 斥
斥.mnemonic:
The gang use an axe to chop splinters out of the door of anyone they want to drive out
of the neighborhood. / No pronunciation needed

诉.equation: words 讠 (67c) + drive out 斥 (719a) = tell 诉
诉.mnemonic:
The lurid words scrawled on the wall urge people to drive out any foreigners; the
authorities have asked people to tell the police if they know who was responsible. /
The dwarf police official will be stationed at the supermarket (ready to take
reports).

冈.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + shears 乂 (25a) = ridge 冈
冈.mnemonic:
In the outer limits they use shears to trim the hedges on the ridge of the hill, so that
they can peer over into the neighboring kingdom. / [No pronunciation needed]

刚.equation: ridge 冈 (676a) + knife 刂 (74a) = barely 刚
刚.mnemonic:
Imagine crawling up to the ridge (at dusk, to investigate a suspicious noise), knife at
the ready, but there’s barely enough light to see what’s going on. / When you peer over
you see the giant and his gang making camp.

听.equation: mouth 口 (5) + axe 斤 (240) = listen 听
听.mnemonic:
At the mouth of the cave he was working away with an axe (to clear the trees which
had blocked it) when he thought he heard something and stopped to listen. / A tingle
ran up the giant’s spine.

足.equation: mouth 口 (5) + stop (30a) = foot 足
足.mnemonic:
At the mouth of the cave they all stopped — nobody dared put a foot inside. / Only
when the fairy zoomed in to check it out did they relax.

跟.equation: foot 足 (280) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = with 跟
跟.mnemonic:
The landowner puts his foot
stubbornly in the door (at the police station).
“I’m going to stay here until you come
with me. / Theres a giant on my land threatening to blow things up with
gunpowder!”

眼.equation: eye 目 (93) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = eye 眼
眼.mnemonic:
The little girl plucked at the stuffed donkey’s one remaining eye but it stubbornly
refused to budge.
“I can’t have a donkey with one eye!” / Teddy had always had a yen
to be a surgeon and found a new pair of eyes for it.

酒.equation: water 氵 (78a) + whisky bottle 酉 (102a) = liquor 酒

走.mnemonic:
(On a mountain path there is a landslide and soil is pouring down across the path) He
had to wait for the earth to stop moving before he could walk any further. / Teddy was
bouncing around, saying,
“I should have come with someone who had the right
zodiac sign!”

起.equation: walk 走 (275) + snake 巳 (275a) = rise up 起
起.mnemonic:
You take your pet snake for a walk— you’ve trained it to rise up to greet your friends.
/ Teddy wants to take a photo of it. “Say Cheese!” he says.

年.mnemonic:
“These clouds surpass anything we’ve seen all year.” (picture a group of glider pilots
looking at the sky and judging which clouds will give the best thermal up-currents). /
[DIY pronunciation]

殳.equation: several 几 (7) + right hand 又 (52) = strike 殳
殳.mnemonic:
(An attempt at a world record) The challenger shakes hands with several of the
dignitaries who wish him luck, and then sees how many garden gnomes he can knock
over with a single strike with his long pole. / [No pronunciation needed]

没.equation: water 氵 (78a) + strike 殳 (169a) = not 没
没.mnemonic:
(At the funfair) at one sideshow he had to strike, with a pole, anything that appeared
out of the water, but everything stayed submerged and he complained, “There is not a
thing to hit!” / The fairy suggested to him that he should try his luck at the maze
instead.

广.mnemonic:
/ (At the Formula 1 team
’
s secret test track) They have to build a shelter for the
spare wheels and other equipment, broad enough to accommodate the ghostly teddy
and his gang (who guard it all).

床.equation: shelter 广 (170) + tree 木 (10a) = bed 床
床.mnemonic: He took a tree into the shelter to make a bed. / [DIY pronunciation]

长.mnemonic:
/ On the bridge of the spaceship the captain looked at the long row of wheels on
the engineer
’
s desk. He had no idea which one to turn, so sent the fairy flitting off
to find Mr Chang, the engineer.

思.equation: field 田 (156a) + heart 心 (161) = think 思
思.mnemonic:
One of the farmer’s fields is heart -shaped and he always goes there to think. / The
giant had brought a (giant!) sequoia so that the farmer could sit under it (with his
back against the trunk) to do his thinking.

交.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + father 父 (367) = pay 交
交.mnemonic:
The boy bangs a saucepan lid by his father’s ear.
“Pay me my pocket money!” / But
the father only grunts — he’
s watching the two giants jousting on TV.

较.equation: car 车 (83) + pay 交 (380) = comparison 较
较.mnemonic:
(At the freeway toll booths) The cars are queuing up to pay, with the drivers in the
two queues making comparisons about how long they had been there. / The queues
are building up because the two dwarves, who man the two toll booths, are both
watching the jousting on their TV sets.

丸.equation: nine 九 (38) + splinter (695a) = pellet 丸
丸.mnemonic:
A youngster is sitting idly in the sun carving splinters off an old baseball, until what is
left is just a pellet. / [No pronunciation needed]

执.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + pellet 丸 (695b) = hold on to 执
执.mnemonic:
The dead man had a pellet in his hand— he was holding on to it as if it was
important. (perhaps it’s a clue to a homicide!) / [No pronunciation needed]

热.equation: hold on to 执 (695c) + fire 灬 (185a) = hot 热
热.mnemonic:
You hold on to the metal poker while stoking the fire, but it becomes so hot that you
drop it. / The dwarf growls, “Rrr … rug! You’ve burnt a hole in my rr..rug!”

太.equation: big 大 (47) + a drop 丶 (22a) = too much 太
太.mnemonic:
The fisherman throws his arms wide to show how big the fish was, but a drop of
sweat falls from his brow and his friends know he is lying — they have seen too much
of this in the past. / The dwarf says, disdainfully, “Are you sure it wasn’t a tiger?”

元.equation: one 一 (1) + pedestal 兀 (301a) = yuan 元
元.mnemonic:
In the town square there is a statue of a unicorn on a pedestal; people throw yuan
coins onto the pedestal for good luck. / At night the fairy comes to collect the yuan
coins for charity.

园.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + yuan 元 (301) = garden 园
园.mnemonic:
The historic yuan coin is enclosed in a display case in the memorial Chinese garden.
/ The fairy sets up a souvenir stall to sell replica yuan coins.

远.equation: road 辶 (26a) + yuan 元 (301) = distant 远
远.mnemonic:
You can see the yuan coins lying on the road sparkling into the distance like cat’s
eyes. / T eddy tries to pick up all the yuan coins but can’t hold them all.

各.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + mouth 口 (5) = each 各
各.mnemonic:
The police pursue the thieves to the mouth of the cave and then stand guard at each of
the exits. / The dwarf is determined to retrieve the stolen gherkins!

路.equation: foot 足 (280) + each 各 (507) = path 路
路.mnemonic:
(At the fork in the road) “If you really can’t decide which way to go, try putting a foot
on each path.” / “This is lunatic,” says the dwarf.

民.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + pile of earth (455a) = the people 民
民.mnemonic:
(At the Gettysburg address) Imagine Abraham Lincoln standing among the corpses
buried under piles of earth, making his famous speech about government “of the
people, by the people, for the people.” / A fairy records the minutes (preserving the
words for posterity).

注.equation: water 氵 (78a) + lord 主 (297) = pour 注
注.mnemonic:
W ater is brought to the new lord who pours it into the ceremonial urn. / The dwarf
dips the lordly jewel into the urn to turn the water into wine.

拾.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + join together 合 (347) = pick up 拾
拾.mnemonic:
(The king says to the princess) “You must accept the hand of the winning suitor and
join together with him in matrimony. each suitor will pick up the marital plate. / The
fairy will sit on the plate with a shallot balanced on her head, and the one who
keeps it balanced there the longest will be the winner.”

步.equation: stop 止 (29a) + few 少 (377) = step 步
步.mnemonic:
“Stop! — only a few may walk in the master’s foot step s.” / The dwarf carefully
removes his boots to lead the chosen few (placing his feet in the footprints).

进.equation: road 辶 (26a) + a well 井 (357a) = proceed 进
进.mnemonic:
The road to the well was so full of people that you could only proceed slowly along
it. / The dwarf, stuck in the queue, was glad that he had brought his hip flask full of
gin.

启.mnemonic: Imagine a door slowly opening (启) to reveal a bright new world (the sound 'qǐ' resembles the sound of a door creaking open). As the door opens, it lets in fresh ideas and opportunities, symbolizing enlightenment and new beginnings. Picture yourself stepping through that door into a realm of possibilities.

适.equation: road 辶 (26a) + tongue 舌 (457a) = suitable 适
适.mnemonic:
(The king likes to walk barefoot about his kingdom) He has a team of lackeys to lick
the road with their tongues to check that it is suitable for his dainty feet. / The dwarf
is dismissive, “What a charade!”

围.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + leather 韦 (706a) = surround 围
围.mnemonic:
(In the glove factory) the highest quality leather is enclosed in a store room
surrounded by security devices. / The fairy keeps the keys on a belt at her waist.

发.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + friend 友 (53) = send out 发
发.mnemonic:
She is writing her Christmas cards and puts a drop of perfume on the cards to her
special friends before sending them out. / The giant gets a large drop on his as he
lives far, far away (and the perfume has to last until the card gets there).

甬.equation: seal (560a) + use 用 (130) = explosion 甬
甬.mnemonic:
(The assassins are planting a booby trap) When the seal is next used it will cause an
explosion and kill the king. / [No pronunciation needed]

通.equation: road 辶 (26a) + explosion 甬 (564a) = pass through 通
通.mnemonic:
When the road was blocked (in a landslip) they used an explosion to clear it so that
traffic could pass through. / The giant used his tongs to help remove the rubble.

矢.equation: clouds (124b) + big 大 (47) = arrow 矢
矢.mnemonic:
The TV weatherman was given new symbols for clouds, but they were so big that he
hardly had any space left on the map to put the wind arrow s. / [No pronunciation
needed]

知.equation: arrow 矢 (490a) + mouth 口 (5) = know 知
知.mnemonic:
He shoots an arrow into the mouth of the cave, so that anyone inside will know he’s
there (there are no doorbells on caves). / The giant appears, complaining,
“I was
just relaxing in my jacuzzi!”

头.equation: ice crystals (246a) + big 大 (47) = head 头
头.mnemonic:
In the cavern, the ice crystals were so big that he hit his head on one. / The fairy
conjured up a lamp for him to see by, just as he was about to stub his toe on another
one.

买.equation: cap 乛 (247a) + head 头 (246) = buy 买
买.mnemonic:
(A schoolboy is going out to get illicit cigarettes) He puts a large cap on his head so
that when he buys them he can smuggle them back in it. / Teddy puts on a bishop’s
miter (trying to help, but only drawing attention to the two of them).

卖.equation: ten 十 (4) + buy 买 (247) = sell 卖
卖.mnemonic:
(At the cathedral in Rome) The pilgrim wants to buy a cross— and there’s no shortage
of people to sell him one! / One trinket seller, a dwarf, is wearing a bishop’s miter
in the hope that people will think his goods are more authentic.

读.equation: words 讠 (67c) + sell 卖 (248) = read 读
读.mnemonic:
She has words written on flash cards, which she sells to schools to help children learn
to read. / The fairy sits on the children’s duvets and listens to them reading at
bedtime.

流.equation: water 氵 (78a) + birth (615b) = flow 流
流.mnemonic:
The woman chose a water birth in the river so that the flowing water would wash the
baby clean. / Two fairies floated lotus blossoms on the water.

完.equation: house 宀 (91a) + yuan 元 (301) = finish 完
完.mnemonic:
The high-class building firm has a custom, when building a new house, to cement a
yuan coin to the roof when the work is finished (to show that they don’t penny-pinch).
/ [DIY pronunciation]

院.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + finish 完 (305) = institute 院
院.mnemonic:
They built a temporary mound in front when they had finished building the Institute. /
The dwarf stood on it to place the commemorative yuan coin above the door, to
declare the Institute for Dwarf Studies open.

将.equation: firewood 丬 (317a) + evening 夕 (69a) + inch 寸 (104a) = lay out
将.mnemonic:
“You should have brought the firewood and laid out the fire by now.” “I was just
going to!” / The two giants jangled the hearth irons, impatient to have their fire
made up.

疋.equation: cap 乛 (247a) + stop (30a) = bolt of cloth 疋
疋.mnemonic: The stall-holder whips off his cap when he realizes that it’s the princess who has stopped at his stall — and he quickly produces his finest bolt of cloth for her inspection.

是.equation: sun 日 (6) + upright 疋 (30b) = is 是
是.mnemonic: (An argument is taking place in a back garden) they are staking a sun flower — is it upright? "It is," says one of the gardeners; "It isn't," says the other. "Is, is, is!" says the first, and so on. / The dwarf who lives next door can't stand it any longer and comes round, brandishing his shillelagh.

手.mnemonic: Teddy takes one hand off the steering wheel, showing off his driving skills (and probably ends up in a ditch!)

戈.mnemonic: Watch the stroke order here. You finish up with the drop (after you finish writing the previous stroke downwards). / This is a picture of an ancient weapon, which was something like a cross between a dagger, an axe and a spear!

我.equation: hand 手 (31) + dagger 戈 (32a) = I 我
我.mnemonic: The dagger was stuck in the huge stone hand (a bit like excalibur) — he pulled it out and declared “I am the One!”. / This woke Teddy who had been asleep under the hand.

中.mnemonic: In the middle of each wheel of his car, the giant had etched a picture of a jonquil. (A jonquil is a type of small daffodil. You’ll have to excuse us if we sometimes have to scratch around for soundwords — some sounds come at the start of very few English words!)

厶.meaning: cocoon, to shut away, self-centered, selfish
厶.mnemonic: This character originally meant 'cocoon', and from this it came to mean 'to shut away', hence 'self-centered' or 'selfish'.

么.equation: slide (34b) + cocoon 厶 (34a) = appendage 么
么.mnemonic: The cocoon had been fixed to the top of the children's slide, as an appendage (perhaps so that the new butterfly, when she emerges, can use the slide like a ski-jump for her first flight!) / This throws the robot's circuits into a dreadful muddle (as he wasn't allowed to remove it when he cleaned the slide).

什.equation: person 亻 (13a) + ten 十 (4) = what? 什
什.mnemonic: Harry stands alone at the crossroads but nobody else turns up. What is wrong? What has he done? / The fairy (hovering at a safe distance) tells him everyone will shun him until he has a wash and doesn’t smell any more!

五.mnemonic: Teddy is splashing in a rock pool and sees a five-spoked wheel but when he goes to pick it up it turns out to be a starfish and it wounds his paw.

七.mnemonic: The character for 'seven' is represented in stories by a diary (a weekly planner where you look seven days ahead to see appointments, rather than a personal journal). / You look in your diary, and notice that in seven days' time your car is due for new wheels. The giant has offered to get them for you cheap.

九.mnemonic: During throwing practice each of the nine members of the baseball team has to throw the baseball cleanly through the spokes of a wheel. Two teddies, who are holding the wheel, keep moving it for a joke.

六.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + eight 八 (20) = six 六
六.mnemonic: The octopus is trying to lift the lid of a huge cooking pot — he has to use six of his legs (leaving only two to stand on!). / Two dwarves arrive carrying a huge locust to put in the pot.

百.equation: thumb tack (40a) + sun 日 (6) = hundred 百
百.mnemonic: You see a thumb tack (on the road), glinting in the sun. When you look more closely, you can see there are a hundred of them. / Teddy has sprinkled them on the new bypass (as a protest against it being built near his home).

边.equation: road 辶 (26a) + power 力 (14) = side 边
边.mnemonic: A powerful machine is clearing a path for a new road by pushing everything to the side. / But it can only do the straight bits so two giants have to be called on to create the bends.

卜.equation: stick 丨 (19a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = fortune teller 卜
卜.mnemonic: "Dip the stick in this ink and let a drop fall on here," says the fortune teller (she can tell your fortune by the pattern the ink makes).

上.mnemonic: A huge wheel hovers above Shanghai with a dwarf sitting on it, directing the boats in the harbor.

下.equation: one 一 (1) + fortune teller 卜 (42a) = below 下
下.mnemonic: The unicorn goes to see the fortune teller, and she looks at what is below the card which he points to with his hoof. / "I see two dwarves sharpening your horn for you."

马.mnemonic: Teddy has shown a horse how to balance on a wheel so they can earn money at the market (performing as street entertainers).

吗.equation: mouth 口 (5) + horse 马 (44) = question mark 吗
吗.mnemonic: Imagine looking inside the mouth of a horse you are thinking of buying, and finding a question mark stamped on the horse’s tongue. / Your robot remarks, “It’s not logical to buy horses at this market.”

妈.equation: woman 女 (15) + horse 马 (44) = mum 妈
妈.mnemonic: A teenage girl is out shopping and she turns to see a woman on a horse— naked like Lady Godiva. "Mum!" the teenager cries, mortified. / Fortunately at that moment the giant comes bumbling through the market (overturning stalls and distracting everyone’s attention, giving the girl a chance to lead the horse away out of sight).

大.mnemonic: The Big Wheel at the fairground is in use all day so the dwarf has to do the maintenance work at night in the dark. (Imagine him grumbling, “How do they expect me to see what I’m doing?”).

夫.mnemonic: She makes her husband use the exercise wheel. 'You are fat because you eat as much food as the giant,' she tells him.

小.mnemonic: The small wheel has to be turned to operate the shower, but the two teddies have to stand one on top of the other to reach it.

尔.equation: bow (51a) + small 小 (50) = thou 尔
尔.mnemonic: Imagine two fastidious courtiers bowing to each other by a small gap in the fence. "after thou," one says. "No, after thou!" and so on.

你.equation: person 亻 (13a) + thou 尔 (51b) = you 你
你.mnemonic: “Harry, thou art clever,” says the wise man. “Why don’t you just say “you”?” harry replies. / Teddy gets all excited and starts singing, “No need to kneel, no need to kneel.”

友.equation: left hand (53a) + right hand 又 (52) = friend 友
友.mnemonic: They run along (right) hand in (left) hand, the best of friends. / But the mischievous Teddy is waiting, and as they pass he flicks his yoyo out and they fall down in a tangle of string.

土.mnemonic: Once again (as in 夫, Character 49), the second stroke of this character is longer than the first. this time it really matters, for there is another character which looks just like 土, except that the first stroke is longer than the second (士, Character 487a). When used as the left-hand side of other characters, 土 gets squashed so that the last stroke slopes upwards (see the next character for an example).

地.equation: earth 土 (54a) + also 也 (8) = ground 地 / earth 土 (54a) + also 也 (8) = -ly 地
地.mnemonic: (A posse is pursuing bandits, and the sheriff dismounts to look at the spent bullets) 'the earth has also been disturbed,' he says, 'they're heading for higher ground.' / His dwarf deputy takes a few men on a detour (to head them off at the pass …) / (The landing party has returned to the spaceship from the planet) 'We didn't just get samples of earth, we also found some great alien artefacts, so treat them gentle.' 'Gently,' corrects the Chief Scientist (who is a stickler for grammar). / 'And can somebody go and sort out the robot— he's miserable because he's got so dusty' (collecting the earth samples).

云.equation: one 一 (1) + swoop (56a) = cloud 云
云.mnemonic: A bird is teasing the unicorn, swooping under its tummy, but each time the unicorn looks round the bird hides in a cloud. / The fairy is so impressed she makes the bird an honorary member of her aviators’ union.

运.equation: road 辶 (26a) + cloud 云 (56) = transport 运
运.mnemonic: The road is obscured by a cloud of dust as the big transport trucks roll past. / The dwarf (covered in dust) calls after them, “I’ll get the union on to you!”

动.equation: cloud 云 (56) + power 力 (14) = move 动
动.mnemonic: A tornado cloud has the power to move almost anything. / It can even knock the dwarf off his donkey.

会.mnemonic: (The captain of the birds’ aerobatic display team is speaking) “If we are going to combine our swooping maneuvers we need to have a meeting to discuss how we will do it.” / The ghostly dwarf (safety officer) is organizing the hay (to act as a safety cushion).

王.equation: one 一 (1) + earth 土 (54a) = king 王
王.mnemonic: The unicorn (telling the story of excalibur) sticks his horn into the earth, then withdraws it with a flourish to demonstrate how the young arthur became king. / [No pronunciation needed]

玉.equation: king 王 (60a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = jade 玉
玉.mnemonic: The king is out jogging and as each drop of sweat touches the ground it turns to jade.

国.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + jade 玉 (60b) = country 国
国.mnemonic: The jade crown is enclosed in a theft-proof case at the exhibition as it is a very important part of the country’s heritage. / The legendary ghostly fairy who came from the Gobi desert guards it.

月.mnemonic: You are out walking at night when the moon light falling on a wheel lights up the dwarf who is sitting on it. 'That you, eh?' he whispers gruffly.

朋.equation: moon 月 (61) + moon 月 (61) = companion 朋
朋.mnemonic: “Why did you buy two moon-shaped balloons?” her mother asks. “Because the first moon needed another moon as a companion,” the little girl says. / But just then one of the moons gets punctured, but (before the child can start to cry) the fairy quickly flies over, flicks her wand, and it’s as good as new.

有.equation: left hand (53a) + moon 月 (61) = have 有
有.mnemonic: "Why is there a moon on the left hand side of every picture?" the girl's mother asks. "Because I only have a moon stencil," the girl replies. / Teddy practices his favorite yoga posture (and says "Why not draw me instead?")

未.mnemonic: Yet again, as in 夫 (49) and 土 (54a), the second stroke of this character is longer than the first. This is another occasion where it does matter, because we shall be meeting the character 末 (793a) later. It is unfortunately true that some small details like this do matter, whereas other small differences don’t — as mentioned at the start of this chapter. We will do our best to draw attention to the differences that matter and you will soon get used to these; you will also soon be routinely recognizing minor variants of the same character.

妹.equation: woman 女 (15) + not yet 未 (64a) = younger sister 妹
妹.mnemonic: (Two lads in the pub) 'What about that woman over there — she’s not yet married,' says one. 'You can’t fancy her, she’s my younger sister!' his mate replies. / 'Anyway the dwarf has his eye on her, and he’s a Major.'

丷.mnemonic: The 'horns' usually come at the top of a character (but not always — see the following character).

来.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + not yet 未 (64a) = come 来
来.mnemonic: The stags are going off on their hunt, but the fawn is too young. 'Your horns are not yet grown so you cannot come yet,' he is told. / The fairy (who comes to babysit him) fixes up lights to amuse him and guide the returning hunters.

了.mnemonic: For the expedition to the new planet, the robot has brought plenty of spare wheels so that he can change them whenever there is a transition to a different sort of terrain — just his luck that this is a water world! / When the repairs to the wheel are complete the two teddies rush around calling loudly to the passengers to get back on board.

兄.equation: mouth 口 (5) + boy 儿 (21) = older brother 兄
兄.mnemonic: At the mouth of the cave the boy is acting as a look-out for his older brother (who has sneaked inside to see what’s in there).

兌.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + older brother 兄 (67a) = convert 兌
兌.mnemonic: “I found these horns,” the little girl says, holding them up proudly. “My older brother is going to convert them into something for me to play with.”

讠.mnemonic: This common component, which appears on the left-hand side of characters, is a simplification of the character 言 which we’ll meet later (Character 375). In our stories we usually refer to words which are written down rather than spoken.

说.equation: words 讠 (67c) + convert 兌 (67b) = say 说
说.mnemonic: (A new aid for blind people has been invented) “all you have to do is run this gadget over the written words and it will convert them into signals which it can then say out loud.” / The ghostly giant (who is the guardian of this new device) shows the blind people how to use it.

吾.equation: five 五 (36) + mouth 口 (5) = yours truly 吾
吾.mnemonic: A literary character for “I” or “we”. The starfish puts the pen nib in his mouth to lick it, and signs off the letter with a flourish, “Yours truly.” / [No pronunciation needed]

语.equation: words 讠 (67c) + yours truly 吾 (68a) = language 语
语.mnemonic: (Writing a letter to your bank manager) You try to think of the correct words to finish with, and eventually write “Yours truly” — thinking that this is the sort of language that is expected. / Teddy, realizing that the letter is finally finished, cries “Eureka!” (although it’s pouring with rain so he’ll get soaking wet posting it).

夕.mnemonic: The final stroke is written in a downwards direction.

多.equation: evening 夕 (69a) + evening 夕 (69a) = many 多
多.mnemonic: Walking along the pier each night, the friendly policeman says 'Evening, evening' to the many couples he passes. He even stops to call 'Good evening' to the ghostly giant who guards the dome at the end of the pier.

名.equation: evening 夕 (69a) + mouth 口 (5) = name 名
名.mnemonic: (The worried young man is looking for his girlfriend) By evening he had reached the mouth of the cave and called out her name. / The fairy brought her out, having conjured up a mink coat to keep her warm.

外.equation: evening 夕 (69a) + fortune teller 卜 (42a) = outside 外
外.mnemonic: In the evening (after a hard day’s work) the fortune teller likes to sit outside (and enjoy the fresh air). / The dwarf from the next stall comes over to share her bottle of wine (he’s too stingy to buy his own!)

刀.mnemonic: The giant fixed a knife to the wheel as instructed, but he had grave doubts about whether it would really cut the crops as it rolled along.

分.equation: eight 八 (20) + knife 刀 (72) = division 分
分.mnemonic: The octopus, with a knife in each tentacle, divides the huge pizzas into lots of equal pieces. / The giant (who is on his tea break) finds it great fun to watch.

刂.mnemonic: When 'knife' appears as the right-hand side of composite characters, it takes this form.

至.equation: swoop (56a) + earth 土 (54a) = until 至
至.mnemonic: The birds (following the farmer sowing his seeds) swoop down to the earth one after another until there are no seeds left. / [No pronunciation needed]

到.equation: until 至 (74b) + knife 刂 (74a) = arrive 到
到.mnemonic: Until the magic knife has finished trimming the decorations it is not safe for the guests to arrive. / The dwarf says, “I have grave doubts about the safety of using magic knives.”

倒.equation: person 亻 (13a) + arrive 到 (74) = topple; invert 倒
倒.mnemonic: Harry arrives on the film set of a 'harry potter' film, just as a burning candle topples over (and threatens to set light to everything). / Teddy tries to douse the flames by flicking water from a fire bucket. But the dwarf shows him how to douse it properly. 'Invert the whole bucket over the fire, like this,' he says.

天.equation: one 一 (1) + big 大 (47) = heaven 天
天.mnemonic: The unicorn was so big that his horn seemed to touch the heavens. / But he made an ideal umpire for the two giants playing tennis (as he was tall enough to see clearly what was going on).

明.equation: sun 日 (6) + moon 月 (61) = bright 明
明.mnemonic: It’s the sun shining on the moon that makes it bright. / The fairy likes to wear her mink shawl in the bright moonlight.

氵.mnemonic: This very common fragment is an abbreviation of the character 水 which we’ll meet later (Character 523) — it takes this form when appearing as the left-hand part of composite characters. Note that the final stroke is written in an upwards direction.

汉.equation: water 氵 (78a) + right hand 又 (52) = Han Chinese 汉
汉.mnemonic: The Han Chinese are the majority ethnic group in China. They sprinkle water on their right hands before shaking hands to demonstrate that they are Han Chinese. / The dwarf is reaching up his hand so that he can be included (because he doesn’t like to miss out on things).

另.equation: mouth 口 (5) + power 力 (14) = other 另
另.mnemonic: Man has two types of power: physical strength is one, but 'mouth power', the power of persuasion, is the other.

别.equation: other 另 (79a) + knife 刂 (74a) = don’t 别
别.mnemonic: Use the other knife, don’t use that one, we haven’t sharpened it yet. / Two fairies hold a belt between them to sharpen the knives on.

如.equation: woman 女 (15) + mouth 口 (5) = if 如
如.mnemonic: The woman stands at the mouth of the cave, hesitating, “it looks iffy to me” she says. / The fairy flutters over and gives her a magic ruby to keep her safe.

彳.equation: slide (34b) + person 亻 (13a) = step forward 彳
彳.mnemonic: Harry is standing at the bottom of the slide ready to step forward and catch any children who are going too fast. / [No pronunciation needed]

丁.mnemonic: You will also see this used to mean fourth in a sequence, much as (d) or (iv) are used in english.

亍.equation: one 一 (1) + nail 丁 (81b) = footstep 亍
亍.mnemonic: The unicorn has a nail stuck in his hoof so you can hear his footsteps clattering when he’s walking along the road.

行.equation: step forward 彳 (81a) + footstep 亍 (81c) = OK 行 / step forward 彳 (81a) + footstep 亍 (81c) = line 行
行.mnemonic: The sheriff looks up and down the street before he dares to step forward, but then he thinks he hears a footstep behind him. he whirls round … but it’s OK. / It’s just the fairy fixing up a shingle at the doctor’s house (and she’d dropped it). / (Outside the sickbay, the morning after the planet-leaving party). You are about to step forward into the sickbay when you hear a footstep behind you. You look back and see a long line of people queueing up. / The fairy is well known for her hangover cures!

冖.equation: hands reaching down (82b) + cover 冖 (82a) = unveil
冖.mnemonic: Imagine a row of people lined up along the roof of a new building — the band strikes up below then their hands reach down to pull on a cover to unveil the company’s name on its new headquarters.

学.equation: unveil (82c) + child 子 (17) = study 学
学.mnemonic: (At the opening of the new college) a local celebrity unveils a statue of a child in front of the main building — the child is studying a book. / [DIY pronunciation]

车.mnemonic: The giant is changing the wheel on his car, but he is careless when he takes the old wheel off and it rolls down the hill and demolishes the church!

连.equation: road 辶 (26a) + car 车 (83) = linked up 连
连.mnemonic: Imagine an isolated stretch of road with a single car on it that can’t go anywhere because the road isn’t linked up to the highway system yet. / The two fairies take pity on the owner and magic up some dried lentils to use as gravel to make a temporary road surface.

开.equation: one 一 (1) + two hands (85a) = open 开
开.mnemonic: The unicorn is looking for the secret passageway and comes across two hands painted on the rock face. 'this must mean that it needs two hands to open it.' / He looks around and sees the giant's kite flying nearby — he will be perfect for the job.

去.equation: earth 土 (54a) + cocoon 厶 (34a) = go 去
去.mnemonic: Earth covers her cocoon so the butterfly is getting worried. 'I won’t be able to go when the time comes,' she thinks. / Then, miraculously it starts to pour with rain and the earth is washed away, but the dwarf park attendant now mistakes the cocoon for a piece of chewing gum and puts it in his rubbish sack!

法.equation: water 氵 (78a) + go 去 (86) = law 法
法.mnemonic: Water always goes anti-clockwise down the plughole — that’s a law of physics. / “Far out,” says Teddy.

耳.mnemonic: Note the stroke order here — it often seems to catch people out.

取.equation: ear 耳 (88a) + right hand 又 (52) = acquire 取
取.mnemonic: (The secret agent has been waiting for ages for his contact to show up). When the contact finally arrives they shake hands and he whispers in the agent’s ear, “You must acquire the secret plans to the military base,” and then he walks away. / It starts to pour with rain so Teddy is keen to go, because he’s getting soaking wet and his chewing gum has run out.

千.equation: beret  (89a) + ten 十 (4) = thousand 千
千.mnemonic: (Picture a war-grave cemetery at the site of a commando landing) — rows and rows of crosses with a regimental beret on each one — a thousand in neat rows.

前.equation: feet (90a) + canoe (90b) = in front of 前
前.mnemonic: Your feet in a canoe are out in front of you! / [DIY pronunciation]

安.equation: house 宀 (91a) + woman 女 (15) = peace 安
安.mnemonic: The woman is alone in the house at last, and looking forward to some peace and quiet. / But the giant starts working on his anvil next door, and there is peace no more!

字.equation: house 宀 (91a) + child 子 (17) = Chinese character 字
字.mnemonic: The child has to stay in the house to learn her Chinese characters. / She recites them out loud until the dwarf babysitter dozes off, and when she hears the “zzz” sounds she sneaks out to play.

目.mnemonic: In the factory the dwarf keeps his eye on the wheel. He’s in a bad mood (because the relief shift hasn’t turned up yet).

自.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + eye 目 (93) = self 自
自.mnemonic: “You’ll need to put a drop of this in your eye,” the doctor said, “but you’ll have to do it your self. / I can hear from the “zzz” sound that my dwarf assistant is asleep again.”

咱.equation: mouth 口 (5) + self 自 (94) = we 咱
咱.mnemonic: The two friends stand at the mouth of the cave. 'I'm not going in there by my self,' she says. 'You don't have to, we'll go together,' says her friend. / 'If we don't go in, we won't find out what the fairy has brought back from Zanzibar for us.'

阝.mnemonic: This is the only component whose meaning depends on where it appears in a character. When it’s on the left it means “mound”, but when it’s on the right it means “city”. examples of each follow in the next few characters. We realise that this is confusing but because everyone else observes this distinction, we will too. the situation arose because 阝 is an abbreviation of two completely different older characters, one which always appeared on the left and another which always appeared on the right. A similar situation applies to a few other characters, but modern Chinese doesn’t distinguish between them so you don’t have to worry about those!

阳.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + sun 日 (6) = in the open 阳
阳.mnemonic: It’s best to read this and the next story together. Yin and yang (this character and the following one) are the two opposing forces in taoist philosophy. Yang is the force associated with being overt, in the sun, positive and masculine; and yin with things hidden or occult, cloudy, negative or feminine (insert here a comment of your choice about male chauvinism!). In the stories the fairy and the giant are getting in touch with their masculine and feminine sides, respectively. / When the sun shines, the creatures that live in the mound come out in to the open. / The fairy visits the mound in the day-time to develop her yang side.

阴.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + moon 月 (61) = hidden 阴
阴.mnemonic: When the moon is up, the creatures burrow into the mound so that they are hidden. / The giant now comes at nighttime, to develop his yin side.

那.equation: furs (98a) + city 阝 (96a) = that 那
那.mnemonic: Every time she sees a fur coat in a shop window in the city she says, “That one, I want that one.” / The dwarf is well and truly fed up with this. “It’s enough to drive you to narcotics,” he grumbles.

哪.equation: mouth 口 (5) + that 那 (98) = which? 哪
哪.mnemonic: (At the dentist) She opens her mouth and points to a tooth, “It’s that one which hurts.” “Which?” asks the dentist (peering more closely). / Teddy, trying to be helpful, has brought along some narcotics in case she needs some…

西.mnemonic: The enormous wheel that hangs on the west gate of the city is the perfect size for the giant to use as a shield (so he pinches it!)

要.equation: west 西 (100) + woman 女 (15) = want; ask for 要
要.mnemonic: As a Western woman alone in an eastern culture, think of the King of Siam’s tutor in “the King and I” — so this is a film set story. As the director calls “Cut!” for the umpteenth time, the actress playing the leading role shouts, “What do you want from me?” / She storms off stepping on the dwarf’s foot, causing him to shout “Yeow!” For good measure she also kicks the giant’s foot and he says “Yeow!” too — (but more in surprise than pain). “All I did was ask for her autograph!” he complains.

从.equation: person 人 (12) + person 人 (12) = from 从
从.mnemonic: Harry unwraps the large parcel — and inside is a statue of Harry himself. he looks through the packaging to see who it’s from. / [DIY pronunciation]

寸.mnemonic: Note that the vertical stroke is off center and has a hook at the end.

村.equation: tree 木 (10a) + inch 寸 (104a) = village 村
村.mnemonic: They have to measure the girth of the tree in inches, to check that it will be the right size for the village square. / The giant has offered to erect it for them and it will look stunning once it is decorated.

时.equation: sun 日 (6) + inch 寸 (104a) = time 时
时.mnemonic: The sun dial is marked out in inches to tell the time. / The fairy is sitting on it smoking a cheroot!

身.mnemonic: The railway has been attacked and there is a gap in the tracks over the bridge. The giant lets the wheels of the train run over his body and saves the day — but he’s now too tired to go back to his usual job of shunting carriages.

射.equation: body 身 (107) + inch 寸 (104a) = shoot 射
射.mnemonic: The sniper moves his body forward an inch at a time until he has a clear view and can shoot at his target.

谢.equation: words 讠 (67c) + shoot 射 (108a) = thank 谢
谢.mnemonic: The bank robbers had used their guns to shoot holes in the bank’s door spelling out the words “thank you” before they left. / The two dwarves agree to let them stash the money in their shed until the heat is off. (Try to visualize both of the dwarves — maybe one is holding open the shed door, while the other one keeps a look out for the cops!)

家.equation: house 宀 (91a) + pig 豕 (109a) = household 家
家.mnemonic: If you have a pig in your house it must be included in your household. / The two giants debate the meaning of the jargon on the census form.

山.mnemonic: Taking a break from pushing the huge wheel up the mountain, the giant stops for a swig of shandy.

羊.mnemonic: The stupid sheep has got his head stuck in the spokes of the wagon wheel. The fairy has to come and yank him out.

样.equation: tree 木 (10a) + sheep 羊 (111) = appearance 样
样.mnemonic: The tree is a good place for the escaped sheep to hide while he changes his appearance. / But the dwarf (shepherd) finds him and yanks him off back to the flock.

班.equation: king 王 (60a) + knife 刂 (74a) + king 王 (60a) = team 班
班.mnemonic: At the inter-kingdom quiz, the two kings sit on either side of the beautiful jewelled knife that will be presented to the winning team. / For the final deciding question the teams have to identify a picture of the giant in a bandanna.

出.equation: mountain 山 (110) + mountain 山 (110) = exit 出
出.mnemonic: (Arriving in the train station in Switzerland) You can see mountain upon mountain out of each exit. / The giant is sitting on one of them chewing gum.

石.equation: thumb tack (40a) + mouth 口 (5) = stone 石
石.mnemonic: You’re trying to fix up a notice saying “Slippery Stones” with a thumb tack at the mouth of the cave, but (ironically) it keeps slipping on the stone.

础.equation: stone 石 (115a) + exit 出 (114) = plinth 础
础.mnemonic: You crash your baggage cart into a huge cube of stone outside the exit (from the station) — it’s a plinth for a new statue. / Teddy quickly sticks his chewing gum on the plinth, and starts to help you gather up your things (but as usual his help is just what you don’t need!)

岁.equation: mountain 山 (110) + evening 夕 (69a) = years old 岁
岁.mnemonic: (A mother is scolding her young son) “You must never be out by yourself on the mountain in the evening until you’re 16 years old.” / The ghostly dwarf whose job is to protect climbers on the mountain (not young boys) is angry too, as he hands the boy back to his mother. “It’s not safe to be on the mountain alone at your age,” he tells him.

但.equation: person 亻 (13a) + dawn 旦 (117a) = but 但
但.mnemonic: Harry says, “It’s dawn, wake up!” his friend, still half asleep, mumbles, “But, but, but … it’s still dark!” / “We’ve got to go — the dwarf said the dandelions have to be picked at dawn.” (And we all know how cross he’ll be if they don’t do it right…)

得.equation: step forward 彳 (81a) + get (118a) = obtain 得
得.mnemonic: He steps forward to get the gold medal — he had won by obtaining the best overall score. / But there was a dearth of gold so the fairy had conjured up a temporary medal until a proper one could be made. / He steps forward to get the silver medal — the way he had performed had been the best (but he had lost technical marks). / The robot polishes the medal as it’s dusty and he likes all silver things (like himself) to be shiny. / He steps forward to get the bronze medal — he had done all the bits he must do (the compulsory bits) but nothing more. / Teddy puts the bronze medal on a daisy chain to try and cheer up the third place competitor.

公.equation: eight 八 (20) + cocoon (34a) = public 公
公.mnemonic: The octopus lives in a cocoon which he is so proud of that he throws it open to the public. / The giant sounds a gong to attract visitors.

以.equation: barb (120a) + a drop 丶 (22a) + person 人 (12) = using 以
以.mnemonic: Harry puts a drop of colored wax on the barb of his fish-hook, using the bright colors of the wax to attract the fish. / Teddy tries it too, and catches an eel.

之.mnemonic: The giant is playing the Wheel Of Fortune and wins a trip to Japan (he has always wanted to see Mount Fuji).

为.equation: power 力 (14) + a drop 丶 (22a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = act as; for 为
为.mnemonic: The potion which confers great power from only a couple of drops is the Gauls’ magic potion in the film of “asterix”. On the film set the main actor has to act as if he is strong. / The fairy conjures up fake weights for him to lift. The dwarf grumbles as he carries the weights. “Why does somebody have to carry these things around for him?”

办.equation: power 力 (14) + eight 八 (20) = manage 办
办.mnemonic: Both this and the previous character consist of “power” plus a couple of drops. To distinguish between them, we have cheated slightly here, and think of this one as being “power” plus “eight”. / The “power-octopus” is a big eight-armed machine in the factory, so complicated that it needs someone to manage it full-time. / The dwarf banishes everyone else from the vicinity.

干.equation: one 一 (1) + ten 十 (4) = dry; work 干
干.mnemonic: Picture the unicorn standing on a white cross on the floor, which is his mark on the film set of 'The Wizard of Oz' — they are filming in the emerald City. everything is ready but they must wait for dry weather. / Then the giant can erect the lighting gantry. The dwarf kicks the gantry poles angrily. 'When are we going to get any work done?'

午.equation: clouds (124b) + ten 十 (4) = noon 午
午.mnemonic: The morning clouds go in one direction, the afternoon clouds go in another direction, so when the clouds cross it must be noon. / Teddy whoops with delight, as this means it’s lunchtime.

禾.equation: beret (89a) + tree 木 (10a) = grain 禾
禾.mnemonic: The beret (that they’d been using as a frisbee) is now stuck in a tree and they won’t be able to retrieve it without trampling the grain growing around the tree. (Who’s going to be the one to ask the farmer if they can get it?)

和.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + mouth 口 (5) = with 和 / grain 禾 (126a) + mouth 口 (5) = mix 和
和.mnemonic: The cowboy rolls the grain around in his mouth and says, 'This needs something to go with it.' / The fairy says, 'Try it with some of my herbs.' / (A baby alien has sneaked aboard the spaceship) They find it sitting on the storeroom floor, stuffing grain in its mouth and getting all the carefully-labeled grain samples mixed up. / The ghostly fairy who protects the planet comes to fetch the alien and take it home.

母.mnemonic: The mother is making a wheel out of icing for the birthday cake. Teddy has offered to make a chocolate mousse (but is spattering it all over the place).

每.equation: clouds (124b) + mother 母 (127) = every 每
每.mnemonic: Mother looks at the clouds every single morning (to see if it will be fine for a walk in the park). / Teddy wants to go to the maze (and get lost again).

海.equation: water 氵 (78a) + every 每 (128) = sea 海
海.mnemonic: Water from every river ends up in the sea. / Teddy is excited, and says, “With all that water, that’s why you get the “high seas”!”

用.mnemonic: Try to visualize a particular setting for this story — what type of wheel are we talking about here? / The dwarf says, “You’re too young to use the wheel.”

半.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + criminal (130a) = half 半
半.mnemonic: (At the prison Christmas pantomime) the toughest criminal grabbed the horns as he wanted to be the front half of the pantomime bull. / The dwarf governor (who suspected trouble) made them put bandages over the horns so that nobody could get hurt.

利.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + knife 刂 (74a) = benefit 利
利.mnemonic: Each grain must be split open with a knife to get the full benefit of the nutrients. / The dwarf cooks it in a liter of water and leaves it to soak.

生.mnemonic: Every house in the village has plants with labels displayed in the windows. It’s the custom that when a new child is born, a plant is chosen to symbolize their life (and labeled with the child’s name).

胜.equation: moon 月 (61) + life 生 (133) = triumph 胜
胜.mnemonic: The astronauts who first discover moon life will return in triumph. / DIY pronunciation

姓.equation: woman 女 (15) + life 生 (133) = surname 姓
姓.mnemonic: (A group of women are talking together) “even though it’s the woman who gives birth to life, the baby still gets the man’s surname.” / They have been watching the dwarf across the road putting up his name on a shingle.

星.equation: sun 日 (6) + life 生 (133) = star 星
星.mnemonic: Our sun is vital to all life on our planet, yet it is just a star. / The giant, lying on his back on the shingle on the beach, looks up at the night sky and ponders.

先.equation: calf (137a) + boy 儿 (21) = ahead 先
先.mnemonic: It looks as if the calf is taking the boy to market (rather than vice versa) because the calf keeps walking ahead. / [DIY pronunciation]

告.equation: calf (137a) + mouth 口 (5) = inform 告
告.mnemonic: (The cattle are all falling sick — only a calf is left standing) the calf goes to the mouth of the cave to inform the vet. / The dwarf assistant listens to the symptoms and says dismissively, “All this fuss, it’s probably only gout.”

洗.equation: water 氵 (78a) + ahead 先 (137) = wash 洗
洗.mnemonic: There’s only limited hot water in the rooming house so she races to get to the bathroom ahead of everyone else and have a good wash. / But Teddy has beaten her to it, and is washing his sheets in the sink.

可.equation: mouth 口 (5) + nail 丁 (81b) = may 可
可.mnemonic: The meaning is 'may' in the sense of 'being permitted to', but since this is a bit abstract we will cheat here and use a play on words: we will associate this character with Mayday celebrations in an english village, where children dance round a Maypole. / Note that the shaft of the nail is displaced to the right to make room for the mouth. / The carpenter holds nails in his mouth while fixing up the May pole for the May celebrations. / Teddy cuts up strips of curtains for the streamers.

河.equation: water 氵 (78a) + may 可 (140) = river 河
河.mnemonic: Water for the dunking stool at the May celebrations has to be brought from the river. / The fairy puts herbs into it to hide the smell!

何.equation: person 亻 (13a) + may 可 (140) = what 何
何.mnemonic: Harry arrives at the May celebrations, and a drink is pressed into his hand. he drinks, but splutters, “What is this stuff?” / It’s the fairy’s new herbal drink (and the fairy looks piqued).

阿.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + may 可 (140) = ah 阿
阿.mnemonic: Up on the mound sits the May queen on her throne. everyone goes “Ah” when they see her, she looks so splendid. / [No pronunciation needed]

啊.equation: mouth 口 (5) + ah 阿 (143a) = eh! 啊
啊.mnemonic: The doctor says, “Open your mouth and say “ah” — yes, these tonsils will have to come out.” “that bad, eh?” the patient says in surprise. / The giant says “Ah” in sympathy (he had to have his tonsils out as a child and he’d cried!)

首.equation: feet (90a) + self 自 (94) = head 首
首.mnemonic: You need to lift your feet right above the rest of your self if you’re going to be able to stand on your head. / Teddy shows you how … show-off!

道.equation: road 辶 (26a) + head 首 (144) = way 道
道.mnemonic: You emerge from the forest to find a road at last, but scratch your head: which way should you walk? / The dwarf grumbles, “Whichever way we go, I doubt it will be the right one.”

工.mnemonic: You trudge off every day to work at the wheel factory, and the only thing you look forward to is the giant sounding his gong for tea breaks and for the end of the shift.

江.equation: water 氵 (78a) + work 工 (147) = river 江
江.mnemonic: We have already seen the other character for river (Character 142).

厂.mnemonic: This is one of the very few basic building blocks that has a DIY pronunciation, so you can choose simply to learn the pronunciation, or make up a wheel story. Perhaps Teddy is rolling a large wheel off a cliff, almost hitting Mr Chang (Chang later tells this tale to his grandson, who we will meet in the next chapter working on the spaceship).

后.mnemonic: We must drag all the old furniture out and pile it up at the rear of the house. / The dwarf is going to turn the house into a hotel.

而.equation: thumb tack (40a) + comb (151a) = and yet 而
而.mnemonic: (Using a comb as a musical instrument) he had marked his notes on the comb with thumb tacks, and yet it still sounded awful. / The fairy fled, saying 'I've remembered an urgent appointment elsewhere!'

扌.mnemonic: This is the form which 手 “hand” (Character 31) usually takes when it forms the left-hand side of a character. Note the stroke order (compared with that for 手), and also note that the final stroke is drawn upwards.

找.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + dagger 戈 (32a) = look for 找
找.mnemonic: At the crime scene the detective pulls the policeman’s hand away from the dagger saying, “We must look for fingerprints first.” / Teddy’s alibi is that he was watching the jousting on TV.

打.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + nail 丁 (81b) = hit 打
打.mnemonic: He uses his hand to steady the nail before he hits it. / It is difficult to get on with the work because Teddy keeps darting in and out between his legs.

对.equation: right hand 又 (52) + inch 寸 (104a) = correct 对
对.mnemonic: If, when you shake hands, you have your right hands lined up to the nearest inch, you will be doing it in the correct way. / The ghostly etiquette-guarding dwarf makes the class practice daily.

树.equation: tree 木 (10a) + correct 对 (154) = tree 树
树.mnemonic: This is the modern character for “a tree”, rather than using 10a on its own. (A little girl is insistently pointing to objects and naming them) “Tree!” “Yes, correct, it’s a tree.” / Now shoo,” says the dwarf grumpily, “and let me get on with my work.

男.equation: field 田 (156a) + power 力 (14) = male 男
男.mnemonic: Out in the field the farmer needs more power to work the churn. “I’ll need another male,” he says. / The fairy flicks her wand and a nanny goat appears! (“Oops— wrong sort!” she says).

理.equation: king 王 (60a) + in 里 (157) = reason 理
理.mnemonic: (The king’s mother-in-law is visiting yet again) “Is the king in?” she asks the guard, and the guard has to think up a new reason each time why the king can’t see her. / Teddy peeks out to let the king know when she leaves (and the coast is clear).

电.mnemonic: They wanted to convert the water wheel to run on electricity— the two dwarf experts from Denmark were called in (each connecting up one of the two wires).

冂.mnemonic: This usually forms a 3-sided enclosure, with another character inside it. Note the hook at the bottom of the second stroke. For the stories, think of the 'outer limits of the kingdom' — a desolate, dangerous area of rocky hills, probably inhabited by bandits.

同.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + piled up (150b) = same 同
同.mnemonic: At the outer limits of the kingdom were piled up heaps of discarded boxes, all the same. / The fairy used her magic tongs to arrange them in neat piles.

心.mnemonic: The heart of the wheel (its hub) is as big as the giant’s shin bone.

必.equation: heart 心 (161) + slide (34b) = inevitably 必
必.mnemonic: In the theme park there is a huge slide through the heart of the forest — inevitably it gets clogged up (with twigs and leaves). / Each day the dwarf has to send his beaver down the slide to clean it out.

相.equation: tree 木 (10a) + eye 目 (93) = mutual; appearance 相
相.mnemonic: Eyes peering out from behind all the trees suggest Sherwood Forest, and the film set of “robin hood” — but this is a Chinese remake! at the auditions the actors are milling about, profuse with their mutual praise. / Particularly two giants from Shanghai, auditioning for the part of Little John. Two dwarves from Shanghai are also competing for the role (sadly not having understood that the role of Little John calls for a giant rather than a dwarf). They won’t be told and are spending ages perfecting their appearance.

想.equation: mutual 相 (163) + heart 心 (161) = think about 想
想.mnemonic: The conjoined twins share a mutual heart. The surgeon has to think about how best to separate them. / Fortunately he’s practiced on two teddies in Shanghai!

今.mnemonic: It needs the combined strength of everyone in the operating theater to pull the knee back into position. The surgeon gets everyone ready and then says, “ready, Steady … Now!” / The giant had been drinking too much gin (so fell over and dislocated his knee).

念.equation: now 今 (166) + heart 心 (161) = study 念
念.mnemonic: “Now I think it is time for the heart,” said the surgeon, as he turned to study his “heart Transplant Manual” (imagine him with his nose in this blood-spattered book). / DIY pronunciation

弓.mnemonic: This is a (stringed) bow, for a violin or for archery.

张.equation: a bow 弓 (173a) + long 长 (172) = sheet 张
张.mnemonic: In archery practice, The chief tries to show off by taking his long bow and aiming at sheets of paper pinned to trees in the forest. / The giant tries to put him off by jangling his keys loudly.

本.mnemonic: The source of the wheel (i.e., where it was made) was written round its edge, so Teddy looked to see where his bun was made!

体.equation: person 亻 (13a) + source 本 (174) = body 体
体.mnemonic: Harry was reading the source book on anatomy and when he opened it a hologram of a body floated out from the pages. / Then Teddy hopped out dressed up as a teacher and started pointing out all the different body parts.

书.mnemonic: The giant balanced his book on the wheel but the wheel turned and the book fell on his shoe-polish.

位.equation: person 亻 (13a) + stand 立 (177) = place 位
位.mnemonic: (At a group photo) Harry asks where to stand and they find him a place. / "Wait!" cries the dwarf photographer, "I'll find him a place when I'm ready."

拉.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + stand 立 (177) = pull 拉
拉.mnemonic: (On the slopes of a volcano) a huge hand appears and helps the boy to stand, giving him a strong pull. / The giant points to the approaching lava (which the boy hadn’t seen).

啦.equation: mouth 口 (5) + pull 拉 (179) = exclamation 啦
啦.mnemonic: This is an abbreviation for 了啊 (“le” plus “a”, Characters 66 and 143). (Think of this as a continuation of the previous story) The boy points at the mouth of the cave, pulling at his rescuer’s sleeve. “Over there ! Look !” he exclaims. / The robot, covered in bits of lava, has reached the safety of the cave and is trying to clean himself up.

火.mnemonic: Supposedly a picture of a flame with two sparks, but you may prefer to think of it as a person running, hands in the air, shouting “fire!” It takes a severely squashed form when appearing at the bottom of characters (see 185a). Some books give a different stroke order. / The wheel is on fire but the ghostly teddy (who was supposed to be guarding it) can’t put out the flames because when he tries to pick up the hose his paw passes straight through it!

灯.equation: fire 火 (181) + nail 丁 (81b) = lamp 灯
灯.mnemonic: (In an old castle) “If we could find a way of putting some fire on a nail in the wall we could have a lamp in the room.” / The giant dunks twigs in candle-wax to make the first rudimentary lamp. (Does this story sound familiar?)

占.equation: fortune teller 卜 (42a) + mouth 口 (5) = occupy 占
占.mnemonic: The fortune teller’s “drop” is a short horizontal line here (see the note for 42a). The fortune teller’s magic mouth was so popular that her tent was always occupied, and so a big crowd formed outside. / The dwarf janitor had to be called to clear them away.

站.equation: stand 立 (177) + occupy 占 (183) = station 站
站.mnemonic: You’re standing impatiently outside the occupied toilets at the train station (and your train is due any minute). / The dwarf is sitting in the janitor’s office (and is in no mood to be helpful).

灬.mnemonic: This is the form taken by 'fire' when appearing as the bottom part of other characters.

点.equation: occupy 占 (183) + fire 灬 (185a) = speck 点
点.mnemonic: All the places round the camp fire were occupied, except two seats with white specks on them. / It was toothpaste left by the two teddies who’d gone off to the dentist.

店.equation: shelter 广 (170) + occupy 占 (183) = store 店
店.mnemonic: If you occupy the shelter for a certain length of time, you are allowed to turn it into a store. / The two dwarves set up a shop selling dentist's supplies (picture them surrounded by false teeth, drills, etc.)

果.equation: field 田 (156a) + tree 木 (10a) = fruit 果
果.mnemonic: The developers want to dig up the field with the tree in it, but can’t because the tree produces a rare fruit. / The ghostly teddy who looks after the tree says, “Go away, Go away!”

棵.equation: tree 木 (10a) + fruit 果 (187) = number of trees 棵
棵.mnemonic: Each tree only bore a few fruits so it took a number of trees to produce enough fruit for a pot of jam. / The giant used the whole crop to make his lemon curd.

课.equation: words 讠 (67c) + fruit 果 (187) = lesson 课
课.mnemonic: (In school) Next to their pictures, the words for the fruits are written up during the lesson. / In the next lesson the dwarf teaches them how to make lemon curd (and is irritated at having to sit in for the cookery teacher).

攵.equation: clouds (124b) + shears 乂 (25a) = tap 攵
攵.mnemonic: The topiarist was dreaming of trimming clouds with his shears (creating wonderful shapes) — until he felt a tap on his shoulder (waking him up as his lunch break was over).

政.equation: upright 正 (29) + tap 攵 (190a) = government 政
政.mnemonic: A ranger stands by the upright stone and taps it, saying, “You can’t come in here — this is an area protected by the government.” / The dwarf has to take his jungle tours elsewhere.

古.equation: ten 十 (4) + mouth 口 (5) = old 古
古.mnemonic: A big cross has been painted above the mouth of one of the caves, to remind the forgetful old woman who lives there which cave is hers!

故.equation: old 古 (191a) + tap 攵 (190a) = former 故
故.mnemonic: (The finale of the conjuror’s act) “This time I’m going to use this very old wand to tap the hat, because it belonged to my former magic teacher.” / The dwarf assistant wondered what the conjuror would say when he pulled a goose from the hat (as the dwarf hadn’t been able to find a rabbit).

姑.equation: woman 女 (15) + old 古 (191a) = aunt 姑
姑.mnemonic: “Why does that woman look so old?” asks the little girl. “Shh! She’s your Aunt!” her mother replies in a whisper. / “If you’re rude about her the giant will come and fill your mouth with sticky goo.”

胡.equation: old 古 (191a) + moon 月 (61) = beard 胡
胡.mnemonic: The old moon has become reckless and has let his beard grow.

湖.equation: water 氵 (78a) + beard 胡 (193a) = lake 湖
湖.mnemonic: The water gets in the wizard’s beard as he drinks from the lake. / The lake fairy appears and says, “Who ’s that? Who ’s there?”

克.equation: old 古 (191a) + boy 儿 (21) = gram 克
克.mnemonic: In school the old boys are being taught about grams (they’d learned to use ounces when they were younger). / The dwarf teacher curses the newfangled metric system.

辛.equation: stand 立 (177) + ten 十 (4) = spicy 辛
辛.mnemonic: (At the children’s party) The children have to stand on the cross painted on the ground until they get the signal to start, then they must follow the spicy smell trail. / The giant had laid the trail by strapping a bag of curry powder to his shin!

亲.equation: stand 立 (177) + tree 木 (10a) = kin 亲
亲.mnemonic: Imagine you are standing in a tree— not an ordinary tree but your family tree, and your kin are all around you. / Some of your kin will be high enough to touch the giant’s chin.

产.equation: stand 立 (177) + cliff 厂 (149) = produce 产
产.mnemonic: Imagine standing on a cliff overlooking the sea — it’s the ideal spot for a factory to produce buckets and spades. / Teddy buys the first ones and slides down the channel leading to the beach.

卡.equation: above 上 (42) + fortune teller 卜 (42a) = card 卡
卡.mnemonic: Above the fortune teller’s head floats her business card. / Teddy secretly borrows her magic carpet to fly up behind her and get a closer look.

还.equation: road 辶 (26a) + not 不 (9) = still 还 / road 辶 (26a) + not 不 (9) = give back 还
还.mnemonic: The spaceship driving instructor says: “It’s not a road, you don’t have to observe the speed limits here.” The pupil replies, “I’m still a bit nervous.” / “Just wait till the fairy shows you how to use the hyper drive!” / (The bandits have robbed a bank and are fleeing the posse) “Let’s not use the road — we might meet the posse and we’d have to give back the gold.” / But the ghostly fairy who guards the bank catches up with them and calls out, “Hands up!”

看.equation: hand 手 (31) + eye 目 (93) = look at 看
看.mnemonic: On an old sailing ship, The Captain raises a hand to shade his eyes to look at the enemy ship. / He sees the dwarf loading a cannon.

讨.equation: words 讠 (67c) + inch 寸 (104a) = ask for 讨
讨.mnemonic: The young child is learning to read, and traces her finger along the words inch by inch — but has to ask for help when any big words crop up. / Teddy has to keep quiet, and is building a tower of blocks in the corner (but every now and again there is a loud crashing sound!)

回.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + mouth 口 (5) = return 回
回.mnemonic: The tribe’s oracle mouth is displayed in the museum in an enclosed area; people keep returning to see it (because it’s so unusual). / The ghostly fairy conjures up a shimmering halo to protect it.

妾.equation: stand 立 (177) + woman 女 (15) = concubine 妾
妾.mnemonic: (In the old days) You had to stand with a foot on a woman to claim her as your concubine. / [No pronunciation needed]

接.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + concubine 妾 (203a) = receive 接
接.mnemonic: He stretches out his hand to the new concubine to receive her into his household. / She has brought a huge plate of jelly as a gift which it took two giants to carry!

差.equation: sheep (204a) + work 工 (147) = fall short 差
差.mnemonic: The sheep keep working at their long-jump practice by trying to jump across the river, but keep falling short. / Their dwarf coach keeps a chart of their progress and he’s not impressed!

着.equation: sheep (204a) + eye 目 (93) = catch 着
着.mnemonic: In the cowboy town they were keeping an eye on the sheep, hoping to catch the sheep rustlers. / The fairy is teaching the posse jousting skills (ready for the confrontation with the sheep rustlers). / In the spaceship the sheep is patrolling the corridors, putting his eye to each door to see if he can catch anything funny going on. / Imagine his surprise when he sees the robot practicing his juggling!

匕.mnemonic: Notice how this differs from “seven” (Character 37) where the sloping stroke extends to both sides of the hook stroke. Many books give the stroke order as the other way round.

能.equation: cocoon 厶 (34a) + moon 月 (61) + two spoons 匕 (206a) = able to 能
能.mnemonic: You see someone picking up a cocoon in the moon light … it’s a waiter practicing picking things up using two spoons held in one hand — he has to be able to do this before he can pass his waitering exams. / DIY pronunciation

作.equation: person 亻 (13a) + suddenly 乍 (207a) = do 作
作.mnemonic: In a blinding flash, Harry suddenly knew what he had to do. / [DIY pronunciation]

昨.equation: sun 日 (6) + suddenly 乍 (207a) = yesterday 昨
昨.mnemonic: (At the all night birthday party) She sees the sun coming up and suddenly realizes it is a new day. It isn’t her birthday any more — that was now yesterday. / [DIY pronunciation]

左.equation: left hand  (53a) + work 工 (147) = left 左
左.mnemonic: Showing a new employee how to work the big machine in the factory) “Use your left hand, your wedding ring hand, to work the controls as they are all on the left side of the machine.” / DIY pronunciation

做.equation: person 亻 (13a) + former 故 (191) = do 做
做.mnemonic: Harry’s former wife runs into the church during the wedding ceremony just as he is saying, “I do.” / DIY pronunciation

坐.equation: from 从 (103) + earth 土 (54a) = sit 坐
坐.mnemonic: This character doesn’t split neatly left-right or top-bottom into its two parts; you could also see this as two people sitting on the earth. / From the earth rises a beautiful throne to sit on. / [DIY pronunciation]

座.equation: shelter 广 (170) + sit 坐 (211) = seat 座
座.mnemonic: You run into the shelter to sit out the storm — fortunately there’s a seat to sit on. / [DIY pronunciation]

右.equation: left hand (53a) + mouth 口 (5) = right 右
右.mnemonic: Put your left hand, the one with your wedding ring on it, over your mouth and stretch your other hand out to the floor so that you lean to the right. / The dwarf demonstrates how this yoga posture looks (Imagine what he looks like!)

现.equation: king 王 (60a) + see 见 (214) = the present 现
现.mnemonic: The king has special powers and can see everything going on at the present time in his kingdom. / [DIY pronunciation]

观.equation: right hand 又 (52) + see 见 (214) = observe 观
观.mnemonic: In the evening you meet an old friend in the street. You shake hands and see that he’s wearing a special watch, and he tells you he’s off to observe the stars. / In the observatory the ghostly giant will be up on the gantry readying the big telescopes and looking after them until your friend gets there.

再.mnemonic: The dwarf plans to run a small wheel again and again across his xylophone—and is looking forward to annoying the neighbors.

苦.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + old 古 (191a) = bitter 苦
苦.mnemonic: The donkey finds that the old grass tastes bitter. / Teddy says, “Mix it with couscous and it will taste better!”

入.mnemonic: Although it is not an HSK Level A character, it is useful to be able to recognize the sign 入口, “entrance”.

内.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + person 人 (12) = inside 内
内.mnemonic: Harry is on a bus tour and is about to enter the outer limits. the driver announces, 'All passengers must stay inside the bus while we travel through this dangerous area.' / The dwarf tour guide checks off the names of the passengers on his list (to make sure they're all accounted for).

呐.equation: mouth 口 (5) + inside 内 (219) = shout 呐
呐.mnemonic: (A drugs bust) they are standing at the mouth of the cave when, from inside, comes a shout. / "The dwarf has found a stash of narcotics!"

肉.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + person 人 (12) + person 人 (12) = meat 肉
肉.mnemonic: In the outer limits stands a totem pole made from one statue of Harry on top of another Harry: it’s advertising harry’s meat stall. / The dwarf ignores the rules and sneaks off the tour bus to buy meat for his Sunday roast.

两.equation: one 一 (1) + from 从 (103) + outer limits 冂 (160a) = couple 两
两.mnemonic: The zoo gets a unicorn from the outer limits, to obtain a couple of unicorns which they hope might breed (where have we heard this before?!) / The two teddies are set the task of watching the unicorns’ body language, to see if they are getting on!

辆.equation: car 车 (83) + couple 两 (222) = number of cars 辆
辆.mnemonic: (In the car showroom) the elderly couple sit in the car that they like most, tired after having been shown a number of cars. / The two dwarf salesmen had talked and talked, but the technical language (about big ends etc.) had been incomprehensible to them.

俩.equation: person 亻 (13a) + couple 两 (222) = two people 俩
俩.mnemonic: Harry only invited couples to his party, so that every time the doorbell rang, there were two people standing there. / Two teddies tried to gatecrash the party as a lark.

满.equation: water 氵 (78a) + scales (225a) = full 满
满.mnemonic: She has to measure the water on the scales, as her measuring jug is already full (of something else). / Teddy doesn’t know how to use the scales and has to read the manual.

互.mnemonic: (In the past a friend gave you their spare wheel when you had a flat tire) You now see this friend with a flat tire and stop to give them your spare wheel as a reciprocal favor. Behind you a dwarf hoots impatiently, wanting to get past.

它.equation: house 宀 (91a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = it 它
它.mnemonic: He builds a small house to accommodate his silver spoon. "Why does it get all the attention?" asks his pet dog jealously. / "Because spoons tarnish and dogs don't!" replies the giant.

比.equation: spoon 匕 (206a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = compared with 比
比.mnemonic: He took one spoon, then another spoon, and compared one with the other. / Teddy didn’t care which one he had, as long as he got to eat his beans.

批.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + compared with 比 (228) = criticize 批
批.mnemonic: The teacher looked at the hands of the children and compared them with her own — she criticized any failings such as dirty nails. / The ones with clean hands were allowed to take the pizza to the Giant.

切.equation: seven 七 (37) + knife 刀 (72) = cut 切
切.mnemonic: You look in your diary and see that seven days ago you met with the murder victim, so you use a knife to cut out the incriminating page. / The two dwarves are going round checking on everyone’s alibis.
