司.mnemonic: The blades were piled up in the production department because the sales department couldn’t sell them fast enough.

词.equation: words 讠 (67c) + department 司 (231b) = word 词
词.mnemonic: (A new boy is being shown round the factory) "the words department makes the words, so whenever you need a word they will supply it. / The fairy keeps statistics on which words are being requested most often."

典.equation: bend 曲 (232a) + eight 八 (20) = reference book 典
典.mnemonic: One of the octopus’ tentacles has a permanent bend in it, so he puts a heavy reference book on it to try to straighten it out. / The two teddies try using the same method to get rid of the dent in their frisbee!

纟.mnemonic: This fragment always appears as the left-hand side of composite characters. We will meet another form of it later (607b).

红.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + work 工 (147) = red 红
红.mnemonic: A young girl is sewing a quilt. She rests for a moment with the thread trailing across her work, then she looks down and sees a red stain spreading across the fabric — she has pricked her finger without realizing. / Luckily the fairy knows a magic stain-removing spell that she learnt in Hong Kong!

细.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + field 田 (156a) = slender 细
细.mnemonic: He strung the thread backwards and forwards across the field (to protect the crops from the birds). It was very slender so it would do the job without blocking the light. / The dwarf was satisfied as this would shield the crops from the birds (and save him from trying to keep them away).

其.mnemonic: “Do you want this or that wheel?” asked the salesman. “Whichever is cheaper,” the fairy said.

期.equation: this or that 其 (235) + moon 月 (61) = due 期
期.mnemonic: (Two telescopes are set up to watch the lunar eclipse) the curator says, “You can use this or that one to see the moon— but hurry, the eclipse is almost due.” / But clouds obscure the view, so the giant, on a nearby hill, holds up a lump of cheese with a bite out of it for them to look at instead!

基.equation: this or that 其 (235) + earth 土 (54a) = foundation 基
基.mnemonic: “Do you want to use this or that earth for the foundation?” the builder asks, pointing. / “In either case we’ll need so much that it’ll take the giant and his jeep to transport it.”

新.equation: kin 亲 (196) + axe 斤 (240) = new 新
新.mnemonic: He was so fed up with his kin that he felt like taking an axe to them and finding a new lot. / The giant chuckled to himself as he thought of the shindig that would create.

经.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + channel (244b) = go through 经
经.mnemonic: (On a potholing expedition) the leader tied a thread around his waist and swam along the channel to the next cave; this made it easier for the others to go through in turn after him. / Sitting in the final cave was a giant who jingled some bells (to lead them to him). [Note that we will distinguish jingling (bells) from jangling (coins, keys, chains and all other objects)].

轻.equation: car 车 (83) + channel (244b) = lightweight 轻
轻.mnemonic: Cars are being allowed along the drainage channel but only lightweight ones. / The giant who maintains the channel, has spotted a chink in the channel floor (and so has imposed a weight restriction).

乛.mnemonic: Compare this with 'knee' (166a): this is a much longer stroke with a short hook at the end.

实.equation: house 宀 (91a) + head 头 (246) = real 实
实.mnemonic: He dreamt that the house was falling on his head; he woke up and thought it was real, because his bed was covered in dust. / But it was only ash — the fairy had been chain-smoking cheroots!

旨.equation: spoon 匕 (206a) + sun 日 (6) = purpose 旨
旨.mnemonic: He left his spoon to warm in the sun— with the sole purpose of making it easier to bend back into shape.

指.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + purpose 旨 (251a) = point at 指
指.mnemonic: (In the school classroom the small children are doing hand-painting) The small boy puts his hand on the wall on purpose— and the others all point at the culprit. / Teddy suggests that they turn the paint splodge into a picture of a geranium.

仑.equation: tent 人 (19b) + spoon 匕 (206a) = meditate 仑
仑.mnemonic: You must sit in your tent with a spoon balanced on your finger in order to meditate properly.

论.equation: words 讠 (67c) + meditate 仑 (252a) = discuss 论
论.mnemonic: Read these inspirational words and meditate on them; afterwards we will discuss your insights. / The ghostly dwarf from London (imagine him with bowler hat and umbrella) has agreed to materialize and join the discussion.

认.equation: words 讠 (67c) + person 人 (12) = recognize 认
认.mnemonic: Words have been scrawled up around the school, saying “Harry go home!” — but everyone recognizes the handwriting. / The dwarf teacher finally spots the culprit in action and runs to apprehend him.

只.equation: mouth 口 (5) + eight 八 (20) = only; one of a pair 只
只.mnemonic: The octopus is at the mouth of the cave (it is an underwater cave and we are on the film set for “20,000 Leagues under the Sea”). The main scene will only work if they can film lots of swirling water. / Teddy suggests using a jacuzzi. They go to see if they can film in the giant’s jacuzzi. He gives them one of a pair of his old gloves to make a squid costume out of!

织.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + only 只 (254) = weave 织
织.mnemonic: This thread can only be used for weaving, not knitting. / The giant likes to relax in his jacuzzi and passes the time knitting (but finds this message written on the new thread he has just bought).

识.equation: words 讠 (67c) + only 只 (254) = knowledge 识
识.mnemonic: (A cookery program on TV) “The words of a recipe only mean something if you have some knowledge of how to cook.” / The fairy holds up a shallot (and adds, “You have to know your onions!”)

戊.equation: cliff 厂 (149) + dagger 戈 (32a) = fifth 戊
戊.mnemonic: Dagger and cliff are fused together here (similar to the situation for 我, Character 32). He embeds the dagger in the cliff, in the middle of the painted star, to indicate that he is of the fifth generation.

成.equation: blade (231a) + fifth 戊 (259a) = turn into 成
成.mnemonic: The blade of the fifth lord’s dagger turned into chocolate. / He looked around and there was a chunky fairy smiling mischievously — clearly she had done it.

城.equation: earth 土 (54a) + turn into 成 (259) = town 城
城.mnemonic: (A property developer shows investors a site) "This whole expanse of earth will turn into a town," he says. / The chunky fairy lands heavily at his feet and says, "Over my dead body."

者.equation: old man (261a) + sun 日 (6) = specialist 者
者.mnemonic: The old man has been out in the sun for too long (and has a sunburnt head) — so he has to go and see a specialist. / Teddy gives him a jersey to put over his bald head.

都.equation: specialist 者 (261) + city 阝 (96a) = all 都
都.mnemonic: The sheriff was wounded in the gunfight and had to go back east to the specialist in the city — all the people in the town clubbed together to pay for the treatment. / The giant carried him so that the sheriff could doze on the way to the hospital. / The spaceship crew land on the planet looking for a specialist in the city— but get directed to the metropolis. / The giant carries the sick alien wrapped up in a duvet.

犭.mnemonic: Recall that this is a distorted form of 犬 'dog' (238a).

猪.equation: dog 犭 (263a) + specialist 者 (261) = pig 猪
猪.mnemonic: The dog specialist (in the poodle parlor) looked up, astonished, as a customer brought a pig into the shop. / The giant wanted a jeweled collar (for his pet pig).

老.equation: old man  (261a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = old 老
老.mnemonic: The old man ate everything with a spoon. He said, “You can do what you like when you are as old as I am.” / Teddy copies him, slurping loudly.

孝.equation: old man  (261a) + child 子 (17) = filial piety 孝
孝.mnemonic: This strange-sounding phrase means respect for parents and ancestors, which is a powerful tradition in China. The old man was delighted to see that the child had been brought up to show filial piety (“Unlike most of the youth of today,” he mutters).

教.equation: filial piety 孝 (265a) + tap 攵 (190a) = teach 教
教.mnemonic: The filial piety teacher taps impatiently on his desk — he wants to get on and teach the lesson. / But the pupils are all distracted, listening to the sounds of the jousting tournament next door — two dwarves and two giants are in the semi-finals.

然.equation: dog food  (266b) + fire 灬 (185a) = however 然
然.mnemonic: The dog food has gone bad so they throw it on the fire— however, the fire then goes out. / The fairy can’t bear the rancid smell (so conjures the fire into life again).

当.equation: small 小 (50) + snout (267a) = act as 当
当.mnemonic: The top three strokes are a distorted version of 'small' (Character 50). The character means 'to act as' and also 'to regard as'. They look like small snouts, but act as gasmasks. / The giant is handing them out; he has them all dangling from his fingers.

尼.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = nun 尼
尼.mnemonic: In the convent hospital each corpse is sprinkled with holy water from a spoon by the senior nun. / [No pronunciation needed]

呢.equation: mouth 口 (5) + nun 尼 (268b) = as for 呢
呢.mnemonic: The nun’s mouth dropped open in disbelief. As for the other nuns, what about them? had they noticed it? / No — which was just as well, as they’d go nuts if they realized there was a robot creeping up behind them!

户.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + corpse 尸 (268a) = door 户
户.mnemonic: They put a drop of oil on the corpse so that they could squeeze it through the door. / The dwarf, outraged, said, “Remember who that is!”

所.equation: door 户 (269) + axe 斤 (240) = building 所
所.mnemonic: The burglars broke down the door with an axe to gain access to the building. / The ghostly teddy who guarded the building threw bars of soap at the intruders.

己.mnemonic: Teddy is fitting new special self-cleaning wheels to his jeep (and ends up in need of some cleaning, himself!).

记.equation: words 讠 (67c) + self 己 (271) = note down 记
记.mnemonic: (Stuck in a traffic jam) he has no paper, so has to write the words down on himself, as he wants to note down what has occurred. / In particular, he wants to report the dwarf who has blocked the road with his jeep.

纪.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + self 己 (271) = discipline 纪
纪.mnemonic: (A driver is trying to develop good habits) He ties some color-coded threads round the controls to encourage him to be more self-disciplined with his driving. / The dwarf won’t rent him a jeep until his driving improves.

已.mnemonic: When they got to the fair, there was already a long queue for the Big Wheel. Teddy sat in the queue eating Easter eggs (getting chocolate everywhere).

导.equation: snake 巳 (275a) + inch 寸 (104a) = guide 导
导.mnemonic: (A guided tour in the desert) Nobody saw the snake inching along the ground behind the tour guide. / That is, until Teddy ran up and doused it with a bucket of water (and chaos ensued).

钅.mnemonic: We will treat this as a basic building block. It is the form taken by the character 金 (which is in HSK Level B) when acting as the left-hand side of another character.

钟.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + middle 中 (33) = clock 钟
钟.mnemonic: In the middle of a gold block is set a clock. / The giant who made it had also etched his jonquil design into the gold. (Remember jonquils? See Character 33).

种.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + middle 中 (33) = species 种
种.mnemonic: The teacher says, “In the middle of the grain is the DNA which defines the species.” / Teddy jumps up waving a jonquil asking, “Does it work for this too?”

根.equation: tree 木 (10a) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = root 根
根.mnemonic: The tree stubbornly refused to move a single root. / The giant went to get his gunpowder to shift it.

银.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = silver 银
银.mnemonic: (At a retirement ceremony) “I want a gold watch,” he says stubbornly, “Silver is for wimps.” / The fairy whispers, “Don’t get stressed, it will upset your yin.”

良.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = good 良
良.mnemonic: A drop of oil on the stubborn hinge did a good job of loosening it up. (“Good,” said the workman, relieved).

娘.equation: woman 女 (15) + good 良 (286a) = young lady 娘
娘.mnemonic: In a deportment class, balancing books on the head, etc, the woman in charge said, 'Good, we'll make a young lady of you yet.' / DIY pronunciation.

食.equation: tent 人 (19b) + good 良 (286a) = food 食
食.mnemonic: (At the scout camp) “The tent’s up, that’s good. Now I need food,” said the scoutmaster. / The fairy started peeling shallots for a meal.

饣.mnemonic: This simplified form of Character 287 is used when it forms the left-hand part of a composite character.

饿.equation: food 饣 (288a) + I 我 (32) = hungry 饿
饿.mnemonic: (In the factory canteen the food is late in arriving) “Food, I want food! I’m hungry!” shout the workers. / The dwarf is the ringleader and urges them on.

问.equation: gate 门 (27) + mouth 口 (5) = ask 问
问.mnemonic: At the castle gate you put your mouth close to the grill and ask (politely) to be allowed in. / "Just this once," says the dwarf gatekeeper grudgingly.

闻.equation: gate 门 (27) + ear 耳 (88a) = hear 闻
闻.mnemonic: You put your ear to the gate to try and hear what’s going on inside. / The fairy is telling a fairy tale, starting with, “Once upon a time…”

访.equation: words 讠 (67c) + direction 方 (291) = visit 访
访.mnemonic: The words on the card are directions, as this is your first visit to these friends. / Teddy and Fang (the dog) are mucking about in the back seat of the car.

放.equation: direction 方 (291) + tap 攵 (190a) = release 放
放.mnemonic: The convicts communicate directions to their accomplice by tapping (on the pipes in Morse code), so that he can carry out the plan to release them. / The dwarf jailor sets Fang the dog to chase them as they try to escape.

旁.equation: stand 立 (177) + cover 冖 (82a) + direction 方 (291) = beside 旁
旁.mnemonic: He had to stand up tall to put the cover over the direction sign, and set up a new sign beside it. / The fairy had asked him to divert passing traffic past her new pancake stall.

万.equation: one 一 (1) + bow (51a) = ten thousand 万
万.mnemonic: The village elder goes on a pilgrimage to see the fabled unicorn, bows to him and explains the plight of the village. 'Ten thousand blessings will fall upon your village as a reward for your courage,' says the unicorn magnanimously. / [DIY pronunciation]

主.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + king 王 (60a) = lord 主
主.mnemonic: (At the ceremony) A drop of the special oil fell on the king when the man was presented, indicating that the man is to be made a lord. / Teddy is playing around twirling the jewel of office until it’s needed for the ceremony.

往.equation: step forward 彳 (81a) + lord 主 (297) = towards; go 往
往.mnemonic: Step forward, Lord Vader, and walk towards the ewok, said the director (on the film set of Star Wars). But there is no ewok! / Wang! Where's Wang the dwarf? calls the director. Teddy starts running around calling, Wang! Wang! Somebody go, go and find him!

兀.equation: one 一 (1) + boy 儿 (21) = pedestal 兀
兀.mnemonic: The unicorn uses his horn to lift the small boy onto the pedestal (so that people can see the new “chosen one”). / [No pronunciation needed].

玩.equation: king 王 (60a) + yuan 元 (301) = play 玩
玩.mnemonic: The king likes to sneak out with his pockets full of yuan coins and play the slot machines! / DIY pronunciation

南.equation: ten 十 (4) + outer limits 冂 (160a) + offend (307a) = south 南
南.mnemonic: If you offend against the rules in the outer limits, by going in areas marked with a cross, you’ll be sent to the South pole (to help count the penguins). / The fairy will conjure up a flying nanny goat to take you there!

幸.equation: earth 土 (54a) + offend (307a) = good fortune 幸
幸.mnemonic: You take a pot of earth to the housewarming party, which seems to offend the new householder — this mystifies you as in your own culture it brings good fortune. / The dwarf behind you has brought a pot of shingle (and wonders how this will be received …)

平.equation: dry 干 (124) + horns 丷 (65a) = level 平
平.mnemonic: The cow put her horns inside the tumble dryer — which were such a weight that she had to check it was level before she could switch it on. / The fairy used the level surface as a ping-pong table.

苹.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + level 平 (309) = apple 苹
苹.mnemonic: To test whether the grass is level he uses an apple (seeing if it rolls around). / The fairy suggests using a ping-pong ball instead (so she can eat the apple!)

评.equation: words 讠 (67c) + level 平 (309) = comment on 评
评.mnemonic: A sign-writer trying to get his words level (on a shop sign) asks people across the street to comment on how he’s doing. / The fairy gives a definitive view by using her ping-pong ball as a spirit level.

事.mnemonic: The Affair of the Wheel is the title of a slushy romance between two cyclists. After a few pages the dwarf throws the book down in disgust, saying, What a charade!

此.equation: stop 止 (29a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = this 此
此.mnemonic: Stop using that spoon to eat your soup — use this one!

倍.equation: person 亻 (13a) + spit (315a) = multiple 倍
倍.mnemonic: Harry had to spit to get rid of the vile taste — but as the spit hit the floor it turned into a tiny version of him. he kept spitting until there were multiple versions of him everywhere. / The dwarf had to be called on to take the baby Harry’s away!

部.equation: spit (315a) + city 阝 (96a) = section 部
部.mnemonic: Spitting was not allowed in many parts of the city but the bylaw varied from section to section. / The dwarf could boot out anyone found breaking the law.

北.equation: firewood 丬 (317a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = north 北
北.mnemonic: He packed his firewood and his trusty spoon in his pack, and set off for the far north. / Teddy ran after him crying, 'You've forgotten your bacon.'

东.mnemonic: The giant took the big wheel from the east gate of the city (to fix his cart) and made a quick getaway on his donkey!

乐.mnemonic: This story is attempting to give you some help with what is really a DIY pronunciation. With the pronunciation lè, 乐 also means “happy”, as in the phrase “happy Birthday”. / The music box on wheels (that is, a barrel organ) has been set up outside the dwarf’s house again, and he comes out to complain: “You, eh? I’ve told you before, go and play somewhere else!”

算.equation: bamboo (321a) + eye 目 (93) + two hands (85a) = reckon 算
算.mnemonic: The pirate captain raised the bamboo telescope to his eye and steadied it with two hands. 'I reckon I’ll need fifty men to storm that castle,' he said to his crew. / But it was actually a sand castle that the ghostly dwarf, who guarded the island, had built to deter pirates.

第.mnemonic: (In the panpipes factory) the bamboo pipes have to be sorted into the right sequence and labeled Number 1, Number 2, and so on. / The dwarf has been demoted (so is given this job, which he thinks is beneath him).

弟.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + sequence (322a) = younger brother 弟
弟.mnemonic: The goats were all being lined up, the size of their horns determining their place in the sequence. One goat noticed that his younger brother had sneaked in above him. / He told the dwarf (organizing the line-up), so that his brother was demoted.

劳.equation: glowing (324a) + power 力 (14) = toil 劳
劳.mnemonic: Keeping the furnaces glowing at the power station was hard toil. / The fairy played loud music for the workers (so that they could hear it over the roar of the furnaces while they worked).

加.equation: power 力 (14) + mouth 口 (5) = add 加
加.mnemonic: A power socket has been installed by the mouth of each cave, to add the cave dwelling area to the electricity grid. / Two giant workmen arrive, carrying a huge jar of plugs and switches between them.

驾.equation: add 加 (325) + horse 马 (44) = drive 驾
驾.mnemonic: The cart salesman says, “all you have to do is add a horse and you can drive it away!” / The two dwarves who are thinking of buying the cart are now arguing over who should smash the jar against the side to “launch” it (like a boat) and who should drive it away.

咖.equation: mouth 口 (5) + add 加 (325) = coffee 咖
咖.mnemonic: As he put the mug to his mouth he remembered that he must add milk to his coffee. / The giant got a carton of milk from his fridge.

非.mnemonic: “It’s un-wheel” was the catch-phrase of the famous giant with the speech impediment!

啡.equation: mouth 口 (5) + un- 非 (328) = coffee 啡
啡.mnemonic: As he put the mug to his mouth he realized it was unsweetened coffee. / The famous giant pulled a face.

排.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + un- 非 (328) = line 排
排.mnemonic: He had to use his bare hands to un couple the line of rail cars. / The fairy whispered to him to disconnect the pipes too.

反.equation: drag (150a) + right hand 又 (52) = oppose 反
反.mnemonic: The older form of this character uses 'cliff' instead of 'drag' and you will still see this form around. / The young prince has to be dragged along to shake hands with all the dignitaries, but is opposed to all the pomp and circumstance (and vows to do things differently when he is king). / Teddy is happy though, as he gets to play fanfares on his trumpet.

饭.equation: food 饣 (288a) + oppose 反 (331) = meal 饭
饭.mnemonic: There were so many types of food that the fussy guest was opposed to (on ideological grounds) that it was difficult to put together a meal for him. / The dwarf chef demands that a fanfare be played (to accompany his entrance with the resulting meal, after all the trouble he’s gone to).

板.equation: tree 木 (10a) + oppose 反 (331) = board 板
板.mnemonic: The tree is opposed to the new highway and he is certainly opposed to them nailing a board to him announcing the development. / Teddy pulls the board off and bandages up the holes.

米.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + tree 木 (10a) = rice 米
米.mnemonic: (A party game) "animal horns have been hidden in the tree, with rice inside — you have to climb up, find a horn and bring it down without spilling any rice. Who wants to try it?" / "Me", says Teddy, "me, me, me!"

青.equation: plant  (133a) + moon 月 (61) = blue-green 青
青.mnemonic: The character means either “blue” or “green” (though there are also separate characters for each of these two colors) so we have called it both! / (On a cliff face) The plant in the moon- light looks blue, but it’s actually green. / Only the giant is tall enough to see into the chink of rock where the plant is growing.

请.equation: words 讠 (67c) + blue-green 青 (335) = please 请
请.mnemonic: The words are written in big blue-green metal letters on the cliff face: 'Please don’t disturb the plants.' / Teddy clambers up on the letters to peer into the chink.

清.equation: water 氵 (78a) + blue-green 青 (335) = transparent 清
清.mnemonic: When water falls on the blue-green plant it turns it transparent. / The giant verifies this by peeping into the chink in the rock.

晴.equation: sun 日 (6) + blue-green 青 (335) = fine weather 晴
晴.mnemonic: The sun turning the plant blue-green again is the first sign of fine weather. / The fairy keeps flitting up to the chink to check out this natural barometer.

忄.mnemonic: This is the form taken by Character 161 when it appears as the left-hand side of a character. Some books suggest writing the strokes in the order left to right.

情.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + blue-green 青 (335) = state of affairs 情
情.mnemonic: The heart of the plant only turns blue-green when this state of affairs (the fine weather) persists. / Only the fairy can get right inside the chink to see the color change.

睛.equation: eye 目 (93) + blue-green 青 (335) = pupil 睛
睛.mnemonic: Color your eyes with blue-green eye shadow to set off your pupils! / The giant fancies a job in advertising and is trying to write jingles (this is his first effort, for a cosmetics company).

精.equation: rice 米 (334) + blue-green 青 (335) = splendid 精
精.mnemonic: “Blue-green rice is splendid!” / Another of the giant’s advertising jingles.

亮.equation: gazebo (342a) + several 几 (7) = shining 亮
亮.mnemonic: In the gazebo were several golden statues of a young boy, shining so brightly in the sun that they were dazzling passers by. / The two dwarves had found the statues languishing in a back room at the museum and had polished them up (rather too well it seems!)

亭.equation: gazebo (342a) + nail 丁 (81b) = pavilion 亭
亭.mnemonic: Inside the gazebo is a box of nails so the carpenter can convert it into a pavilion (by nailing up walls to it).

停.equation: person 亻 (13a) + pavilion 亭 (343a) = halt 停
停.mnemonic: Harry wanders into the pavilion and halts in his tracks. / A beautiful fairy hovering near the ceiling sends a tingle down his spine.

单.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + field 田 (156a) + ten 十 (4) = single 单
单.mnemonic: They carried horns into the field and tied them to a cross which marked where the single rare flower would bloom. / Unfortunately the giant came along and picked it, thinking it was a dandelion!

间.equation: gate 门 (27) + sun 日 (6) = room 间
间.mnemonic: The huge city gates blocked the sun from the room. / The two giants set up a generator to provide lighting.

简.equation: bamboo 竹 (321a) + room 间 (345) = simple 简
简.mnemonic: The bamboo stands ready in the room and a piece of paper headed 'Simple Instructions' is pinned to the wall. / The two teddies (rather optimistically) set about building their own generator.

合.equation: combine (59a) + mouth 口 (5) = join together 合
合.mnemonic: (At the end of the marriage ceremony) They combine mouths in a kiss for so long that they found they had become joined together. / All very embarrassing, as they had to shuffle along to the fairy who brewed up a herbal remedy to separate them.

拿.equation: join together 合 (347) + hand 手 (31) = using 拿
拿.mnemonic: To make a long table the carpenter joined together two pieces of wood — with a hand underneath to support them while using a screwdriver in the other hand. / The fairy had supplied wood with a beautiful swirling grain from the gnarled tree.

哈.equation: mouth 口 (5) + join together 合 (347) = laugh 哈
哈.mnemonic: At the mouth of the cave the potholers are joined together with ropes so nobody can get lost, when they hear a laugh booming round the cave. / They run in all directions, falling over the ropes, and the giant laughs even harder.

给.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + join together 合 (347) = for 给
给.mnemonic: He took colored threads which he joined together, plaiting them into a bracelet. But who is it for? / For Teddy’s new girlfriend, the geisha.

答.equation: bamboo 竹 (321a) + join together 合 (347) = answer 答
答.mnemonic: In the old days a couple had to ask the village bamboo pipe if they were suitable to join together in marriage — a lot depended on the answer it gave. / But it was really only the fairy in the dark who was whispering the answers.

片.mnemonic: (It’s the racing driver’s birthday) They all want a slice of the birthday cake, which is in the shape of a steering wheel. The two dwarf chefs carry the cake up to the penthouse where the party is in full swing.

叫.equation: mouth 口 (5) + entangle (354a) = call 叫
叫.mnemonic: At the mouth of the cave you have become entangled in the brambles and call for help. / Unfortunately the two mountain rescue dwarves are watching the jousting on TV (and can’t hear you calling as they are too busy arguing over which is the better team).

收.equation: entangle (354a) + tap 攵 (190a) = accept 收
收.mnemonic: (In a mountaineering contest) A climber gets entangled in his ropes and has to tap on the rocks to attract attention — he has to accept help from his bitter rival. / The giant, who would normally do the rescues, is away watching the show-jumping.

卑.mnemonic: You could think of this as 'drop' plus 'field' plus a distorted form of 'entangle'.

啤.equation: mouth 口 (5) + inferior 卑 (356a) = beer 啤
啤.mnemonic: Normally I don’t let anything inferior into my mouth, but what I really fancy right now is a beer. / It’s just what I need to go with my pizza, said the fairy (slumming it a bit).

讲.equation: words 讠 (67c) + a well 井 (357a) = talk 讲
讲.mnemonic: Mysterious words have appeared, etched around the inside of the village well, and the villagers can talk about nothing else. / The two teddies, eager to investigate down the well, jangle the chain as one lowers the other down in the bucket.

改.equation: self 己 (271) + tap 攵 (190a) = alter 改
改.mnemonic: The magician needed merely to tap himself with his wand to alter his appearance completely. / Teddy (dressed up as his assistant) cries, “Will you look at this guy!”

冫.equation: water 水 (80a) - 1 drop = ice 冫
冫.mnemonic: This is 'water' (80a), but with one less drop.

况.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + older brother 兄 (67a) = situation 况
况.mnemonic: His older brother fell head-first through the ice. 'That’s another fine situation you’ve got me into,' he spluttered. / The ghostly ice-guardian dwarf bounds across on his kangaroo to investigate.

欠.equation: bow (51a) + person 人 (12) = lacking 欠
欠.mnemonic: As he watched the man bowing to him, Harry found it difficult not to laugh as the man’s trousers fell down, lacking a belt to keep them up!

次.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + lacking 欠 (361a) = times 次
次.mnemonic: The ice in the rink was lacking depth for the umpteenth time. / The dwarf started keeping statistics on the number of times it happened (so that he wouldn’t be blamed by the management).

吹.equation: mouth 口 (5) + lacking 欠 (361a) = puff 吹
吹.mnemonic: The mouth of the cave is lacking a door so every puff of wind comes in. / But the owner wants to see who’s coming to visit, so he can welcome them before the (overzealous) ghostly giant, who guards the cave, chases them away.

欢.equation: right hand 又 (52) + lacking 欠 (361a) = joyful 欢
欢.mnemonic: (The New Year celebration at the orphanage) They all shake hands, and despite lacking the basics they are all joyful. / The ghostly giant, who guards the orphanage, hands out handfuls of hand-made streamers!

久.mnemonic: (A guided tour in an old mine) “This wheel hasn’t turned in a long time.” But then it moves! Two teddies had got it moving for a joke (and roll around laughing to see the tour guide’s shocked face).

爪.mnemonic: The old lady always puts a beret over her hands when she reaches down for something, so that nobody can see that her hand looks like a claw.

爱.equation: hand down (365c) + friend 友 (53) = love 爱
爱.mnemonic: The dying duke says, “I intend to hand down all my possessions to you when I die; you have been a true friend to me and shown me the only love I’ve ever known.” / The dwarf butler actually smiles: this had melted the ice in his heart.

暖.equation: sun 曰 (6) + love 爱 (365) = warm 暖
暖.mnemonic: If you look very closely, you will see that the “cover” in the middle of the right-hand side of this character has been abbreviated into a simple horizontal line. / The holidaymakers lie in the sun and love the feeling of being so warm. / The ghostly teddy (who guards their well-being) rides up and down the beach on a nanny goat (to warn people against getting sun burnt).

父.equation: eight 八 (20) + shears 乂 (25a) = father 父
父.mnemonic: (At the barber’s) Picture the octopus wielding a pair of shears, giving a haircut to your father! / In the next chair along, a dwarf is grumpily trimming the beard of Fu Manchu (he doesn’t hold with all this personal vanity).

吧.equation: mouth 口 (5) + wait for 巴 (368a) = suggestion 吧
吧.mnemonic: You open your mouth and wait for the dentist to start work, but instead he says, “I have a suggestion. / Perhaps you’d like to try my robot assistant — he can do this work at a bargain price.”

把.equation: hand 才 (152a) + wait for 巴 (368a) = handle 把
把.mnemonic: (There’s a queue for the sale at the department store) You are at the front of the queue waiting for the shop to open, your hand ready to grab the door handle as soon as the door is unlocked. / Behind you, Teddy excitedly hops around, hoping to bag a bargain.

爸.equation: father 父 (367) + wait for 巴 (368a) = dad 爸
爸.mnemonic: (In the queue for the sale at the department store) Imagine waiting for your father to come (so that you will be allowed in). When he arrives you shout, “Dad!” / You both race off to stop the dwarf grabbing all the bargains.

爬.equation: claw 爪 (365a) + wait for 巴 (368a) = climb 爬
爬.mnemonic: The cat sits with its claws at the ready, and waits for the mouse to come through the hole in the fence — but the mouse climbs over the fence instead. / Nothing will stop him getting to one of the fairy’s legendary parties.

关.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + heaven 天 (76) = switch off 关
关.mnemonic: (Arriving at a fancy dress party in Heaven) "You shouldn’t wear horns in Heaven— especially not plastic ones with flashing lights — switch them off at once!" / The voice came from a ghostly giant sitting on a gantry above the Pearly Gates.

送.equation: road 辶 (26a) + switch off 关 (372) = deliver 送
送.mnemonic: The delivery van driver’s instructions read: “Park on the road, and switch off your engine before you deliver the packet. / Otherwise the dwarf will make a song and dance about the fumes.”

联.equation: ear 耳 (88a) + switch off 关 (372) = connect 联
联.mnemonic: The bomb disposal expert has been called to deal with an old unexploded bomb. He puts his ear to the casing until he hears the mechanism switch off, then he can connect up the two wires to make it safe. / Two fairies fly in with a bowl of lentil soup (to relax with, afterwards).

言.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + two 二 (2) + mouth 口 (5) = words 言
言.mnemonic: In the biplane you can see your co-pilot’s mouth moving but can’t hear what she’s saying (because of the wind noise), so you fit a canopy or lid on top so you can hear each other’s words. / The fairy has a yen to go for a ride (and might do so now that the canopy will protect her hairdo!)

信.equation: person 亻 (13a) + words 言 (375) = letter 信
信.mnemonic: Harry is good at reading documents written using old words, and people often bring him old letters to decipher. / The dwarf brings him an old shin bone with writing on it (hoping to catch him out).

少.equation: small 小 (50) + slide (34b) = few 少
少.mnemonic: The small slide is for small children only — but there are only a few slides like this in the whole country. / Teddy clambers up and shouts with joy as he slides down. (Teddies are allowed on too as they’re very light.)

省.equation: few 少 (377) + eye 目 (93) = province 省
省.mnemonic: The governor chooses a few trusted officials to keep an eye on the province while he is away on his next foreign trip. / DIY pronunciation

饺.equation: food 饣 (288a) + pay 交 (380) = dumpling 饺
饺.mnemonic: (In the canteen) “The food is a disgrace, you have to pay so much, and then all you get is dumplings.” / The two teddies (the cooks) are watching the jousting on TV (so haven’t bothered to cook anything else).

牛.mnemonic: While the criminal was on parole he had to wear a label with a picture of a cow on it — to show that his job was to look after the cows. / It took two fairies (working in shifts) to keep an eye on this notorious criminal.

件.equation: person 亻 (13a) + cow 牛 (384) = number of things 件
件.mnemonic: (In the “Best Trained Cow” competition at the village fete) Harry trains his cow to do a number of things (juggling, standing on its head, etc.) / It finishes by genuflecting to the two dwarf adjudicators.

哥.equation: may 可 (140) + may 可 (140) = older brother 哥
哥.mnemonic: At the May celebrations there are two May poles, one for the older children and one for the smaller children. A small boy dances round the smaller pole and looks across to where his older brother is dancing round the other one. / The giant accompanies the dance with a tune on chimes made out of girders.

歌.equation: older brother 哥 (386) + lacking 欠 (361a) = song 歌
歌.mnemonic: Your older brother is totally lacking in talent but insists on singing his favorite song. / The giant accompanies him on the chimes made out of girders (but unfortunately can't quite drown him out).

应.equation: shelter 广 (170) + haul up (388a) = should 应
应.mnemonic: The hermit who lives in the mountains hauls up his supplies (in a basket on a rope) and stores them in a shelter next to his hut. each delivery should be enough to last a month (but he always eats them up early). / [DIY pronunciation]

向.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + skylight (389a) = towards 向
向.mnemonic: (Looking around caves, with a view to buying one) A drop of rain falls on the skylight and they realize it faces towards the prevailing winds. / The two dwarves argue back and forth for a while but finally decide this is a small price to pay to live in Shanghai.

响.equation: mouth 口 (5) + towards 向 (389) = noisy 响
响.mnemonic: The mouth of the cave faces towards a noisy part of town. / The two teddies, though, are excited: 'We’ll be able to see all the Shanghai goings-on.'

化.equation: person 亻 (13a) + seven 七 (37) = change 化
化.mnemonic: Harry looks in his diary and sees that in seven days he has to change the clock on the church tower (to daylight saving time). / Last time he had forgotten and the ghostly dwarf who guards the tower had given him a hard time.

花.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + change 化 (391) = flower 花
花.mnemonic: Rather than just having grass, she wants to change part of her garden into a flower bed. / She employs the ghostly giant to look after the flowers, and he uses his hard hat to protect them from frost.

历.equation: cliff 厂 (149) + power 力 (14) = experience 历
历.mnemonic: He builds the power plant at the bottom of the cliff— experience has taught him that this is a good place for a hydroelectric plant. / The dwarf has to go round checking for leaks in the pipes.

旧.equation: stick 丨 (19a) + sun 日 (6) = outdated 旧
旧.mnemonic: Using a stick and the sun is a very outdated way of telling the time. / The two dwarf park keepers mutter to each other — they think it is a joke that something so outdated is still used, and in their park too.

业.mnemonic: The dwarf had created a whole industry out of making wheels, employing yetis to do the work.

亚.equation: one 一 (1) + industry 业 (395) = Asia 亚
亚.mnemonic: The unicorn industry churns out plastic unicorns, each with “Made in Asia” stamped underneath. / [No pronunciation needed]

並.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + Asia 亚 (396a) = stand side by side 並
並.mnemonic: In the high plateaus of Asia they play tunes on animal horns— standing side by side (like a huge set of pan-pipes).

碰.equation: stone 石 (115a) + stand side by side 並 (396b) = bump into 碰
碰.mnemonic: Imagine setting up large (Stonehenge-type) stones like a huge domino run — they stand side by side so that when each one falls it will bump into the next one. / The dwarf warns people to be punctual or they will miss this big event.

史.mnemonic: The history lesson was about the invention of the wheel. Teddy is bored by this class and sits munching shallots at the back. (Smell his breath!)

使.equation: person 亻 (13a) + an official 吏 (398a) = use 使
使.mnemonic: (In the gallery of modern art) Harry asks the official if he can use his camera. / He wants a picture of the sculpture “Teddy sitting on a pile of shallots”.

英.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + center 央 (401a) = hero 英
英.mnemonic: Who’s that standing in the center of the grass? It’s a statue of the english hero, Nelson. / DIY pronunciation

块.equation: earth 土 (54a) + separate 夬 (402a) = lump 块
块.mnemonic: (Someone sneaks into the vegetable gardens) he separates the good earth from the lumps which he dumps on a neighboring plot. / The ghostly dwarf, who looks after the gardens, hoists a red kite to alert the authorities.

快.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + separate 夬 (402a) = quick 快
快.mnemonic: If conjoined twins are joined near the heart then the operation to separate them has to be quick. / The ghostly dwarf (who has been assigned to protect the twins) decides that he can’t be expected to hang around and watch so goes outside to fly his kite during the operation.

决.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + separate 夬 (402a) = decide 决
决.mnemonic: (You’re out skating on a frozen lake) the ice starts to separate, and you have one foot on each side of the gap so have to decide very quickly which way you’re going to jump! / [DIY pronunciation]

觉.equation: unveil (82c) + see 见 (214) = feel 觉 / unveil (82c) + see 见 (214) = sleep 觉
觉.mnemonic: They unveiled the new name on the front of the saloon so that everyone could see—it had been named after the local outlaw. But how would this make the relatives of the outlaw’s victims feel? (picture a widow bursting into tears). / The spaceship captain unveiled the statue (to be left behind on the planet) and wondered why everyone was laughing — they could see the sculptor who had been sleeping under the cover. / The crew wondered why a statue of two dwarves jousting was appropriate.

定.equation: house 宀 (91a) + upright 疋 (30b) = decide on 定
定.mnemonic: Since the house was on a riverbank he checked carefully that it was upright before deciding on whether to buy it. / The dwarf was happy that he could tie up his dinghy alongside.

重.mnemonic: The heavy wheel on your tractor squashed this rare jonquil, said the policeman. The dwarf can't see what the fuss is about (It was only a flower, for goodness' sake, he mutters).

董.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + heavy 重 (407) = director 董
董.mnemonic: (The young office workers have sneaked into the washrooms for a “cigarette” break) “this grass is heavy, man,” says one. But the other one sees that the director is coming! / [No pronunciation needed]

懂.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + director 董 (408a) = understand 懂
懂.mnemonic: The heart director (head of the cardiac unit) is trying to get his students to understand irregular heartbeats. / He gets Teddy to lead a donkey round the lecture hall (so that the hoofbeats drum out an irregular rhythm).

垂.mnemonic: Notice how this differs from 重 (Character 407 above), and be careful with the stroke order. The two small vertical strokes on either side are joined on at the top and bottom in some typefaces (this is shown in the large character here) and not in others (as shown in the stroke-order diagrams).

睡.equation: eye 目 (93) + droop 垂 (409a) = sleep 睡
睡.mnemonic: His eyes began to droop and all he wanted to do was sleep. / Fortunately the ghostly dwarf (guarding the emperor’s palace) was wearing shades, so nobody could see his eyes close.

飞.mnemonic: The chairman of a car component company is reporting to shareholders) “We were prepared to fly the wheel long-distance to the famous giant— it was worthwhile to get the celebrity endorsement.

气.equation: clouds  (124b) + one 一 (1) + bird’s wing  (410a) = air 气
气.mnemonic: Clouds gathered above the unicorn when he discovered the dead bird with a broken wing. he sniffed the air angrily. / He would tell his suspicions to the dwarf police chief when he arrived.

汽.equation: water 氵 (78a) + air 气 (411) = steam 汽
汽.mnemonic: (A crime scene in a bathroom) "Water in the air becomes steam— so if we run the hot tap to warm the room again, we will see if the victim wrote a message on the bathroom mirror," the junior detective said. / "I’m the chief, I’ll give the orders," said the dwarf police chief in charge.

乙.mnemonic: Be careful not to confuse this with 'bird’s wing' (410a).

亿.equation: person 亻 (13a) + second 乙 (413a) = hundred million 亿
亿.mnemonic: Harry came second in the contest to guess the distance from the earth to the Sun. he said a hundred million miles. / The dwarf logged the guesses on a blackboard set up on an easel.

艺.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + second 乙 (413a) = art 艺
艺.mnemonic: (In the soccer stadium) When cutting the grass it is the second cut that creates the patterns and turns it into a work of art. / The dwarf planned the design out on an easel.

乞.equation: clouds (124b) + second 乙 (413a) = beg 乞
乞.mnemonic: The clouds had a competition to make interesting shapes. Second prize went to a cloud who took the form of a person begging.

吃.equation: mouth 口 (5) + beg 乞 (415a) = eat 吃
吃.mnemonic: The refugee didn’t speak the local language so pointed to his mouth, to beg for something to eat. / The giant took pity on him and gave him a chapati.

全.equation: tent 人 (19b) + king 王 (60a) = whole 全
全.mnemonic: The king sat in his tent— he was so plump that he took up the whole space. / DIY pronunciation

色.equation: bow (51a) + wait for 巴 (368a) = color 色
色.mnemonic: The monk was bowing at the pedestrian crossing, waiting for the lights to change color. / His dwarf servant (fed up of this) impatiently pressed the button!

角.equation: bow (51a) + use 用 (130) = dime 角
角.mnemonic: The beggar bows to passers by and says, “Buddy, I could use a dime.” / He’s a bit miffed as further down the sidewalk two teddies are jousting to amuse the crowds (and raking it in).

确.equation: stone 石 (115a) + dime 角 (418) = certain 确
确.mnemonic: (They tossed for it) The dime landed on the stone. "heads — I win!" "Let me see — are you certain?" / [DIY pronunciation]

嘴.equation: mouth 口 (5) + heron (420a) = mouth 嘴
嘴.mnemonic: The heron had a fish in its mouth, which it took back to its nest to put into the mouth of its young chick. / [DIY pronunciation]

最.equation: sun 日 (6) + acquire 取 (88) = utmost 最
最.mnemonic: “I would acquire the sun for you if I could, to prove my utmost love,” he said (while she reached for the sick bag!). / [DIY pronunciation]

炎.equation: fire 火 (181) + fire 火 (181) = inflammation 炎
炎.mnemonic: I can’t believe it — a fire on top of last week’s fire, now the inflammation on my burnt hand is even worse.

谈.equation: words 讠 (67c) + inflammation 炎 (422a) = talk over 谈
谈.mnemonic: In a hospital, the nurse said, “I’ve written down a few words for you about this inflammation but our burns expert can talk over the details with you. / I’ll get the fairy to fly off and arrange a tannoy call for him.”

写.equation: cover 冖 (82a) + with 与 (423a) = write 写
写.mnemonic: (In the school exam) The boy covers his paper with his hand so that his neighbor can’t see what he writes. / The two teddies have been put one at each end of the shelf (so that they can’t distract anyone during the exam).

号.equation: mouth 口 (5) + choke 丂 (424a) = date 号
号.mnemonic: (Reading the newspaper at breakfast) he’s just put a spoonful of cereal in his mouth, and chokes when he spots the date on the newspaper (he’s forgotten their wedding anniversary!) / The dwarf butler is very houseproud, and mutters as he sweeps up the cornflakes from the floor.

度.equation: shelter 广 (170) + twenty 廿 (425a) + right hand 又 (52) = degrees 度
度.mnemonic: The farmer agrees to buy twenty shelters for his cows and shakes hands on the deal; under the agreement they’ll be delivered when the temperature drops to twenty degrees. / The dwarf salesman says he’ll also throw in a duvet jacket for the cowherd.

态.equation: too much 太 (48) + heart 心 (161) = attitude 态
态.mnemonic: (At the vet’s surgery) “Too much food is bad for your heart— you need to develop a more healthy attitude to eating.” / The dwarf tells the tiger to eat smaller portions (not noticing the tiger sizing him up …)

帀.equation: one 一 (1) + towel 巾 (427a) = encompass 帀
帀.mnemonic: The grateful villagers made the unicorn a large towel which was big enough to encompass him (even his horn).

师.equation: knife 刂 (74a) + encompass 帀 (428a) = master 师
师.mnemonic: (At the murder crime scene) They drew a chalk line round the body which encompassed the knife he was holding — they would need a master detective to solve this. / The giant spotted a blood-soaked shillelagh on top of a wardrobe ("I didn’t need to be a master detective to spot this," he said).

带.mnemonic: (In the embassy laundry) at the end of each month they had to wash thirty sashes for the ambassador, a different one for each day. "Why can't he just wear a belt?" the laundry worker complained. / The dwarf (supervisor) told him that they were all dyed different colors (and it was important for protocol reasons to have the right one for each occasion).

示.equation: two 二 (2) + small 小 (50) = sign 示
示.mnemonic: A small (remote-control model) biplane is flying around, doing sign writing in the sky. / The dwarf smokes his cheroot (and sulks now that nobody’s interested in his smoke rings any more).

票.equation: west 西 (100) + sign 示 (430) = ticket 票
票.mnemonic: (Tours of the White house) at the entrance to the West Wing, the sign says “Special tickets beyond this point”. / The two dwarves see a ticket on the ground and both pounce on it.

漂.equation: water 氵 (78a) + ticket 票 (431) = pretty 漂
漂.mnemonic: On the water floats a ticket to see the “Pretty Maids” dancing troupe. / The two dwarves both pounce on this ticket too.

礻.mnemonic: This is the form which Character 430 takes when appearing as the left-hand side of other characters. This is easy to mistake for “clothes” 衤 (439a), which has one more stroke.

社.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + earth 土 (54a) = society 社
社.mnemonic: Look for the sign on the building depicting little heaps of earth— that’s the house where the mole-fanciers’ society meets. / The dwarf sherpa is giving a talk tonight on ‘Mountains and Mole-hills’.

祝.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + older brother 兄 (67a) = best wishes 祝
祝.mnemonic: A sign in the sky — his older brother had organized a skywriting airplane to send him best wishes for his birthday. / The dwarf said it was juvenile (probably because he hadn’t thought of it).

视.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + see 见 (214) = to watch 视
视.mnemonic: (At the zoo) The sign says, “This is the place to see the feeding of the tigger” — so all the children stop to watch. / The dwarf feeds it shallots (for this is what tiggers like best) whilst grumbling to himself that people in his position shouldn’t have to feed the animals that are there purely for entertainment.

表.mnemonic: The little girl puts an old dress on a plant in her front garden, as an indicator of which house is holding the birthday party. / Two teddies stand on either side of the front gate, bowing to guests as they arrive.

衣.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + dress  (436a) = clothes 衣
衣.mnemonic: She lifts the lid of the box to reveal a beautiful dress— she has never been given clothes like this as a present before. / She doesn’t know that the giant is her secret admirer and that he wants to see her looking her best at the Easter parade.

农.equation: cover 冖 (82a) + dress (436a) = farm 农
农.mnemonic: She wears overalls to cover her dress when she is working on the farm. / [DIY pronunciation]

初.equation: clothes 衤 (439a) + knife 刀 (72) = beginning 初
初.mnemonic: The doctors cut away his clothes with a knife before beginning their examination (of the road accident victim). / The giant tries to stick the clothes together again with chewing gum!

皮.mnemonic: Watch the stroke order for this one.

被.equation: clothes 衤 (439a) + skin 皮 (440a) = by 被
被.mnemonic: A caveman sees his friend wearing new clothes made of animal skin. Who were they made by? By Ugg? By Zugg? Or by Nugg? / Actually, by the dwarf who lives down in the basement of his cave.

破.equation: stone 石 (115a) + skin 皮 (440a) = damage 破
破.mnemonic: Rubbing a stone on his skin was damaging it. / But the dwarf itched where he had been scratched by the porcupine.

坏.equation: earth 土 (54a) + not 不 (9) = bad 坏
坏.mnemonic: (An archaeologist is digging) “This is not earth— I’ve got a bad feeling about this.” / At that moment a ghostly dwarf, who guards the tombs, appears and unleashes a hyena to chase him off.

且.mnemonic: They really needed wheels to move the heavy bookcase but all the two teddies could find were chestnuts to put underneath it!

姐.equation: woman 女 (15) + bookcase 且 (443) = older sister 姐
姐.mnemonic: Who’s that woman cleaning the bookcase? That’s my older sister. / She’s cleaning up after the two teddies had a jelly fight.

助.equation: bookcase 且 (443) + power 力 (14) = help 助
助.mnemonic: We need more man power to move the heavy bookcase— we need some help. / The dwarf takes a swig of his magic juice and picks up the bookcase nonchalantly with one hand. (At last, his chance to shine!)

祖.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + bookcase 且 (443) = ancestor 祖
祖.mnemonic: (In the library) The sign on the genealogy bookcase shows where to look for books on the ancestors. / Teddy dresses up as a Zulu (as he has heard his ancestors came from Africa).

组.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + bookcase 且 (443) = organize 组
组.mnemonic: You decide to put different colors of thread on the shelves of the bookcase to organize its contents. / When the thread runs out, Teddy offers to dress up as a Zulu to mark the African section for you.

宜.equation: house 宀 (91a) + bookcase 且 (443) = appropriate 宜
宜.mnemonic: The new house has lots of bookcases, and it is going to take a long time to find an appropriate place for each book. / 'Easy if you know how,' says the fairy as she waves her magic wand.

谊.equation: words 讠 (67c) + appropriate 宜 (449) = friendship 谊
谊.mnemonic: He was looking for a greeting card with words which would be appropriate to show his friendship (but they were all soppy birthday cards). / The dwarf gave up and sent an email instead.

直.equation: ten 十 (4) + treasure (451a) = straight 直
直.mnemonic: (The girl has found a map) “at the cross roads there is hidden treasure! Let’s go straight there and dig it up straight away!” / The fairy says, “Hang on, I’ll come too, just give me a minute to get out of the jacuzzi.”

真.equation: straight 直 (451) + eight 八 (20) = truly 真
真.mnemonic: The octopus pickpocket has been caught red-handed and is being handcuffed by the police: picture them needing four sets of handcuffs … 'I’ll go straight from now on,' the octopus says, 'truly I will.' / DIY pronunciation

毛.mnemonic: The fairy spins wool on her spinning wheel, to make a pulllover for her pet mouse.

笔.equation: bamboo (321a) + wool 毛 (453) = pen 笔
笔.mnemonic: A length of bamboo with wool tied around it (to give a good grip) makes a colorful pen. / Teddy cooks up beetroot to make ink for the pen.

弋.equation: pile of earth (455a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = stake 弋
弋.mnemonic: The builders are marking out a building site, using a drop of red paint on a small pile of earth to indicate where each stake should go.

代.equation: person 亻 (13a) + stake 弋 (456a) = substitute 代
代.mnemonic: Harry is playing football and hurts himself by running into a large stake, so they have to bring on a substitute for him. / The dwarf team manager rolls dice to determine who should replace him.

舌.equation: thousand 千 (89) + mouth 口 (5) = tongue 舌
舌.mnemonic: A thousand believers flocked to the mouth of the cave, hoping to hear the famous magical tongue speak.

刮.equation: tongue 舌 (457a) + knife 刂 (74a) = blow 刮
刮.mnemonic: The young chef runs his tongue along the knife to lick it clean and then blows on it to dry it. / The ghostly giant, guardian of kitchen cleanliness, excuses this as he loves the taste of garlic too!

话.equation: words 讠 (67c) + tongue 舌 (457a) = speech 话
话.mnemonic: (In an ancient tomb) The explorer can read the ancient words written above the door, but has to get his tongue round them, as the door will only open if he recites the whole speech in one go. / The ghostly dwarf guarding the tomb plays the harmonica to put him off.

活.equation: water 氵 (78a) + tongue 舌 (457a) = alive 活
活.mnemonic: (In the desert you find someone lying on the sand) You put your last drops of water on their tongue to revive them, hoping they’re still alive. / The ghostly fairy who guards desert travelers suddenly notices what’s going on, and flies up with a magic hosepipe (to refill your water bottle).

术.equation: tree 木 (10a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = craft 术
术.mnemonic: She saw a drop of rain on the tree, and it gave her the idea of making drop-like Christmas-tree decorations to sell at the craft fair. / They draw grudging admiration from the dwarf selling shoes at the next stall.

支.equation: ten 十 (4) + right hand 又 (52) = branch 支
支.mnemonic: At the cross roads in the maze the two friends shake hands and each chooses a branch of the maze to head down. / The first one to reach the center of the maze where the giant sits in his jacuzzi, is the winner.

技.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + branch 支 (462) = skill 技
技.mnemonic: The expert can tell, simply by putting his hand on each branch, what each type of wood can be used for — a rare skill. / The dwarf then tells the workers which jeep to load it on to.

寺.equation: earth 土 (54a) + inch 寸 (104a) = temple 寺
寺.mnemonic: The pilgrims kneel on the earth and move forward inch by inch towards the holy temple. / [No pronunciation needed]

等.equation: bamboo (321a) + temple 寺 (464a) = wait for 等
等.mnemonic: He takes the bamboo to the temple but has to wait for the chief monk to come and grade it, etc. / Teddy is bored, and plays in the dung. (Yuk!)

持.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + temple 寺 (464a) = maintain 持
持.mnemonic: We need a hand over at the Indian temple— we need to do some work to maintain it. / The fairy conjures up chapatis for all those who come to help.

特.equation: cow 牛 (384) + temple 寺 (464a) = special 特
特.mnemonic: The cow who lived in the temple was special (a sacred cow!) / The dwarf's job is to bring in special turf for it each day. ('It's just a cow!' he grumbles).

义.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + shears 乂 (25a) = just 义
义.mnemonic: When one worker asked for a drop of oil on his shears, they all wanted one, claiming that that would be only just. / The dwarf supervisor reluctantly agreed, saying, 'These days, workers have it too easy.'

风.equation: several 几 (7) + shears 乂 (25a) = wind 风
风.mnemonic: Several pairs of shears have been stuck in the ground to start making a wind break. / The giant brings some fungus to fill in the gaps between them.

数.equation: trouble  (469a) + tap 攵 (190a) = number; count 数
数.mnemonic: The actor is in trouble, and taps on the rock face in the cave, furiously trying to get the secret door to work — it is the film set of an 'Indiana Jones' movie. But it keeps jamming and the number of takes is rising. / The dwarf is standing behind the door, ready to shoot the hero as he comes through (and is getting fed up at the delay). Teddy jumps up and down and urges him to shoot anyway. He gets so excited that he forgets to count the number of takes.

楼.equation: tree 木 (10a) + trouble (469a) = story 楼
楼.mnemonic: The tree is in trouble— it has been spotted knocking bricks off the building next door as the workmen are trying to build the latest story (which would overshadow the tree). / The fairy negotiates with the building’s owners to use a picture of the tree as their new logo (so then the tree is happy).

层.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + cloud 云 (56) = tier 层
层.mnemonic: The corpse rose up on a cloud, carrying it up past each tier of the building in turn. / The fairy flew alongside announcing to the people watching at each level: “This is what happens when you are stung by a killer bee.”

室.equation: house 宀 (91a) + until 至 (74b) = a room 室
室.mnemonic: (Searching for a place to stay the night) He went from house to house until he found a room he liked. / The dwarf proudly showed off the amenities: "It has a heater, a cooker, a sink — the whole shebang."

屋.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + until 至 (74b) = accommodation 屋
屋.mnemonic: We'll have to find somewhere to keep the corpse until the funeral — and we need to find accommodation for all the mourners before we can set a date. / The giant, who has been fatally wounded, had been very popular...

握.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + accommodation 屋 (473) = grasp 握
握.mnemonic: In one hand he clutched the accommodation guide, while the other grasped the reins of his horse. / "Whoa!" shouted the dwarf, as his horse trotted straight past all the good hotels.

提.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + is 是 (30) = carry 提
提.mnemonic: A hand is designed to carry things. / The fairy (giving etiquette lessons) demonstrates by carrying a tea cup.

让.equation: words 讠 (67c) + above 上 (42) = allow 让
让.mnemonic: The number of words in her essay went above the allowed limit. / She would have to wrangle with her supervisor the dwarf, to see if it would be accepted.

页.equation: thumb tack (40a) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = page 页
页.mnemonic: The small boy tried to use a thumb tack to pin the sea shell to the page. / The dwarf (teacher) said, “I told you yesterday that wouldn’t work!”

题.equation: is 是 (30) + page 页 (477) = topic 题
题.mnemonic: Teacher to schoolchild: "I told you to write a page, this isn't a page!" "It is, it is a page," said the child, "just not a full page. It's all I can write on this topic." / The fairy said to the teacher, "You have to admit, there's not a lot you can write about teaspoons."

员.equation: mouth 口 (5) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = member 员
员.mnemonic: You have to hold a sea shell in your mouth (during the initiation ceremony) before you can become a member of the seafood club. / The fairy then gives you a commemoration yuan coin.

圆.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + member 员 (479) = circle 圆
圆.mnemonic: (At the summer seafood fair) The enclosed area “for members only” was shaped like a circle. / All members had to show their special yuan coins to the fairy to gain entry.

高.mnemonic: You have to be very tall to reach the skylight as it is so high up. / The giant would normally open it for you, but he’s laid up with gout.

搞.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + high 高 (481) = engaged in 搞
搞.mnemonic: The doctor held his hand up high, palm out, to indicate that he was engaged in something and couldn’t be interrupted. / Teddy didn’t want to wait so rolled around on the floor pretending to have gout.

台.equation: cocoon 厶 (34a) + mouth 口 (5) = platform 台
台.mnemonic: The ants each brought a cocoon to the mouth of the cave, where they stacked them up to form a platform (for their annual rock concert — they use the cave as it has good acoustics!).

治.equation: water 氵 (78a) + platform 台 (483a) = treat 治
治.mnemonic: The colored water was ready on the platform for the demonstration of how to treat diseases. / His dwarf assistant dipped a wilted geranium in the water, to demonstrate the water’s reviving powers.

抬.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + platform 台 (483a) = raise 抬
抬.mnemonic: Give me a hand with the platform to raise it up (The parts are lying assembled on the ground). / The fairy then waves her wand and a magnificent tiger appears on the platform for all to see and admire.

始.equation: women 女 (15) + platform 台 (483a) = start 始
始.mnemonic: A woman walks onto the platform to start the performance (with an operatic aria). / Teddy (in the audience and impatient to see the main attraction) throws a shallot at her.

谷.equation: eight 八 (20) + tent 人 (19b) + mouth 口 (5) = valley 谷
谷.mnemonic: The octopus climbs on top of his tent, but even here the water comes up to his mouth. It had been a silly idea to pitch his tent in the valley. (It’s a strange octopus who doesn’t like water!) / [No pronunciation needed]

容.equation: house 宀 (91a) + valley 谷 (486a) = tolerate 容
容.mnemonic: If you have a house in the valley, you have to tolerate the noise of the air force jets practicing their low level flying. / The fairy thinks it’s wrong. (She can fly quietly, so why can’t they?)

士.mnemonic: This character differs from 'earth' (Character 54a) in that here the first (upper) horizontal stroke is longer than the second (lower) one.

志.equation: scholar 士 (487a) + heart 心 (161) = intention 志
志.mnemonic: The scholar has set his heart on carrying out his latest project: 'It's my intention to record every species of orchid in the world.' / His assistant the dwarf (who thinks if he's going to be dragged around, it might as well be in the sun) says, 'Let's start in Jamaica.'

声.equation: scholar 士 (487a) + corpse 尸 (268a) = voice 声
声.mnemonic: The scholar stands over the corpse ready to give his anatomy lesson — but a voice speaks from the corpse and makes him jump! (One of the students has put a small speaker inside it — if you look at the “corpse” you will see the extra stroke that is the microphone). / [DIY pronunciation]

壮.equation: firewood 丬 (317a) + scholar 士 (487a) = sturdy 壮
壮.mnemonic: The scholar was so unworldly that when sent for firewood he came back with sturdy poles instead of kindling.

装.equation: sturdy 壮 (489a) + clothes 衣 (437) = pretend 装
装.mnemonic: The children put on sturdy boots and warm clothes and sneak into the indoor ski slope at night with ropes, so they can pretend to be climbing everest. / The ghostly giant (guarding the ski slope) jangles his keys to scare them off.

医.equation: container 匚 (491a) + arrow 矢 (490a) = heal 医
医.mnemonic: The tribal doctor has a container full of arrows (like acupuncture needles, tipped with various drugs) with which to heal his patients. 'This won't hurt,' he says. / 'Easy for you to say,' says the giant, apprehensively.

您.equation: you 你 (51) + heart 心 (161) = you 您
您.mnemonic: When you say “you” and put your heart into it, it is a much more polite way of saying “you”. / You would use this form of address to a ninja or a fairy (or indeed a fairy ninja!)

束.mnemonic: The dwarf loads bundles of little wheels into his clay-pigeon launcher to practice his shooting.

整.equation: suck (495a) + upright 正 (29) = entire 整
整.mnemonic: The fat admiral sucks in his tummy and stands upright— and has to hold this position while the entire fleet sails past. / Last in line is Teddy in his junk.

嗽.equation: mouth 口 (5) + imperial decree (496a) = cough 嗽
嗽.mnemonic: The town crier opens his mouth to proclaim the imperial decree but can only cough. / The dwarf thrusts a soda into his hand before he ruins the whole occasion.

亥.mnemonic: Yet another pig! (See Characters 109a and 263 for the others.)

该.equation: words 讠 (67c) + pig 亥 (497a) = ought 该
该.mnemonic: (In the old church, looking for the famous tomb) The floor is covered with words carved into the flagstones, and the pig snuffles about. “It ought to be here somewhere,” he says. / He asks the giant if he can borrow his guidebook.

孩.equation: child 子 (17) + pig 亥 (497a) = youngster 孩
孩.mnemonic: “That child’s riding on a pig!” “It’s OK, he’s only a youngster.” / The fairy sits on a hydrant, laughing.

咳.equation: mouth 口 (5) + pig 亥 (497a) = cough 咳
咳.mnemonic: At the mouth of the cave the pig coughs discreetly to announce his presence. / The fairy emerges from behind her curtain to see who it is.

刻.equation: pig 亥 (497a) + knife 刂 (74a) = quarter 刻
刻.mnemonic: The pig uses a knife to cut his lunch apple neatly into quarters. / The dwarf fixes up a curtain round the pigsty (so that passers-by can’t see his prissy pig).

隹.equation: person + lord with an extra stroke
隹.mnemonic: This is a picture of a bird, but looks like a composite character made up of 'person' plus 'lord with an extra stroke'. Feel free to make up a story along these lines if you like. (also, compare this character with 'to live in', Character 298).

谁.equation: words 讠 (67c) + pigeon 隹 (501a) = who? 谁
谁.mnemonic: (In the dusty tomb) The pigeon writes words in the dust with his beak, asking “Who is in the tomb?” / The ghostly fairy who guards the tomb answers, “The sheikh.”

推.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + pigeon 隹 (501a) = push 推
推.mnemonic: She had nursed the wounded pigeon back to health. She took the pigeon in her hand and gave it a push to launch it into the air. / The ghostly giant, who would look after it, attached a piece of colored tape to its leg (so he could see which one to keep an eye on).

难.equation: right hand 又 (52) + pigeon 隹 (501a) = difficult 难
难.mnemonic: The toddler tries to shake hands with the pigeon— but is finding it difficult as the pigeon doesn’t have hands! / The fairy takes the lad back to his nanny.

准.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + pigeon 隹 (501a) = accurate 准
准.mnemonic: Landing on the ice, the pigeon slides neatly to a stop (exactly in the right place); it has done this so often that it’s now very accurate. / [DIY pronunciation]

集.equation: pigeon 隹 (501a) + tree 木 (10a) = assemble 集
集.mnemonic: The pigeon flies to the tree to join the others who are already assembled ready for the performance. / The fairy, wearing a G-string, comes out to announce the first act.

处.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + fortune teller 卜 (42a) = place 处
处.mnemonic: He pursues the fortune teller from place to place (to ask more details about her predictions). / He asks the dwarf, 'Which way did she go?' But the dwarf ignores him and just sits chewing his gum.

客.equation: house 宀 (91a) + each 各 (507) = guest 客
客.mnemonic: The old duke has given a house to each of his children, providing he can stay with each of them as a guest whenever he wishes. / The dwarf brings the duke’s curtains along (so that everywhere he stays, he will feel at home).

丙.equation: one 一 (1) + inside 内 (219) = third 丙
丙.mnemonic: When the unicorn gets inside the ark he sees that he is only the third creature aboard (after Noah and his wife).

病.equation: illness 疒 (510b) + third 丙 (510a) = disease 病
病.mnemonic: After seeing the same mysterious illness for the third time, he is finally able to diagnose the disease. / "Bingo," says the dwarf doctor's assistant sarcastically, "I knew what it was all along."

杂.equation: several 九 (38) + tree 木 (10a) = mixed 杂
杂.mnemonic: The baseball has got stuck in the tree; when you climb up to get it, you find all kinds of stuff mixed together up there. / [DIY pronunciation]

怎.equation: suddenly 乍 (207a) + heart 心 (161) = how? 怎
怎.mnemonic: Suddenly his friend’s heart stopped. he would have to get it restarted. Yes, but how? / [DIY pronunciation]

庄.equation: shelter 广 (170) + earth 土 (54a) = premises 庄
庄.mnemonic: The shelter protected the earth so that work could begin on the new premises (regardless of the weather).

脏.equation: moon 月 (61) + premises 庄 (513a) = dirty 脏
脏.mnemonic: With the moon shining on the premises, you can see the shopkeepers taking their dirty washing out. / [DIY pronunciation]

总.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + mouth 口 (5) + heart 心 (161) = always 总
总.mnemonic: They hung horns above the mouth of the cave, and placed an animal’s heart underneath, in accordance with tradition. 'Now we will always have enough to eat.'

责.equation: plant  (133a) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = duty 责
责.mnemonic: The prize exhibit at the art gallery is a plant growing in a sea shell; it’s the night watchman’s duty to top up the water. / The fairy has given him a zircon-encrusted watering can to use.

绩.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + duty 责 (515) = achievement 绩
绩.mnemonic: The spider developed a way of making particularly strong thread and felt it was his duty to teach it to all the other spiders too — a great achievement. / Even so, his whole team could only just make a G-string which did not satisfy the dwarf.

负.equation: bow (51a) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = shoulder 负
负.mnemonic: The men on the beach bow down to pick up sea shells to put in sacks which they then shoulder. / The dwarf has to sample the seafood but gets food poisoning (and takes out his temper on the poor men).

才.mnemonic: The fairy has only just got brand new wheels for her bike — she’s proud of them too as they are the latest style.

团.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + only just 才 (518) = group 团
团.mnemonic: (An exhibition of precious jewels from the taj Mahal must be enclosed in a secure glass case; imagine the museum staff scurrying around trying to get everything ready) they only just get the jewels enclosed when the first group of people arrives. / The ghostly fairy (who’s going to guard the exhibit) complains that it smells of tandoori.

因.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + big 大 (47) = because 因
因.mnemonic: He’s so big, he’ll have to be enclosed. Why? Because he’ll frighten everyone. / Poor giant, that will upset his yin.

恩.equation: because 因 (520) + heart 心 (161) = favor 恩
恩.mnemonic: Because she has such a kind heart she is always doing favors for people.

嗯.equation: mouth 口 (5) + favor 恩 (521a) = eh 嗯
嗯.mnemonic: You open your mouth to ask your friend a favor but, before you can ask, they say, “So what do you want me to do now? Eh?” / [DIY pronunciation]

困.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + tree 木 (10a) = difficulty 困
困.mnemonic: The tree doesn’t want to be enclosed so keeps reaching down with its branches and pulling up the fence. The workers have difficulty erecting the fence with this going on! / The dwarf foreman is cunning and brings along his pet canary to sing and calm the tree down.

水.mnemonic: The water wheel has stopped and the ghostly teddy, who looks after it, is shaking his head — he can’t fix it because he’s dropped his shades in the mechanism (and can’t see very well without them).

求.mnemonic: (In a wheel shop) A request comes in for a new wheel. The owner chooses two fairies wearing sparkling chokers to deliver it right away to his best client.

球.equation: king 王 (60a) + request 求 (524) = ball 球
球.mnemonic: The king is playing football, but not very well. The king has to keep requesting the queen to return his ball from her private garden. / She asks her two fairies, wearing sparkling chokers, to deliver it back each time.

忽.equation: don’t 勿 (526a) + heart 心 (161) = sudden 忽
忽.mnemonic: The surgeon in the operating theatre shouts, “Don’t touch the heart!” Suddenly blood spurts everywhere. / The giant comes by with his hoover to clean everything up!

物.equation: cow 牛 (384) + don’t 勿 (526a) = thing 物
物.mnemonic: The cow says, “Don’t touch me — I washed my hair this morning and I can’t do a thing with it!” / The dwarf whooped with derision.

易.equation: sun 日 (6) + don’t 勿 (526a) = easy 易
易.mnemonic: Don’t lie in the sun for too long — it’s easy to get sun burnt. / The dwarf decides to put up this warning on an easel (as he’s fed up of telling everyone).

踢.equation: foot 足 (280) + easy 易 (528) = kick 踢
踢.mnemonic: The soccer player draws his foot back; it is going to be an easy shot, and he kicks the ball hard into the goal. / The giant cheers the winning team (almost deafening everyone around him).

场.equation: earth 土 (54a) + sunny (530a) = site 场
场.mnemonic: They look for a patch of earth that is sunny, for the site of their new plantation.

汤.equation: water 氵 (78a) + sunny (530a) = soup 汤
汤.mnemonic: Put the water in a sunny spot (to warm it) before making the soup. / When it’s ready make sure to fill the giant’s tankard first.

扬.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + sunny (530a) = make known 扬
扬.mnemonic: The signaller holds his hand aloft where it is sunny (so that his mirror catches the sun) to make known that the invaders are coming. / At the next relay station it’s cloudy, so the fairy has to fly onwards with the message, “The Yanks are coming!”

奶.equation: woman 女 (15) + sigh 乃 (533a) = milk 奶
奶.mnemonic: The woman gives a sigh of relief as the baby starts to drink the milk (and so stops crying). / Teddy bounds in with a nightlight so the baby can sleep (but trips up, much to the amusement of the baby).

极.equation: tree 木 (10a) + reach 及 (534a) = extremity 极
极.mnemonic: The tree reaches out — with the tips of its branches it can just reach the extremity of the neighboring tree. / This is where the fairy has hung her G-strings to dry.

级.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + reach 及 (534a) = grade 级
级.mnemonic: If the thread (from the silkworm) can reach across the loom without breaking, it is high grade silk. / They can use the leftovers to make the fairy a new G-string.

原.equation: cliff 厂 (149) + spring (536a) = original 原
原.mnemonic: The whisky distillery was sited by the spring under the cliff— the water there gave the whisky its original flavor. / Each bottle carried the trademark picture on the label, of a fairy sitting on a yuan coin.

愿.equation: original 原 (536) + heart 心 (161) = desire 愿
愿.mnemonic: He realized that the original girl he had first lost his heart to was the one he now truly desired. / The dwarf wondered sadly if she still had the lucky yuan coin he had once given her.

吉.equation: scholar 士 (487a) + mouth 口 (5) = auspicious 吉
吉.mnemonic: When the scholar (who is normally engrossed in his books in his cave) appears at the mouth of his cave, blinking in the sunlight, the villagers take it as an auspicious sign.

结.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + auspicious 吉 (538a) = tie up 结
结.mnemonic: They used the invisible thread, produced by the silkworms on the most auspicious days, to tie up their hair into ornate patterns. / Two fairies work together to put gel on any stray hairs to stick them down.

喜.equation: auspicious 吉 (538a) + feet  (90a) + mouth 口 (5) = celebration 喜
喜.mnemonic: It’s auspicious if the first feet to enter the mouth of your cave at the start of a year are animal feet — when this happens it is a cause for celebration. / Teddy brings a sheep early in the morning to ensure this happens.

周.equation: outer limits 冂 (160a) + foul mouth (540a) = week 周
周.mnemonic: You will be sent to the outer limits if you are foul mouthed and you'll have to stay there for a week. / Even the giant was sent there for telling dirty jokes.

调.equation: words 讠 (67c) + week 周 (540) = tune 调
调.mnemonic: The lyricist for a songwriting team lives on an island where he can concentrate. When the week’s quota of words is finished, they are ready to have a tune written for them. / The two dwarves collect them each week in their dhow (and take them to his partner, the composer, on the mainland).

尚.equation: small 小 (50) + skylight  (389a) = esteem 尚
尚.mnemonic: The double glazing salesman claims, “If you install a small skylight, you will be held in great esteem by your neighbors.”

躺.equation: body 身 (107) + esteem 尚 (542a) = lie down 躺
躺.mnemonic: If you hold your body in high esteem, you will lie down for a power nap in the middle of the day. / Teddy tries this, but bounces about so much that he gets tangled up in the covers.

堂.equation: esteem 尚 (542a) + earth 土 (54a) = hall 堂
堂.mnemonic: The site of the famous battle that saved the town was an esteemed piece of earth, so that’s where they built the town hall. / The fairy conjured up a shiny replica tank to sit outside the hall as a memorial.

常.equation: esteem 尚 (542a) + towel 巾 (427a) = often 常
常.mnemonic: If you esteem the towel the king gave you, you will use it often. / DIY pronunciation

掌.equation: esteem 尚 (542a) + hand 手 (31) = palm 掌
掌.mnemonic: (Briefing to the recipients at the military medal ceremony) “If the esteemed lady offers you her hand, take care to notice if it is palm up (shake it) or palm down (kiss it). / Teddy, holding the medals ready, will jangle them if you look like getting it wrong.

条.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + tree 木 (10a) = slip of paper 条
条.mnemonic: The squirrels pursue one another through the branches of the tree until one slips on a slip of paper and falls. / Two fairies stretch a towel between them to catch him.

务.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + power 力 (14) = work at 务
务.mnemonic: He pursued power by climbing the corporate ladder, working at his desk into the night. / His rival, the dwarf, chose instead to woo the CEO’s daughter.

备.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + field 田 (156a) = prepare 备
备.mnemonic: He pursued the pig round the field whilst his wife prepared the other ingredients for the meal. / They had promised the dwarf bacon for dinner.

鱼.equation: bow (51a) + field 田 (156a) + one 一 (1) = fish 鱼
鱼.mnemonic: The small boy bows to the unicorn in the field, and proffers the dead fish (but the unicorn is unable to revive it). / The fairy holds a burial ceremony and reads a eulogy in the pouring rain.

复.equation: clouds (124b) + sun 日 (6) + pursue 夂 (506a) = repeat 复
复.mnemonic: (At the fighter pilot training school) The pilots have to fly into the clouds, then out again into the sun, then pursue the instructor’s aircraft; and they must repeat all this until they get it right every time. / The dwarf (in charge of the kitchen) grumbles because the food is getting cold.

壬.equation: beret (89a) + scholar 士 (487a) = ninth 壬
