壬.mnemonic: Ceremonial berets are given out each year to the top ten scholars in the country. Yours has a baseball embroidered on it to show that you came ninth. / [No pronunciation needed]

任.equation: person 亻 (13a) + ninth 壬 (551a) = whatever 任
任.mnemonic: Harry is at bat in the ninth innings of the baseball match. The coach says, “Do whatever it takes!” / Harry hits the ball and the dwarf calls out, “For goodness’ sake, run!”

计.equation: words 讠 (67c) + ten 十 (4) = calculate 计
计.mnemonic: The exam paper was littered with words with red crosses through them. The examiner used these to calculate the final grade. / The dwarf posted up the GPA scores on the notice board.

设.equation: words 讠 (67c) + strike 受 (169a) = establish 设
设.mnemonic: (Laying the foundation stone at a hospice) He had to strike very hard to etch the words into the stone which would mark where the hospice movement was first established. / The dwarf, wearing a bright yellow shirt, was keeping onlookers at a safe distance.

划.equation: dagger 戈 (32a) + knife 刂 (74a) = plan 划
划.mnemonic: The actors, one with a dagger and one with a kitchen knife, carefully plan the fight which will be the climax of their play. / The ghostly dwarf grumpily hands out protective clothing so that he won’t get blamed if they come to any harm.

或.equation: mouth 口 (5) + one 一 (1) + dagger 戈 (32a) = or 或
或.mnemonic: The dentist uses a small dagger to probe the teeth in the unicorn’s mouth, asking “Which one hurts, this one — or this one? — or this one?” / The ghostly dwarf, who protects the unicorn, impatiently points out the tooth with the hole in it.

咸.equation: fifth 戊 (259a) + piled up  (150b) = salty 咸
咸.mnemonic: With his fifth arm the starfish piled up the food he had caught to save for a salty snack later. / [No pronunciation needed]

感.equation: salty 咸 (556a) + heart 心 (161) = feel 感
感.mnemonic: "All that salty food was bad for your heart," the doctor said. "But now you've been on that diet, how do you feel?" / Teddy had dressed up as Gandhi (to symbolize frugality and remind the patient that he should eat less salt).

喊.equation: mouth 口 (5) + salty 咸 (556a) = shout 喊
喊.mnemonic: Stranded by the high tide at the mouth of the cave and surrounded by salty water, he shouts for help. / Teddy clambers down the cliff with some drinking water in his handbag!

布.equation: left hand (53a) + towel 巾 (427a) = cloth 布
布.mnemonic: (At the jeweler’s) She tries the ring on her left hand and then places it on the towel on the counter, and the jeweler cleans it with a cloth. / The dwarf rubs his hands at this boost in his sales.

希.equation: shears 乂 (25a) + cloth 布 (558) = wish 希
希.mnemonic: The sheepshearer rubs the magic shears with a cloth and makes a wish. / And (lo and behold) a giant appears with a sheep for him to practice on!

卩.mnemonic: It refers to a seal for authenticating documents, which could be carried around and served as a badge of office.

节.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + seal 卩 (560a) = festival 节
节.mnemonic: In this character the first stroke of “seal” (560a) is lengthened and the vertical stroke starts part-way along it. The character also means “a node or segment”, “an agenda” or “to economize”. Once a year the king allows his seal to be hidden in a huge pile of grass and the children have to find it — the resulting scrum is the highlight of the festival. / Two fairies prepare a huge jelly for the feast afterwards (picture them having to keep plucking stray pieces of grass out of the jelly).

却.equation: go 去 (86) + seal 卩 (560a) = despite 却
却.mnemonic: “I told you to go and get me my favorite ornamental seal,” the king said grumpily, “But despite this you’ve come back with the wrong one.”

脚.equation: moon ᄌ (61) + despite 却 (561a) = foot 脚
脚.mnemonic: There was a bright moon, but despite this he kept stubbing his foot on the tree roots in the forest. / At this rate he would be too late to see the two teddies jousting at the secret gathering in the forest.

报.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + ruler (562a) = report 报
报.mnemonic: The ruler holds out his hand for the report (on the state of the kingdom). / The dwarf bows as he gives it to him.

服.equation: moon 月 (61) + ruler (562a) = obey 服
服.mnemonic: At the first full moon of his reign, the new ruler must pledge to obey the traditions of his office. / The fairy presides over the ceremony sitting on a futon.

痛.equation: illness 疒 (510b) + explosion 甬 (564a) = ache 痛
痛.mnemonic: Doctor, the illness I had before the explosion is gone but now I ache all over. / My dwarf assistant will use special tongs to remove the shrapnel, and then you’ll be fine, replied the doctor.

厄.equation: cliff 厂 (149) + hardship (566a) = uphill 厄
厄.mnemonic: There are lots of hardships involved in living on a cliff, but it’s ideal for someone who likes an uphill struggle! / [No pronunciation needed]

危.equation: bow (51a) + uphill 厄 (566b) = danger 危
危.mnemonic: If you bow to someone you meet as you're going uphill, you run the danger that they will bow too (and will then fall over on top of you). / I once had the full weight of the giant on top of me, because of bowing to him like that.

顾.equation: uphill 厄 (566b) + page 页 (477) = attend to 顾
顾.mnemonic: (A reclusive author lives on a hill) the publisher struggles uphill with the pages of the author’s book, so the author can attend to the corrections. / But the dwarf doesn’t mind as he is a budding author himself and regards the recluse as his guru.

亡.equation: lid 亠 (25b) + conceal (568a) = perish 亡
亡.mnemonic: The mother puts the lid on the box of strawberries to conceal them from her children — but then forgets about them so they perish. (the strawberries, not the children!) / [No pronunciation needed]

忙.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + perish 亡 (568b) = busy 忙
忙.mnemonic: Your heart will perish if you rush around being busy all the time. / The fairy can (magically) see that your arteries are all getting mangled up in there …

忘.equation: perish 亡 (568b) + heart 心 (161) = forget 忘
忘.mnemonic: (The king dictates a letter to his mistress) "Perish the thought that my heart will ever forget you." / His retainer, Wang the dwarf, disapproves as he writes all this down.

望.equation: wane (570a) + king 王 (60a) = gaze 望
望.mnemonic: The old king’s health was on the wane— all he could do now was to gaze out at his kingdom from his balcony. / Sadly he called for his chancellor Wang the dwarf, to put his affairs in order.

雨.mnemonic: It’s handy that the character for “rain” needs a “pouring rain” story! When this appears at the top of other characters, “outer limits” gets compressed into “cover”. remember that the same thing happened for “esteem” (542a). / Picture a wheel left out in the rain: “That Teddy’s useless— he’s always leaving things out in the pouring rain.”

雪.equation: rain 雨 (571) + snout (267a) = snow 雪
雪.mnemonic: The pig slept, dreaming of rain falling on his snout— but when he woke up it was snow (and everything around was white). / [DIY pronunciation]

参.equation: cocoon 厶 (34a) + big 大 (47) + hairs 彡 (573a) = consult 参
参.mnemonic: The silkworm farmer finds that his cocoons are growing big hairs, so takes them along to consult the vet. / The giant (working as the vet’s assistant) jumps onto a chair saying, “I can’t stand creepy crawlies!”

景.equation: sun 日 (6) + capital 京 (239b) = view 景
景.mnemonic: They could see the sun glinting on the capital in the distance — they knew that this was the right house for them as soon as they saw this view from the front porch.

影.equation: view 景 (574a) + hairs 彡 (573a) = shadow 影
影.mnemonic: He had taken photos of all the views, but hairs in the camera had made shadows on all his prints. / DIY pronunciation

匃.equation: wrap 勹 (23a) + person 人 (12) + conceal (568a) = beggar 匃
匃.mnemonic: They conceal Harry by wrapping him up in an old cloak, to look like a beggar.

曷.equation: sun 日 (6) + beggar 匃 (575a) = a drink 曷
曷.mnemonic: As the sun starts to get higher the beggar is getting desperate for a drink.

喝.equation: mouth 口 (5) + a drink 曷 (575b) = to drink 喝
喝.mnemonic: They leave a drink (imagine a glass of beer) by the mouth of the cave to see if anyone will come out to drink it. / They’re hoping to catch sight of the giant who lives there as a hermit.

渴.equation: water 氵 (78a) + a drink 曷 (575b) = thirsty 渴
渴.mnemonic: Watering down the drinks in the pub only works if the customers are thirsty (because then they won’t notice). / Teddy does this behind a curtain (taking a sip from each glass and topping it up with water).

继.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + cut (577a) = continue 继
继.mnemonic: (In the clothing factory) When the worker reaches the end of each reel of thread he has to cut it off and continue straight away with a new reel. / The dwarf supervisor has to check each G-string which has such a join in it.

世.mnemonic: After the round the world cycle ride, only one wheel had lasted the whole trip and this 'world wheel' is now on display in the town hall, together with a photo of the dwarf with the 'golden shallot' being presented to him by the French president.

介.equation: tent 人 (19b) + grain stalks (579a) = between 介
介.mnemonic: The farmer put a tent over the grain stalks, where the harvest mice had built their nest — so that the harvesters would have to go between the tents. / The two dwarf farmhands were jealous of this treatment (when did the farmer ever show so much consideration for them?)

界.equation: field 田 (156a) + between 介 (579) = boundary 界
界.mnemonic: There is a field between the two farms, forming a no-man’s land so that the boundary is disputed. / The two dwarves (who each own one of the farms) each eye the field jealously.

亦.mnemonic: When this appears at the top of other characters, the central two lines are truncated into short vertical lines (as in the following character).

变.equation: as well 亦 (581a) + right hand 又 (52) = transform 变
变.mnemonic: (At the wizard contest) Before starting, they had to touch wands as well as shake hands. But because they had left their wands activated they transformed one another into slugs. / The two dwarf stage-hands carried them off on a bench (to the detransforming clinic).

夭.equation: beret (89a) + big 大 (47) = die young 夭
夭.mnemonic: The child had loved wearing a beret which was too big for him (he had wanted to grow up to be a commando), but tragically he died young. (Picture the beret on the poor lad’s coffin at the funeral).

笑.equation: bamboo 竹 (321a) + die young 夭 (582a) = smile 笑
笑.mnemonic: The prince has married the princess from the neighboring kingdom and their first child is born. It is the custom to place a piece of bamboo in the baby’s hand. If the baby ignores the bamboo it will die young, but the baby grasps it — and the parents smile. / The two dwarf town criers have to trudge round the two kingdoms shouting out the good news.

乔.equation: die young 夭 (582a) + grain stalks  (579a) = tall 乔
乔.mnemonic: The boy died young, while the grain stalks were taller than he was. / [No pronunciation needed]

桥.equation: tree 木 (10a) + tall 乔 (583a) = bridge 桥
桥.mnemonic: They looked for a tree that was tall enough to make a bridge across the river. / The two fairies on the other side own a restaurant famed for its clam chowder.

歹.equation: one 一 (1) + evening 夕 (69a) = evil 歹
歹.mnemonic: Each evening the unicorn comes out of the forest to sleep, because he’s scared that there are evil spirits in there at night. / [No pronunciation needed]

列.equation: evil 歹 (584a) + knife 刂 (74a) = line up 列
列.mnemonic: The evil looking knife was confiscated by the police and lined up (with the others they have seized) for the TV cameras.

例.equation: person 亻 (13a) + line up 列 (584b) = example 例
例.mnemonic: Harry has to line up in the identity parade — they need examples of people similar to the suspect. / The dwarf (policeman) arrives with the suspect on a leash!

死.equation: evil 歹 (584a) + spoon 匕 (206a) = die 死
死.mnemonic: "If you eat from this evil spoon you will die," said the priest. / Teddy said, "I'll stick to my salami then — you don't need a spoon for that."

解.equation: dime 角 (418) + gimlet (586a) = untie 解
解.mnemonic: The fisherman tells the workmen, “I’ll give you a dime if you’ll lend me your gimlet so that I can untie this knot.” / The two teddies sit on the jetty to help (one on either side of the fisherman — they’re the ones who got the nets all knotted in the first place of course).

急.equation: hay (587a) + heart 心 (161) = urgent 急
急.mnemonic: Hay is spilling out of a hole in the scarecrow’s coat, and soon his heart will be exposed — stopping the leak has become urgent. / The fairy uses her G-string to secure it!

彦.equation: produce 产 (197) + hairs 彡 (573a) = a good man 彦
彦.mnemonic: He decides to produce hairs in his factory (to make wigs for bald people) — but he charges nothing as he is a good man.

颜.equation: a good man 彦 (588a) + page 页 (477) = complexion 颜
颜.mnemonic: The good man spends all his time indoors studying the pages of his good books — so ends up with a pale complexion. / The fairy casts a spell on him and he suddenly says, “You know, I have a yen to go outside more.”

许.equation: words 讠 (67c) + noon 午 (125) = permit 许
许.mnemonic: The words on the sign in the street say that from noon parking is permitted. / A parking attendant appears through the pouring rain and Teddy says 'Shoo!' in an attempt to chase him off.

需.equation: rain 雨 (571) + and yet 而 (151) = need 需
需.mnemonic: In the heavy rain, the market trader fixes up a makeshift roof for his stall, and yet it sags (and collapses with the weight of water) — a proper gutter is what he needs. / The giant fixes up a chute to divert the pouring rain for him.

须.equation: hairs 彡 (573a) + page 页 (477) = have to 须
须.mnemonic: He’s stuck hairs on the page to underline certain words (this is in the days before highlighter pens have been invented) but the librarian tells him to remove them. “Do I have to?” he wails (wondering how to remove the glue). / The giant whispers to him, “When my shoelaces got glue on them I just left them to soak in the pouring rain.”

续.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + sell 卖 (248) = carry on 续
续.mnemonic: The spiders have found that if they produce extra thread they can sell it — so each day, after making their webs, they carry on and produce more thread, which can be sold. / The dwarf uses it to make waterproof shoelaces, which he tests out in the pouring rain.

令.equation: combine (59a) + seal 卩 (560a) = command 令
令.mnemonic: The emperor simply signs ordinary documents, but when he combines a seal with his signature it means that it’s a command.

冷.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + command 令 (593a) = cold 冷
冷.mnemonic: The ice queen commands it to be cold. / Teddy complains, “My lungs tickle with the cold air.”

零.equation: rain 雨 (571) + command 令 (593a) = zero 零
零.mnemonic: (The trainee wizard is taking his final exams) he tries to command that rain should fall, but instead the temperature drops to zero. / The fairy whispers, “For rain, you should have used linguini in your spell.”

领.equation: command 令 (593a) + page 页 (477) = to lead 领
领.mnemonic: The herald unrolls the page and reads out the king’s command— it says that everyone must lead their pets around the town on National Pet Day. / Teddy ties a bit of linguini to his hamster and brings it along.

召.equation: knife 刀 (72) + mouth 口 (5) = summon 召
召.mnemonic: The sentry looks over the castle walls — and sees a pirate climbing up towards him, a knife in his mouth. he raises the alarm, crying, “Summon the guards!” / [No pronunciation needed]

绍.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + summon 召 (596a) = continue 绍
绍.mnemonic: The queen is doing her needlework. She has run out of thread, so she summons a servant to fetch more so that she can continue. / The dwarf shouts after him to be quick about it.

昭.equation: sun 日 (6) + summon 召 (596a) = obvious 昭
昭.mnemonic: On the next day when the sun shone brightly, he summoned all his friends to admire his new sundial. One complained, “Why do this on such a hot day?” “Well, isn’t it obvious?”

照.equation: obvious 昭 (597a) + fire 灬 (185a) = shine 照
照.mnemonic: The obvious way to get the fire started is to shine focused sunlight onto it. / The dwarf gets his old jousting shield (and uses the concave side of it as a magnifying mirror).

查.equation: tree 木 (10a) + dawn 旦 (117a) = check up 查
查.mnemonic: (After a storm) he went round the trees at dawn to check up which ones had survived the night. / The fairy flew alongside with a chart showing where the trees were supposed to be.

检.equation: tree 木 (10a) + conference (599a) = inspect 检
检.mnemonic: When a tree arrives at the conference, it must be inspected for contagious diseases. / If it is genuinely healthy, two teddies will escort it to the conference hall.

脸.equation: moon 月 (61) + conference (599a) = face 脸
脸.mnemonic: (At a conference of werewolves) When the full moon comes up the conference delegates have to have their faces checked to see if they really are werewolves. / Two teddies stand on either side of the queue looking out for their friends so they can lend them hairy masks to get past the checks.

险.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + conference (599a) = risky 险
险.mnemonic: The commandos choose a mound to hold their conference (because they like to occupy the high ground), and plan how they are going to carry out the final part of their risky mission. / [DIY pronunciation]

验.equation: horse 马 (44) + conference (599a) = examine 验
验.mnemonic: A horse with a rare disease has been brought along to the conference of vets so that they can examine him. / The dwarf caretaker watches closely as he has always had a yen to be a vet.

式.equation: work 工 (147) + stake 弋 (456a) = style 式
式.mnemonic: He always carries a stake to work, as he thinks it gives him a certain style. (But his teenage son knows better!) / [No pronunciation needed]

试.equation: words 讠 (67c) + style 式 (603a) = try 试
试.mnemonic: (Designing a poster for the production of “1001 Nights”) The draftsman writes the words out in different styles, to try and see which will work best. / The dwarf waits until he’s finished, and only then points out that he’s spelled “Scheherazade” wrong. (You can imagine the ensuing expletives!)

考.equation: old man  (261a) + choke 丂 (424a) = test 考
考.mnemonic: The old man arrives at the hospital choking— but it is just a pretense, to test out the hospital’s response time. / Teddy, on reception, rings a cowbell to summon the doctors who come running.

斗.equation: ice crystals (246a) + ten 十 (4) = fight 斗
斗.mnemonic: The 'cross' is rather distorted here to make room for the 'ice crystals' — note that the 'horizontal' line has a definite slope to it. / Two gangs of boys start throwing ice crystals at each other from either side of the cross roads — and it soon develops into a full-blown fight.

科.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + fight 斗 (605a) = classification 科
科.mnemonic: The farmers are having a grain fight (throwing bags of grain at each other) after the announcement of the grain classification results. / The giant breaks up the fight and imposes a curfew until morning.

研.equation: stone 石 (115a) + open 开 (85) = research 研
研.mnemonic: You are issued with a special stone (with an embedded microchip) to open the door to the secret research lab. (Boffins are always losing normal keys!) / But the fairy has always had a yen to make the lab’s work public, so lets a reporter in to look around.

糸.equation: smallest 幺 (607a) + small 小 (50) = thread 糸
糸.mnemonic: The smallest of the small worms looks just like a piece of thread.

系.equation: beret (89a) + thread 糸 (607b) = cluster 系
系.mnemonic: A spider has woven a web out of threads in the beret, and there is now a cluster of eggs there. / The dwarf wraps it up in a sheet and throws it away.

累.equation: field 田 (156a) + thread 糸 (607b) = exhausted 累
累.mnemonic: The farmer laid a network of steel threads under the field to protect his crops from rabbits — and it worked, as the rabbits rapidly became exhausted trying to dig through them. / The dwarf takes them off and dumps them in the lake.

践.equation: foot 足 (280) + tiny 戋 (609a) = carry out 践
践.mnemonic: (A modern day Cinderella) the old hag slips her tiny foot into the slipper, and it fits! Now the prince has to carry out his promise (and marry her). / The two dwarves (who have been carrying the slipper and cushion around) mutter that she can’t possibly be the genuine article.

钱.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + tiny 戋 (609a) = money 钱
钱.mnemonic: Tiny pieces of gold (found in the river beds) were the first money. / [DIY pronunciation]

浅.equation: water 氵 (78a) + tiny 戋 (609a) = shallow 浅
浅.mnemonic: There was only a tiny amount of water around so all the ponds were shallow. / [DIY pronunciation]

礼.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + hook (612a) = gift 礼
礼.mnemonic: He sees a sign in the sky in the shape of a hook. “aha, the perfect gift for my fisherman friend,” he thinks. / Teddy tries his luck fishing, but only catches a leek!

乱.equation: tongue 舌 (457a) + hook (612a) = chaotic 乱
乱.mnemonic: (At night on a small fishing boat) Someone’s tongue gets a fish hook stuck in it, and chaotic scenes ensue as he thrashes around. / The ghostly dwarf (guardian mascot of the boat) holds a lantern while they extract the hook.

育.equation: new-born baby  (614a) + moon 月 (61) = nurture 育
育.mnemonic: She is over the moon about her newborn baby, and vows to nurture it. / The dwarf grumbles as he has to come out in the pouring rain to read a eulogy.

穴.equation: house 宀 (91a) + eight 八 (20) = hole 穴
穴.mnemonic: The house where the octopus lives is so small that he had to make holes in the walls for his tentacles to poke through.

究.equation: hole 穴 (616a) + nine 九 (38) = investigate 究
究.mnemonic: In one of the holes on the golf course they found a baseball, and decided to investigate how it had got there. / It turned out that two giants had been joking around (playing mini-golf and using the baseball as a golf ball).

空.equation: hole 穴 (616a) + work 工 (147) = empty 空
空.mnemonic: Digging the hole (for the elephant trap) had been a lot of work— but day after day it remained empty. / One day the giant sprained his ankle in it, and was so cross that he filled it in with concrete.

突.equation: hole 穴 (616a) + dog 犬 (238a) = prominent 突
突.mnemonic: The hole which the dog had dug was in a prominent position (right in the middle of the front lawn). / The giant (head gardener) used his toupee to disguise the hole until he could repair it.

深.equation: water 氵 (78a) + hearth (619a) = deep 深
深.mnemonic: During the flood, water rapidly reached the hearth and put the fire out — it was getting really deep now. / This would mean that the giant handyman would shun the village (until the water receded — he doesn’t like getting his feet wet).

抽.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + because 由 (620a) = extract 抽
抽.mnemonic: Hold out your hand because it’s the only way to extract the thorn. / The giant holds out his hand grudgingly, and chokes back tears!

邮.equation: because 由 (620a) + city 阝 (96a) = mail 邮
邮.mnemonic: Because it’s a city there are mail boxes everywhere. / The fairy explains all this to the yokels from the country.

申.mnemonic: Compare this with 'electricity' (Character 159), which has a curly 'tail'.

神.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + state 申 (622a) = gods 神
神.mnemonic: The sign in the sky states that the gods are displeased. / The fairy has begun to shun the villagers (so the gods have to communicate directly!)

寅.equation: house 宀 (91a) + polite (623a) + eight 八 (20) = tiger 寅
寅.mnemonic: He ran flat out towards the octopus’ house, trying to think how he could call out “Open the door!” politely so that he would be let in straight away, before the tiger caught up with him.

演.equation: water 氵 (78a) + tiger 寅 (623b) = perform 演
演.mnemonic: At the finale of the circus act the tiger dives into the water, drenching the audience. He performs this at every show and it’s the big attraction. / Teddy has a yen to do it too.

黄.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + polite (623a) + eight 八 (20) = yellow 黄
黄.mnemonic: If you take your grass and are polite to the octopus, he will sit on it for you until it goes yellow. / The ghostly fairy will then guard it in the aircraft hangar (until it is ready to sell on the black market — it’s that sort of grass!)

共.equation: bacon rashers (625a) + eight 八 (20) = collectively 共
共.mnemonic: One octopus was cooking bacon rashers, another eggs, and collectively they produced a huge breakfast. / The dwarf sounded a gong when it was ready.

画.equation: one 一 (1) + field 田 (156a) + pit 凵 (626a) = drawing 画
画.mnemonic: The unicorn had fallen into the pit in the field (that they had dug as an elephant trap). The reporter (in the days before photography) took ages to make a drawing of the scene for the local paper. / The ghostly dwarf guarding the pit suggested attaching a harness to the unicorn to lift him out.

怕.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + white 白 (22) = fear 怕
怕.mnemonic: His heart has started pounding and his face has gone white— such a look of fear! What has he seen? / It’s a dwarf parking attendant bearing down on his parked car ...

拍.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + white 白 (22) = clap 拍
拍.mnemonic: The chef’s hands are all white so he claps them together (to shake off the flour). / The giant admires the pies (and wonders how many he’ll get).

哭.equation: cry out  (629a) + dog 犬 (238a) = weep 哭
哭.mnemonic: The child cried out as her dog was hit by a passing car, and sat weeping at the side of the road. / The giant, who was passing by in his cool new coupe, stopped to give them a lift to the vet.

器.equation: weep 哭 (629) + cry out (629a) = utensil 器
器.mnemonic: In the kitchen the Chef is weeping, crying out and throwing his utensils about in despair. / He’s lost the “Best Restaurant” competition, and reckons that the dwarf, his main rival, cheated in order to win.

品.equation: mouth 口 (5) + cry out (629a) = goods 品
品.mnemonic: (The cave dwellers are having a yard sale) At the mouth of each cave the owner cries out to attract customers for the goods they have for sale. / [No pronunciation needed]

操.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + chirp (631b) = exercise 操
操.mnemonic: The bird sits on the fitness instructor’s hand and chirps to set the tempo for the exercises. / The giant has joined the class because he is getting too stout.

澡.equation: water 氵 (78a) + chirp (631b) = bathe 澡
澡.mnemonic: The bird sees the water and gives a delighted chirp— it can bathe at last! / [DIY pronunciation]

早.equation: sun 日 (6) + ten 十 (4) = early 早
早.mnemonic: As the car approaches the cross roads the rising sun is directly in the driver’s eyes — if only he didn’t have to get to work so early! / [DIY pronunciation]

章.equation: stand 立 (177) + early 早 (633) = badge 章
章.mnemonic: If you stand there early you will get a badge. / You’ll know the giant is coming with the badges when you hear them jangling together.

草.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + early 早 (633) = straw 草
草.mnemonic: If you cut the grass early in the morning it will make the best straw. / Teddy decides to watch, declaring, “I’m too stout to help with cutting grass.”

包.equation: wrap 勹 (23a) + snake 巳 (275a) = parcel 包
包.mnemonic: (Before the birthday party) They wrap up the multicolored toy snake to make a long thin parcel. / The giant has to bow down low to pick it up before he sets off to deliver it.

饱.equation: food 饣 (288a) + parcel 包 (636) = replete 饱
饱.mnemonic: (At the birthday party) The food and parcels look so colorful on the table. The children eat quickly until they are all replete. / The birthday boy then opens the first parcel, and out jumps Teddy, who then gives a deep bow.

抱.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + parcel 包 (636) = embrace 抱
抱.mnemonic: The latecomer to the birthday party has the parcel in her hand when the birthday boy opens the door, and they hug in a fond embrace. / The dwarf butler tactfully stays bowing until the embrace is over.

跑.equation: foot 足 (280) + parcel 包 (636) = run 跑
跑.mnemonic: In the crowded airport he doesn’t see the parcel until his foot hits it (and there’s the sound of breaking glass) — he decides to run away before anyone sees him. / But Teddy is curious and looks inside, sneezes, and gets covered in fine white powder from the parcel.

商.equation: stand 立 (177) + porthole (640a) = trade 商
商.mnemonic: Imagine standing, looking out through a porthole as your ship comes into port, and seeing all the possibilities for trade. / But when you get ashore, the giant sitting on the quayside advises, “I’d set up in Shanghai if I were you.”

佰.equation: person 亻 (13a) + hundred 百 (40) = $100 check 佰
佰.mnemonic: This is the form of “100” used on checks, banknotes, etc. to prevent alterations. / Harry buys a pet centipede with a hundred legs and has to write out a $100 check. (At a dollar a leg, it gets quite expensive, he muses).

宿.equation: house 宀 (91a) + $100 check 佰 (641a) = stay overnight 宿
宿.mnemonic: At the manor house they write a $100 check to stay overnight. / They’ve arrived just in time for the evening meal — the dwarf head chef is just dishing out the soup.

予.equation: seal 卩 (560a) + nail 丁 (81b) = bestow 予
予.mnemonic: If the proclamation with the king’s seal is nailed to your door, this bestows special status on your house.

预.equation: bestow 予 (642a) + page 页 (477) = in advance 预
预.mnemonic: The authors of this book bestowed a sample page on their publisher in advance, to show them the design. / The dwarf sub-editor said it was useless, and threw the manuscript out the window into the pouring rain!

舍.equation: tent 人 (19b) + dry 干 (124) + mouth 口 (5) = shed 舍
舍.mnemonic: The explorer pitches his tent on the dry ground by the mouth of the cave — and only then spots a shed nearby. / A dwarf sherpa lives there (who will act as his guide — for a fee of course).

舒.equation: shed 舍 (643) + bestow 予 (642a) = spread out 舒
舒.mnemonic: The duke bestows a shed on the cobbler so that he has space to spread out his patterns on the floor. / He’s started making shoes for the giant so he needs lots of space.

兴.equation: haul up  (388a) + eight 八 (20) = excited 兴
兴.mnemonic: The fishermen haul up the octopus into their boat — they get very excited at this rare catch (which will fetch a good price). / On the shore they sell it to a dwarf fishmonger — but the octopus revives and escapes, throwing up shingle everywhere as he makes a dash for the sea.

矛.equation: bestow 予 (642a) + slide  (34b) = spear 矛
矛.mnemonic: Imagine the king playing on the children’s slide — whee! The king decides to bestow the royal warrant on the slide by scratching his initials on it with his bodyguard’s spear.

橘.equation: tree 木 (10a) + stab (646b) = tangerine 橘
橘.mnemonic: A party game: all stand around the tree, and the first one to stab a tangerine is the winner. / The fairy takes the juice outside into the pouring rain, to dilute it.

桔.equation: tree 木 (10a) + auspicious 吉 (538a) = tangerine 桔
桔.mnemonic: In the palace grounds there is a tree, from which, on a certain auspicious day, you are allowed to pick a tangerine to eat. / The fairy takes the juice outside into the pouring rain, to dilute it.

举.equation: excited 兴 (645) + criminal (130a) = raise 举
举.mnemonic: The criminal is excited as he raises his head carefully out of the prison escape tunnel he has dug. / Teddy runs up in the pouring rain, shouting, “I’ve got you some juice!” (and almost gives the game away).

句.equation: mouth 口 (5) + wrap 勹 (23a) = sentence 句
句.mnemonic: They are fed up of his grumbling, so wrap something round his mouth before he can speak another sentence. / But the dwarf is a judo expert and escapes, running out into the pouring rain.

局.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + sentence 句 (649) = office 局
局.mnemonic: The corpse has a sentence written on his hand: 'Look in the manager’s office.' / The fairy (finding a wet judo outfit) says, 'Whoever did this was a judo expert who has been out in the pouring rain.'

够.equation: sentence 句 (649) + many 多 (69) = enough 够
够.mnemonic: The politician had so many sentences in his speech that everyone had soon had enough (too much, in fact). / The dwarf (never one to suffer fools gladly) gets up and walks out, saying, “Blow this, I’m off to the go-kart track.”

付.equation: person 亻 (13a) + inch 寸 (104a) = pay 付
付.mnemonic: At the fairground ride, Harry was an inch too tall, so had to pay the adult fare.

附.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + pay 付 (652a) = near to 附
附.mnemonic: To see the burial mound properly, you have to pay to be allowed near to it. / The dwarf doesn’t allow food inside, except for the foo yong (which he himself sells).

府.equation: shelter 广 (170) + pay 付 (652a) = government 府
府.mnemonic: A shelter has been set up where people go to pay their taxes to the government. / Teddy tries to pay with foo yong.

禺.equation: field 田 (156a) + rump  (654c) = monkey 禺
禺.mnemonic: Out in the field the cow plonks her rump down — but there’s a squeal and she discovers she’s sat on a monkey (who is now looking rather squashed).

遇.equation: road 辶 (26a) + monkey 禺 (655a) = encounter 遇
遇.mnemonic: As a rite of passage, each young monkey has to walk along the road near the jungle, where they encounter various tests. / The first test is to sneak past the dwarf playing his ukulele without him noticing — the trick is to do it when it’s pouring with rain.

脱.equation: moon 月 (61) + convert 兌 (67b) = undress 脱
脱.mnemonic: Whenever the moon comes out it converts him into a werewolf. each time he must quickly undress before his clothes get ripped. / The ghostly giant guards his clothes and gives him a toga to wear for the duration.

聿.mnemonic: We’ve treated this as a basic building block, but if you like you can think of it as “dexterity” plus “two” (or even “dexterity” plus “criminal”) and make up a story accordingly.

建.equation: stride 廴 (657b) + write with brush 聿 (657a) = build 建
建.mnemonic: On the building site the foreman strides about writing on the ground with a brush, to mark out where they should build the walls. / Two dwarf building inspectors come round to check there’ll be enough room for the generator.

健.equation: person 亻 (13a) + build 建 (657) = strong 健
健.mnemonic: Harry is helping to build the floors of the new building, which have to be strong enough to take a lot of weight. / The two dwarf building inspectors will try to find fault with the floor of the generator room.

廷.equation: stride 廴 (657b) + ninth 壬 (551a) = court 廷
廷.mnemonic: The youngest member of the royal family strides arrogantly about the palace with a baseball bat — although he is only ninth in line to the throne. everyone is waiting for him to get his comeuppance when he gets to court.

庭.equation: shelter 广 (170) + court 廷 (659a) = courtyard 庭
庭.mnemonic: They need a shelter to house the overflowing court, so build one over the courtyard outside. / The fairy administers tincture (to ward off colds for those stuck outside in the cold weather).

挺.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + court 廷 (659a) = exceptionally 挺
挺.mnemonic: The head guard holds up his hand to stop people entering the court— only exceptionally does he let someone in. / Teddy gets in because he is carrying the king’s tincture (and is likely to spill it if he has to hold it for long).

肖.equation: small 小 (50) + moon 月 (61) = resemble 肖
肖.mnemonic: The small moon (of the planet) resembles a potato. / [No pronunciation needed]

消.equation: water 氵 (78a) + resemble 肖 (661a) = vanish 消
消.mnemonic: The enchanted water resembles lemonade and is used to make things vanish. / The two giants take a shower in it (but then keep bumping into each other as they are both now invisible).

息.equation: self 自 (94) + heart 心 (161) = stop 息
息.mnemonic: The surgeon is trying to do a heart operation on himself. 'Stop!' cry the other doctors. / The giant brings a shield to use as a stretcher (to take him to the proper operating theater).

留.equation: barred (663b) + field 田 (156a) = remain 留
留.mnemonic: Cows were escaping from the field; the farmer barred the gate to the field so that the cows who had not yet escaped would have to remain in the field. / The two fairies set out to tempt the escaped cows back with lotus leaves.

派.equation: water 氵 (78a) + tributary (664b) = group 派
派.mnemonic: The water in the tributary is particularly good and only a privileged group is allowed to fish there. / The dwarf objects to this because he thinks there’s plenty of pike for everyone.

展.equation: corpse 尸 (268a) + gown (665b) = display 展
展.mnemonic: (The well-loved princess Janet dies…) they put a gown on the corpse before putting it on display (for the mourners to file past). / Teddy makes a placard: “Prinsess Janit this way.” (Spelling is not his strong point).

畏.equation: field 田 (156a) + a skirt (665a) = respect 畏
畏.mnemonic: A farmer discovers a discarded skirt in his field (after he has chased off some youngsters). 'these young people have no respect,' he complains. / [No pronunciation needed]

喂.equation: mouth 口 (5) + respect 畏 (666a) = Hey! 喂
喂.mnemonic: (In the restaurant one diner is angrily talking to another) “Don’t eat with your mouth full — show some respect! Hey, are you listening to me?” / The dwarf head waiter storms over to throw the diners out.

候.equation: man with stick  (667c) + archery contest  (667b) = wait for 候
候.mnemonic: Who’s the man with a stick at the archery contest? he’s waiting for a new string for his bow. / His dwarf manservant must go back to the hotel to fetch one.

齐.equation: culture 文 (25) + grain stalks (579a) = tidy 齐
齐.mnemonic: In one particular culture the grain stalks must be tidy after the harvest. / The fairy then flies off to get the tribal chief to come and make his inspection.

挤.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + tidy 齐 (668) = squeeze 挤
挤.mnemonic: The mother tells her teenage daughter, “Give me a hand to tidy up all these clothes — see if you can squeeze them all into those drawers.” / Meanwhile Teddy has found a G-string (and is twanging it until it is snatched away from him).

济.equation: water 氵 (78a) + tidy 齐 (668) = bring relief 济
济.mnemonic: (After the flood) the water had receded, but before they could tidy up they needed to bring relief supplies to the stranded people. / The dwarf directed operations from his amphibian jeep.

黑.equation: windowpane  (671b) + earth 土 (54a) + fire 灬 (185a) = black 黑
黑.mnemonic: You throw earth at the upstairs window-pane to alert the occupants that their house is on fire— they run out unharmed but they’re black with soot. / The giant lets them stay in his hayloft while their house is rebuilt.

曾.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + windowpane (671b) + sun 日 (6) = used to be 曾
曾.mnemonic: The old stag looks at his horns in the windowpane as the sun goes down. they used to be magnificent, he sighs.

增.equation: earth 土 (54a) + used to be 曾 (672a) = increase 增
增.mnemonic: (A farmer looks at a handful of soil) the earth used to be arid and barren, but he’s enriched it and increased the crop yield tremendously. / [DIY pronunciation]

像.equation: person 亻 (13a) + elephant 象 (674a) = likeness 像
像.mnemonic: Harry pointed at his elephant and then at its passport photo, and said, “But — it’s a good likeness!” / The two dwarf immigration officers at Shanghai are dubious — each takes one end of the elephant but neither end looks like the passport photo …

换.equation: hand 才 (152a) + wonderful (675a) = exchange 换
换.mnemonic: (At the sculpture class) “That hand is wonderful— would you give it to the art college, in exchange for extra lessons? / The ghostly dwarf will guard it as part of our hand collection.”

钢.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + ridge 冈 (676a) = steel 钢
钢.mnemonic: You take gold up to the ridge to trade it for steel. / The giant and his gang are well known for making the best steel around.

鸟.mnemonic: There are two characters for 'bird': this one and the one we have called 'pigeon' (501a). traditionally these are said to apply to long-tailed birds and short-tailed birds respectively.

鸡.equation: right hand 又 (52) + bird 鸟 (678a) = chicken 鸡
鸡.mnemonic: The farmer’s daughter solemnly shakes hands with each bird before it leaves — no chicken is allowed to go for slaughter until she has done this. / The giant loads them onto his jeep to drive them away.

虫.mnemonic: This character also applies to other very small animals, including worms.

虽.equation: mouth 口 (5) + insect 虫 (679a) = although 虽
虽.mnemonic: The exhausted insect (imagine a cute furry caterpillar) crawls into the mouth of the cave, although he has heard that it is haunted. / The ghostly giant, who guards the cave, rushes out brandishing his saber (until he realises that the poor, frightened caterpillar is not a threat).

迎.equation: road 辶 (26a) + aspire (682b) = greet 迎
迎.mnemonic: The road aspired to be declared a Boulevard, so greeted each car effusively to ingratiate itself to everyone. / DIY pronunciation

氏.mnemonic: You can probably see 'vise' and 'pile of earth' here; feel free to make up a story for this if you like.

纸.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + surname 氏 (683a) = paper 纸
纸.mnemonic: It is customary when finishing a piece of embroidery to use thread to sign your surname at the bottom — but it is best to work out the design on paper first. / Teddy jumps onto the embroidery frame (using it as a trampoline), and cries out, “Geronimo!”

氐.equation: surname 氏 (683a) + a drop 丶 (22a) = settle down 氐
氐.mnemonic: Her fiancé has been a playboy, so she will only marry him if he will sign his surname in a drop of his own blood, to swear that he is now ready to settle down.

低.equation: person 亻 (13a) + settle down 氐 (684a) = low 低
低.mnemonic: Harry plans to settle down somewhere quiet where he can keep a low profile. / He finds the perfect place in the corner of the giant’s estate and the giant kindly gives him the deeds so that he will never have to leave.

永.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + water 水 (523) = forever 永
永.mnemonic: Put a drop of this elixir in your drinking water and you will live forever. / Teddy asks, 'Will it keep me looking young?' and takes a swig.

泳.equation: water 氵 (78a) + forever 永 (685) = swim 泳
泳.mnemonic: She wanted to stay in the water forever, now that she had learnt to swim. / Teddy said, “You’re never too young to learn!” as he jumped in, feet first.

族.equation: banner (687a) + arrow 矢 (490a) = clan 族
族.mnemonic: The African tribe have a banner with an arrow painted on it, as the symbol of their clan (to match the arrows they have painted on their faces). / They asks the fairy to fly aloft with it to scare off the neighboring Zulus.

游.equation: water 氵 (78a) + roam (688a) = tour 游
游.mnemonic: The sailing enthusiast said, “We love roaming about on the water, and it’s an easy way for us to tour the world — and it has the added benefit that we can swim whenever we want to. / We even get the fairy to come along to give us yoga lessons on board.”

冬.equation: pursue 夂 (506a) + ice crystals (246a) = winter 冬
冬.mnemonic: A keen photographer likes to pursue unusual ice crystals— although he can only indulge this hobby in winter. / The giant brings his donkey along to carry things (in winter the donkey is free from his summer job of giving rides on the beach).

疼.equation: illness 疒 (510b) + winter 冬 (689) = ache 疼
疼.mnemonic: The illness doing the rounds that winter caused various parts of the body to ache. / The fairy could diagnose it by looking at the color of people’s tongues.

图.equation: enclosed 囗 (24a) + winter 冬 (689) = diagram 图
图.mnemonic: The planned winter garden is enclosed by a wall, and attached to the wall is a diagram showing the planned layout. / The fairy flies around putting toothpaste on the trees so that people can see what it will look like in the snow.

春.equation: held in the hands (692a) + sun 曰 (6) = springtime 春
春.mnemonic: (There is a statue of the king in the palace gardens) When the sun gets high enough so that it looks as if the statue is holding the sun in his hands, that’s the official start of spring. / [DIY pronunciation].

秋.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + fire 火 (181) = autumn 秋
秋.mnemonic: They set fire to the stubble in the grain field, as they do every autumn. / Down-wind, the two giants are choking on the smoke (as they rush to take in their washing before it gets dirty in the smoke).

夏.equation: thumb tack (40a) + eye 目 (93) + pursue 夂 (506a) = summer 夏
夏.mnemonic: A legendary old fish, who has never been caught, has old fishhooks in his leathery skin, and even a thumb tack near his eye. The local fishermen pursue it all summer. / But in the end they resort to hiring the two dwarves with their shark fishing boat (one drives the boat and the other handles the lines on deck).

享.equation: tall (239a) + child 子 (17) = enjoy 享
享.mnemonic: You would think that the tall child would enjoy basketball (but no — it’s no challenge and he gets bored).

熟.equation: cooked  (696b) + fire 灬 (185a) = familiar 熟
熟.mnemonic: The boy scouts cooked food on the fire— but overdid it. “It looks familiar,” said the scoutmaster, bravely tucking into the charred remains. / The fairy tried it and pulled a face. “It tastes like shoe leather,” she said.

坚.equation: firm  (697b) + earth 土 (54a) = resolute 坚
坚.mnemonic: (Awards are being given for bravery after a landslide) “You both stood firm, holding back the earth and resolutely protecting the village.” / The two giants were proud that the general himself had come (to present them with medals for saving the village).

紧.equation: firm  (697b) + thread 糸 (607b) = tight 紧
紧.mnemonic: He takes a firm hold on the thread and pulls it tight. / Teddy then steps gingerly onto it and edges along (thinking that perhaps he’s not cut out for tightrope walking after all!)

览.equation: prostrate (699a) + see 见 (214) = view 览
览.mnemonic: You lie prostrate to see if you can see any mouse holes in the skirting board — you do this every time you view a room you might rent. / Teddy says, “Why not just ask the landlord?”

监.equation: prostrate (699a) + dish 皿 (700a) = supervise 监
监.mnemonic: The head chef in the palace kitchen is the only one who can prostrate himself while holding a dish and not spill the contents; which is why he gets to supervise the other chefs (rather than because of his culinary skills). / [No pronunciation needed]

蓝.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + supervise 监 (700b) = blue 蓝
蓝.mnemonic: The grass he was supposed to supervise overnight had turned blue. / This had been caused by the fairy’s magic lantern (and neither of them had noticed).

篮.equation: bamboo 竹 (321a) + supervise 监 (700b) = basket 篮
篮.mnemonic: The bamboo he has to supervise tonight is going to be made into a basket. / This is for the fairy to keep her magic lantern in (so that it won’t turn the grass blue any more!)

福.equation: sign 礻 (433a) + plenty (702a) = blessing 福
福.mnemonic: A farmer points out to his son a sign of plenty in the sky, 'It is a blessing on us.' / The fairy is promising that we will always have food to eat.

富.equation: house 宀 (91a) + plenty (702a) = wealthy 富
富.mnemonic: In that house they always have plenty, they’re really wealthy. / They even have a dwarf who brings food to them whenever they want it.

丰.mnemonic: Wheels are so abundant at the giant’s wheel shop that most of them just sit there untouched until fungus grows on them.

邦.equation: abundant 丰 (704) + city 阝 (96a) = nation 邦
邦.mnemonic: People are abundant in the city— it seems as if the whole nation is there.

帮.equation: nation 邦 (705a) + towel 巾 (427a) = help 帮
帮.mnemonic: At the Olympics, the athletes from each nation have towels with their national flags on them, which they wave if they need help. / At the opening ceremony the giant uses the Olympic torch to light the fireworks with a bang.

伟.equation: person 亻 (13a) + leather 韦 (706a) = great 伟
伟.mnemonic: Harry buys a new leather coat and feels just great. / He also got a smaller one for Teddy but it won’t go round Teddy’s waist!

圭.equation: earth 土 (54a) + earth 土 (54a) = jade block 圭
圭.mnemonic: This is an old character referring to a small rectangular jade tablet used in feudal times as a token of authority. The archaeologist dug through a layer of earth, then another layer of earth, before he found a jade block.

挂.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + jade block 圭 (708a) = hang 挂
挂.mnemonic: Running his hand over the jade block, he could feel something on the back to hang it up by. / When he hung it up, a ghostly dwarf appeared with some garlic to guard it.

封.equation: jade block 圭 (708a) + inch 寸 (104a) = seal up 封
封.mnemonic: You are sending out miniature jade blocks, an inch long, as Christmas presents, and you seal them up in little envelopes. / The giant has brought along some fungus to seal them with.

街.equation: step forward 彳 (81a) + jade block 圭 (708a) + footstep 亍 (81c) = street 街
街.mnemonic: He steps forward to pick up the jade block, but hears a footstep behind him, and he quickly looks up and down the deserted street. / Two giants appear, one at each end of the street, each armed with a jellyfish!

革.mnemonic: You’ll remember that we just met another character for “leather” (706a).

鞋.equation: leather 革 (711a) + jade block 圭 (708a) = shoe 鞋
鞋.mnemonic: The cobbler rubs the leather with a jade block to soften it up to make the best shoes. / When each pair is made, two fairies fly up and arrange them on a shelf.

双.equation: right hand 又 (52) + right hand 又 (52) = pair 双
双.mnemonic: Someone who has two right hands is shopping for gloves. 'We only sell them as a pair,' all the shops tell him. / 'Try the ghostly giant of Shangri-La. He looks after people who have special requirements.'

炼.equation: fire 火 (181) + select (713a) = smelt 炼
炼.mnemonic: The fire had to be fed with carefully selected wood so that it could be used to smelt the iron. / It took two dwarves to lift the cauldron of lentil soup for the workers at the ironworks.

练.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + select (713a) = train 练
练.mnemonic: You must learn how to select the correct thread if you want to train to become a dressmaker. / Two dwarves come round with a selection of things to test the students’ skills, including lentils (in place of sequins) to sew onto dresses.

段.equation: ivy (715a) + strike 殳 (169a) = piece 段
段.mnemonic: Vandals attack the ivy— they strike it and leave only a single piece in place. / The king calls in the ghostly dwarf to guard his special dandelion (so that it doesn’t meet the same fate).

锻.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + piece 段 (715) = forge 锻
锻.mnemonic: The king donates a gold piece to be forged into a gift. / He gives it to the ghostly dwarf as a reward for guarding his special dandelion.

屯.mnemonic: You might prefer to think of this as 'seven' plus 'pit'; if so, feel free to make up a story accordingly.

顿.equation: store 屯 (717a) + page 页 (477) = session 顿
顿.mnemonic: You are writing a book and store the pages you have produced at the end of each session. / The ghostly dwarf who guards the pages thinks it’s such drivel that he keeps a dunce’s cap on top of them as a paperweight.

烦.equation: fire 火 (181) + page 页 (477) = bother 烦
烦.mnemonic: He’s trying to light a fire with a page torn from the newspaper — but keeps burning his fingers on the matches. “Bother!” he cries. “Bother, bother, bother!” / The fairy offers to fan the flames with her wings.

约.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + ladle 勺 (23b) = approximate 约
约.mnemonic: In the palace kitchens they hang a ladle from a thread and use it as a makeshift pendulum (to time the boiled eggs) — a traditional method, but only approximate!

药.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + approximate 约 (720a) = medicine 药
药.mnemonic: The sick cow has eaten poisonous plants which looked approximately like grass, and so now has to take some medicine. / But as the dwarf assistant reaches for the medicine, the cow stands on his foot and he yells out, “Yeow!”

争.mnemonic: (In the pickpocket’s master-class) The expert demonstrates his skill by bowing to the volunteer and, with great dexterity, simultaneously picking his pocket without being noticed. 'I contend, ladies and gentlemen,' he says, 'that this is the ultimate skill.'

净.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + contend 争 (721a) = clean 净
净.mnemonic: The tour guide in the arctic points to the ice and says, “I contend that this is the most pure and clean water in the world.” / The dwarf in charge of the husky sledge for transport jingles the sledge bells impatiently (he’s heard it all before and wants to move on).

静.equation: blue-green 青 (335) + contend 争 (721a) = calm 静
静.mnemonic: (Two people are arguing over the color of a book in the library) "I contend it's blue!" "I contend it's green!" "Calm down!" says their friend. / The dwarf librarian jingles his bell for quiet.

朝.equation: daybreak (723a) + moon 月 (61) = facing 朝
朝.mnemonic: (During the Apollo missions) At daybreak the moon had set in the USA, so they had to swap to an Australian ground station which was now facing the moon. / The fairy had produced chowder to wake the Australians up.

卓.equation: fortune teller 卜 (42a) + early 早 (633) = eminent 卓
卓.mnemonic: The fortune teller got to her tent early that day, to prepare for an eminent client (picture her polishing up her crystal ball, etc.).

掉.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + eminent 卓 (724a) = drop 掉
掉.mnemonic: The eminent surgeon holds out his hand for the scalpel, but promptly drops it! / The two dwarf clinical assessors exchange glances — they have doubts about the surgeon’s competence (however eminent he is).

桌.equation: fortune teller 卜 (42a) + sun 日 (6) + tree 木 (10a) = table 桌
桌.mnemonic: The fortune teller sets up her stall out of the sun under the shade of the tree, and puts her crystal ball on a table. / The ghostly giant watches over her to dissuade anyone from making a joke of her.

敢.equation: earring (726a) + tap 攵 (190a) = dare 敢
敢.mnemonic: (At the pirates’ lair in the creek, the door is in the shape of a giant pirate’s head) You tap on the earring to gain entry — and a voice booms out, “Who dares to enter here?” / But actually it’s only Teddy on a gantry behind the door (speaking into a megaphone).

休.equation: person 亻 (13a) + tree 木 (10a) = leisure 休
休.mnemonic: Harry always goes to sit high up in the tree whenever he has leisure time. / Today he’s watching the two giants put on a show.

退.equation: road 辶 (26a) + stubborn 艮 (281a) = retreat 退
退.mnemonic: On the single-track road the two drivers sit, both stubbornly waiting for the other to retreat. / The ghostly dwarf, who guards the stretch of road, gets out his tape measure (to see who would have to reverse the least distance).

腿.equation: moon 月 (61) + retreat 退 (728) = leg 腿
腿.mnemonic: The Apollo astronauts had finished their moonwalk. They had to leave the moon and make a rapid retreat into their spacecraft (before their oxygen ran out), but one trapped his leg in the airlock. / Back inside the lunar module the ghostly teddy who looked after the astronauts measured the leg with a tape measure (to see if it was all there!)

豆.mnemonic: The pilgrims piled up their gifts at the feet of the statue — each pilgrim had brought a tin of holy beans. / [No pronunciation needed]

短.equation: arrow 矢 (490a) + bean 豆 (730a) = brief 短
短.mnemonic: William Tell could shoot an arrow to hit a bean on his son’s head, after only a brief glance to judge the distance. / The ghostly teddy who protects the son suggests putting the bean on a pile of dandruff (to give him more room for error)!

吴.equation: mouth 口 (5) + heaven 天 (76) = shout out 吴
吴.mnemonic: Turn your mouth to the heavens and shout out your message, the preacher tells his congregation. / [No pronunciation needed].

误.equation: words 讠 (67c) + shout out 吴 (731a) = mistake 误
误.mnemonic: Monks are poring over a piece of calligraphy. Looking over the beautiful words, they shout out whenever they spot a mistake. / The dwarf, who wrote it, is wounded by their criticism and goes off in a huff.

采.equation: claw (365b) + tree 木 (10a) = pick 采
采.mnemonic: With her claw-like hand the old crone reaches up to the tree to pick the apple.

彩.equation: pick 采 (732a) + hairs 彡 (573a) = colorful 彩
彩.mnemonic: The hairdresser picks out individual hairs to highlight, producing a colorful effect. / Teddy wants a whole new style!

菜.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + pick 采 (732a) = vegetable 菜
菜.mnemonic: The greengrocer knows the best grass to pick, and uses it to display his vegetables to best effect. / But the dwarf owner always dictates the exact style of the display.

釆.equation: beret (89a) + rice 米 (334) = claw marks 釆
釆.mnemonic: The palaeontologist fills his beret with rice and pours it into the fossilized claw marks to measure their volume — each claw mark takes up a whole beret-full of rice. (So it must have been quite a dinosaur!) / [No pronunciation needed]

番.equation: claw marks 釆 (734a) + field 田 (156a) = a time 番
番.mnemonic: Picture huge claw marks scarring the field. They have tried time after time to catch the beast which makes them, without success.

播.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + a time 番 (734b) = sow 播
播.mnemonic: He has to dip his hand, time after time, into the bag as he walks up and down sowing the seeds. / The giant is finding this boring (even though he can do it in half the time it would take most people …)

习.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + blade (231a) = practice 习
习.mnemonic: He uses an ice blade to practice his fencing skills. / The fairy conjures up an insulated sheath to keep it in.

羽.equation: practice 习 (735) + practice 习 (735) = feather 羽
羽.mnemonic: You would have to practice and practice before you could balance a feather on your nose (without sneezing!) / [No pronunciation needed]

翻.equation: a time 番 (734b) + feather 羽 (736a) = turn over 翻
翻.mnemonic: Every time the feather (on the ground) moves in the breeze, it turns over. / The giant is creating the breeze with his fan (on a nearby hill).

译.equation: words 讠 (67c) + watch over (737a) = translate 译
译.mnemonic: (A crime scene: the dead man, a foreigner, has managed to write something on the floor beside him) The police have to watch over the words until someone arrives to translate them. / The dwarf police inspector is impatient — he wants to e-mail for a translation instead.

余.equation: tent 人 (19b) + work 工 (147) + small 小 (50) = surplus 余
余.mnemonic: We have cheated a bit here by breaking the lower part of this character into 'work' and 'small'. The tent they are working in is very small, so you can tell which one it is by the pile of surplus equipment outside.

除.equation: mound 阝 (96a) + surplus 余 (738a) = remove 除
除.mnemonic: The mound of surplus office equipment has grown so big that the council has told them to remove it. / The fairy wants to choose some things for herself first.

茶.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + tent 人 (19b) + tree 木 (10a) = tea 茶
茶.mnemonic: They set up their tent on the grass underneath the tree and the first thing they do is make some tea. / The fairy relaxes, saying, “This is a nice cup of cha.”

奇.equation: big 大 (47) + may 可 (140) = strange 奇
奇.mnemonic: The extra big may pole is for the most senior students; it’s really strange to see them dancing round it like small children. / [No pronunciation needed]

骑.equation: horse 马 (44) + strange 奇 (740a) = ride 骑
骑.mnemonic: That horse may be very strange-looking, but it’s wonderful to ride. / The fairy streaks along on it, explaining, “That’s because it’s half cheetah.”

椅.equation: tree 木 (10a) + strange 奇 (740a) = chair 椅
椅.mnemonic: The tree is a strange shape and part of it has been carved into a chair. / Picture Teddy sitting on it, munching his chocolate Easter egg.

寄.equation: house 宀 (91a) + strange 奇 (740a) = send 寄
寄.mnemonic: “The house is really strange,” she pleads, “please don’t send me there. / The dwarf who lives there is weird, even if he is a genius.”

昔.equation: bacon rashers (625a) + sun 日 (6) = the old days 昔
昔.mnemonic: Leave the bacon rashers out in the sun— that’s how we used to cook in the old days, my boy.

错.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + the old days 昔 (743a) = mistake 错
错.mnemonic: The sailors were paid in gold in the old days— which was a mistake as it attracted pirates. / So the ghostly dwarf stowed away on the ship to guard the gold.

借.equation: person 亻 (13a) + the old days 昔 (743a) = borrow 借
借.mnemonic: In the old days Harry had nothing, and had to borrow everything. / He became jealous of the two pawnbroker dwarves.

散.equation: midnight feast (745a) + tap 攵 (190a) = disperse 散
散.mnemonic: At the midnight feast in the forest they are eating hungrily when the lookout taps out a warning on the tree and they all disperse quickly. / When the dwarf arrives, all he finds is a pile of abandoned sandwiches.

船.equation: boat 舟 (680a) + ravine (746a) = ship 船
船.mnemonic: They sailed the boat down the ravine until it opened out, and there on the lake was a beautiful golden ship. / Beyond the lake the ghostly fairy waited to guide them through the next dangerous channel.

铅.equation: gold 钅 (278a) + ravine (746a) = lead 铅
铅.mnemonic: The pirates store their gold in the ravine, because there (in the gloom) it looks like lead. / [DIY pronunciation]

蛋.equation: bolt of cloth 疋 (748a) + insect 虫 (679a) = egg 蛋
蛋.mnemonic: The bolt of cloth has been invaded by insects, who have laid tiny white eggs in it. / The dwarf (a market trader selling the cloth) tells his customers it’s only dandruff.

林.equation: tree 木 (10a) + tree 木 (10a) = a wood 林
林.mnemonic: It takes two trees to make a wood (and three to make a forest).

楚.equation: a wood 林 (749a) + bolt of cloth 疋 (748a) = clear 楚
楚.mnemonic: For the picnic they took a bolt of cloth to the woods and had to clear a space to spread it out. / When they opened the picnic basket, they found Teddy inside chewing on a sausage!

麻.equation: shelter 广 (170) + a wood 林 (749a) = hemp 麻
麻.mnemonic: They build a shelter in the wood to hide their hemp crop. / When it is ready the fairy takes it to market.

嘛.equation: mouth 口 (5) + hemp 麻 (750) = surely 嘛
嘛.mnemonic: You need some hemp in your mouth— you'll surely feel the effects. / Let's send the robot to get some from the market— nobody will suspect him!

隶.equation: dexterity  (312a) + water 水 (523) = subordinate 隶
隶.mnemonic: On the oil rig out at sea, fixing the pipes under water calls for dexterity, but it’s tedious work so it’s a job that is always given to the subordinates.

康.equation: shelter 广 (170) + subordinate 隶 (752a) = good health 康
康.mnemonic: The manager wants to build a shelter for his subordinates to keep them in good health. / The giant workman comes to build it — with a kangaroo assistant who keeps the tools in her pouch.

录.equation: snout  (267a) + water 水 (523) = record 录
录.mnemonic: Each time the pig dips his snout in the water, the naturalist excitedly records it. / His dwarf assistant thinks this obsession is ludicrous.

绿.equation: thread 纟 (233a) + record 录 (753) = green 绿
绿.mnemonic: Thread-work (like the Bayeux tapestry) is often used to record important events or stories. Picture the story of Robin Hood being stitched — and they keep running out of green thread. / The dwarf had to go out in the pouring rain to get luminous green thread (to do the night scenes).

旅.mnemonic: The brigade are looking for their banner as they are about to travel (and want to fly it over their new barracks). / They find Teddy taking a bath in the pouring rain with a loofah (and he’s pinched the banner to use as a bath towel).

俞.mnemonic: He combined two canoes together to make a catamaran. / [No pronunciation needed].

愉.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + catamaran 俞 (756a) = happy 愉
愉.mnemonic: He had set his heart on a catamaran so is really happy when he finally gets one. / The fairy comes out in the pouring rain to write the name “Eureka” on the side of the boat with her wand (which fortunately conjures up waterproof paint).

输.equation: car 车 (83) + catamaran 俞 (756a) = lose 输
输.mnemonic: The car has a race with the catamaran but in the end it loses. / Halfway round it had crashed into the giant’s shoe.

凉.equation: ice 冫 (360a) + capital 京 (239b) = cool 凉
凉.mnemonic: Ice covers the capital and finally cools it down after the sweltering summer. / The two fairies, who had been languishing in the heat, come to life again.

谅.equation: words 讠 (67c) + capital 京 (239b) = forgive 谅
谅.mnemonic: The peace activists have painted words on walls all over the capital: 'Forgive your enemies' is the message. / The two dwarves object to the uncouth language (and take it in turns to remove the words).

宛.equation: house 宀 (91a) + turn in one’s sleep (760a) = winding 宛
宛.mnemonic: Once again the house had appeared in his dreams, and he turned in his sleep with a groan, knowing that the endless winding path would be next.

碗.equation: stone 石 (115a) + winding 宛 (760b) = bowl 碗
碗.mnemonic: The children roll small stones down the winding marble-run and the stones clatter into a bowl at the bottom. / DIY pronunciation

香.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + sun 日 (6) = fragrant 香
香.mnemonic: The grain drying in the sun gives off a fragrant smell. / The two giants from Shanghai agree that they have never smelled anything like it.

奴.equation: women 女 (15) + right hand 又 (52) = slave 奴
奴.mnemonic: When the woman shakes hands with him he notices the mark on her wrist which marks her out as a slave. / [No pronunciation needed]

努.equation: slave 奴 (762a) + power 力 (14) = exert oneself 努
努.mnemonic: They used slave power to build the pyramids — the foremen made sure that the slaves exerted themselves to the full. / Nowadays it’s just a ruin and Teddy sits on it eating his noodles.

焦.equation: pigeon 隹 (501a) + fire 灬 (185a) = scorched 焦
焦.mnemonic: The pigeon sat by the fire for too long and scorched his tail feathers.

蕉.equation: grass 艹 (218a) + scorched 焦 (763a) = banana 蕉
蕉.mnemonic: The grass had been scorched by the fire, but the bananas on the trees were undamaged. / The two giants (who owned the banana plantation) had been off at a jousting match (and were relieved that their crop was alright).

唐.equation: shelter 广 (170) + rude  (764a) = Tang 唐
唐.mnemonic: They had to build a shelter to hide the rude statues from sensitive eyes, particularly those from the Tang dynasty.

糖.equation: rice 米 (334) + Tang 唐 (764b) = sugar 糖
糖.mnemonic: Rice from the Tang period was as sweet as sugar. / But only the fairy can conjure up Tang rice nowadays.

酸.equation: whisky bottle 酉 (102a) + hesitate (765a) = sour 酸
酸.mnemonic: The alcoholic picked up the whisky bottle but hesitated — the last drink had tasted very sour. / The ghostly giant (from Alcoholics Anonymous) had tampered with his liquor by steeping his sandals in it!

委.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + women 女 (15) = entrust 委
委.mnemonic: The first bag of grain from the harvest was carried proudly on her head by the woman who had been entrusted to present it to the king.

矮.equation: arrow 矢 (490a) + entrust 委 (766a) = short 矮
矮.mnemonic: The apprentice Cupid takes the arrows he has been entrusted with. For his first solo mission he is only allowed to bewitch short people! / Teddy skips about marking likely targets with iodine to identify them.

囱.equation: a drop 丶 (22a) + enclosed 囗 (24a) + pursue 夂 (506a) = chimney 囱
囱.mnemonic: In some typefaces “pursue” gets changed to a leaned-over version of “criminal”. At the zoo, the mother monkey feels a drop of rain so pursues the baby monkey round the enclosure— but the baby monkey climbs up the chimney out of reach.

窗.equation: hole 穴 (616a) + chimney 囱 (767a) = window 窗
窗.mnemonic: Imagine making a hole in the chimney above your fireplace and installing a small window (so that you can watch the smoke going up it — a nice talking point for your visitors). / [DIY pronunciation]

寒.equation: block up (768b) + ice crystals (246a) = freezing 寒
寒.mnemonic: All the pipes in the house are blocked up with ice crystals in the freezing weather. / The fairy pulls lengths of lagging from her handbag (to wrap round the pipes).

赛.equation: block up (768b) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = compete 赛
赛.mnemonic: They had to block up a big trench on the beach with sea shells so that they could compete in the sand-yacht races. / The dwarf sounded the siren to start each race.

扁.equation: door 户 (269) + old book (770a) = flat 扁
扁.mnemonic: They propped the door open with an old book. But it slammed in the wind and knocked the book flat.

遍.equation: road 辶 (26a) + flat 扁 (770b) = everywhere 遍
遍.mnemonic: In holland the roads are all flat— everywhere, there are no hills at all. / Two dwarves sit on a bench bemoaning this (they can’t get a good view anywhere).

篇.equation: bamboo (321a) + flat 扁 (770b) = piece of paper 篇
篇.mnemonic: Imagine pounding slivers of green bamboo flat and joining them together to make a primitive piece of paper. / The two giants each make you something to write with — one makes a pen and the other a pencil so you can try them out.

昌.equation: sun 日 (6) + sun 日 (6) = prosperous 昌
昌.mnemonic: A couple consult an astrologer who tells them, “Looking at your two sun signs I see that you will be prosperous.” / [No pronunciation needed]

唱.equation: mouth 口 (5) + prosperous 昌 (772a) = sing 唱
唱.mnemonic: At the mouths of their caves the prosperous people can afford to employ servants to sing an appropriate song when someone approaches (instead of having doorbells). / DIY pronunciation

冒.equation: hood (773a) + eye 目 (93) = risk 冒
冒.mnemonic: The thief is so well-known that even with his hood pulled down over his eyes he runs the risk of people recognizing him. / The dwarf security guard spots him anyway, as he recognizes the thief's mouth.

帽.equation: towel 巾 (427a) + risk 冒 (773) = hat 帽
帽.mnemonic: (At the boxing match) One trainer threw a towel into the ring, as there was a risk his boxer could get badly injured — the other trainer threw his hat in the air triumphantly. / Unfortunately the hat hit the dwarf referee in the mouth (which started another fight).

辰.equation: cliff 厂 (149) + one 一 (1) + a skirt (665a) = heavenly body 辰
辰.mnemonic: The unicorn, standing on the cliff, sees a skirt round one of the stars. “It’s a planet with rings, or some other heavenly body.”

晨.equation: sun 日 (6) + heavenly body 辰 (775a) = morning 晨
晨.mnemonic: When the sun rises and the other heavenly bodies dim, it is morning. / [DIY pronunciation]

毌.mnemonic: Writing this one often catches people out! It is derived from 'mother' (Character 127) and has the same 'sloping' appearance. Contrast the stroke order with, for example, the stroke order for 'field' (156a).

贯.equation: pierced 毌 (776a) + sea shell 贝 (477a) = pierce 贯
贯.mnemonic: Finding a pierced sea shell on the beach, she thinks, “I could pierce lots of shells and string them together to make necklaces!”

惯.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + pierce 贯 (776b) = habitual 惯
惯.mnemonic: Cupid flies around the neighborhood piercing the hearts of the habitual criminals (in the hope that this will reform them). / The ghostly dwarf, who protects the neighborhood, sits on a gantry, directing who to shoot at.

尺.mnemonic: This time the ruler is not a monarch, but a ruler for measuring things. a 'meter', the unit of length, is 公尺 (gōngchǐ) although a more colloquial character for meter is 米 (mǐ, Character 334).

迟.equation: road 辶 (26a) + ruler 尺 (777a) = late 迟
迟.mnemonic: Some small boys are playing with a ruler on the road (measuring out a hopscotch pitch) — and they’re going to be late for school. / The fairy chases them off, and wipes the chalk marks off the road with a chapati.

尧.equation: high up  (778a) + pedestal 兀 (301a) = Chieftain Yao 尧
尧.mnemonic: Yao was a legendary emperor who lived a little over 4000 years ago. High up on a pedestal stands a statue of the legendary Chieftain Yao (who was in fact so legendary that he had his own Chinese character!) / [No pronunciation needed]

烧.equation: fire 火 (181) + Chieftain Yao 尧 (778b) = burn 烧
烧.mnemonic: The villagers lit a fire beside Chieftain Yao’s statue, but it got out of hand and started to burn the statue and its surroundings. / Luckily the giant had been taking a shower, and directed the spray at the fire to put it out.

既.equation: stubborn 艮 (281a) + swallow (779a) = since 既
既.mnemonic: One of the strokes of “stubborn” is missing here (see 281a). This is the form which “stubborn” takes whenever there is something else to its right in a composite character. The elderly man stubbornly refused to swallow the pills, saying, “Since I’ve never swallowed a pill, I’m not going to start now.”

概.equation: tree 木 (10a) + since 既 (779b) = summary 概
概.mnemonic: “The tree has been there since ancient times, and must be saved,” the summary stated (although there was a huge report with all the details). / The dwarf will be in charge of attaching guy ropes to support the tree.

呀.equation: mouth 口 (5) + tooth 牙 (780a) = oh! 呀
呀.mnemonic: The dentist looks into the mouth, sees a colored tooth and cries, “Oh!” / The giant had tied colored yarn round the tooth when he was trying to pull it out.

穿.equation: hole 穴 (616a) + tooth 牙 (780a) = penetrate 穿
穿.mnemonic: There was a hole in the tooth so the dentist had to penetrate it with his drill. / The ghostly giant had protected his friend’s root channel until he got to the dentist. (We know you normally say “root canal”, but work with us here!)

異.equation: field 田 (156a) + collectively 共 (625) = different 異
異.mnemonic: Imagine each member of your family owning a field, and farming them collectively, even though each person has a different crop.

戴.equation: different 異 (782a) + wound (782b) = wear 戴
戴.mnemonic: In the hospital the patients with different types of wound have to wear different colored gowns (a red gown for arm wounds, a green gown for leg wounds, etc.) / The dwarf grumbles as he has to dye all the gowns.

乘.equation: grain 禾 (126a) + north 北 (317) = ride 乘
乘.mnemonic: An unusual combination, where 'north' is fitted around 'grain' rather than just being placed above, below, or to the side of it. This is to ride in a vehicle (remember that in Character 740 we had another sort of 'ride', which involved sitting astride something, like a horse or a bicycle). The north wind blows through the vast fields of grain— you gaze at this vista as you ride on the train through the Canadian prairies.

剩.equation: ride 乘 (783a) + knife 刂 (74a) = residue 剩
剩.mnemonic: Imagine riding on a train, using a knife to make sandwiches for your lunch — from the residue of last night’s supper. / [DIY pronunciation]

官.equation: house 宀 (91a) + legion (784a) = an official 官
官.mnemonic: The house to be allocated to each member of the legion is decided by the town official. / [No pronunciation needed]

馆.equation: food 饣 (288a) + an official 官 (784b) = public building 馆
馆.mnemonic: After an earthquake, food is handed out by an official at the public building. / The ghostly teddy dressed up as Gandhi is supposed to guard the food but larks about instead.

曼.equation: sun 日 (6) + net 罒 (785a) + right hand 又 (52) = graceful 曼
曼.mnemonic: (The princess comes to award national fisherman’s prizes to the fishermen in the village) They drape a net over poles to shield her from the sun while she shakes hands with the winners; they are all captivated by how graceful she is. / [No pronunciation needed]

慢.equation: heart 忄 (339a) + graceful 曼 (785b) = slow 慢
慢.mnemonic: He has set his heart on the graceful woman — but he’s just too slow to attract her attention. / The dwarf (running a dating agency) suggests that he learns to play a mandolin to serenade her.

罢.equation: net 罒 (785a) + go 去 (86) = stop 罢
罢.mnemonic: You watch the fishermen pick up their nets to go fishing — but you see one of the nets has snagged on a rock and shout, “Stop!” / [No pronunciation needed]

摆.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + stop 罢 (786a) = arrange 摆
摆.mnemonic: The child reaches out her hand to the flowers and her mother calls out, “Stop right there, I’ve spent hours arranging those!” / But just then Teddy comes by on his bike and crashes into them anyway.

舛.equation: evening 夕 (69a) + surpass (168a) = opposing 舛
舛.mnemonic: The evening’s debate surpassed everyone’s expectations: the highlight was the speech given by the opposing team’s captain.

舞.equation: dancing  (787b) + opposing 舛 (787a) = dance 舞
舞.mnemonic: At the dancing competition the opposing teams compete to produce the best dance. / Teddy retires wounded in the first round (when someone stands on his paw with a stiletto heel).

跳.equation: foot 足 (280) + omen 兆 (788a) = jump 跳
跳.mnemonic: You find a bird’s foot in the road — this is an omen and you know that what you have to do is jump over it. / Two dwarf bureaucrats from the town hall arrive to make sure the omen sighting is documented in duplicate.

辅.equation: car 车 (83) + just now 甫 (789a) = assist 辅
辅.mnemonic: (Talking to the police after your car has been stolen) “The car was here just now, officer, but I don’t know where it’s gone. I’d like to be able to assist you, really I would.” / But Teddy has been fooling around (and has hidden the car in your garage, but then gives himself away by hitting the horn).

傅.equation: person 亻 (13a) + spread over (790a) = teacher 傅
傅.mnemonic: Harry spread the icing carefully over the cake, to impress his cookery teacher. / The dwarf teacher thinks teaching food classes is beneath him (and would much rather be teaching car maintenance).

并.equation: horns 丷 (65a) + open 开 (85) = actually 并
并.mnemonic: The old bull is trying to open the gate by hooking his horns under it to pull it open; the (smart-ass) young bull comes across and says, “Actually, all you need to do is push it.”

瓶.equation: actually 并 (791a) + tile 瓦 (791b) = bottle 瓶
瓶.mnemonic: (The archaeologist is pontificating during a lecture) “This may look like a roof tile, but actually it’s a fragment from a huge earthenware bottle.” / In the audience, the fairy whispers to her neighbor, “Actually, it’s a bit of old pot I painted pink.”

祭.equation: Druid (792a) + sign 示 (430) = worship 祭
祭.mnemonic: At Stonehenge again, the Druid waits for a sign (to appear in the sky) before the worship can begin.

察.equation: house 宀 (91a) + worship 祭 (792b) = inspect 察
察.mnemonic: If you want to set up your house as a place of worship, you have to get it inspected.

擦.equation: hand 扌 (152a) + inspect 察 (792c) = wipe 擦
擦.mnemonic: The doctor asks for help: “Give me a hand to inspect this patient — we’ll have to give him a good wipe down first though.” / He pulls back the curtain to reveal ... the giant standing there starkers!

末.mnemonic: Here the first stroke is longer than the second; compare this with 'not yet' (64a). The character means an extremity, the tip or end of something.

袜.equation: clothes 衤 (439a) + tip 末 (793a) = socks 袜
袜.mnemonic: The clothes are hung up to dry on the branches of the tree, and fitted over the tips of the branches are the socks. / The dwarf has been left to look after the baby who is crying “Waaah!” (the baby is cold: it has no clothes as they are all being washed).

兹.equation: feet (90a) + teeny weeny (794a) = jet black 兹
兹.mnemonic: The burglar has trained his pet beetle to help him — the beetle’s teeny weeny feet don’t set off the alarms, and his jet black color doesn’t show up on the security cameras.

磁.equation: stone 石 (115a) + jet black 兹 (794b) = magnet 磁
磁.mnemonic: The children had each brought a jet black stone from the beach, and they wanted to make them into fridge magnets. / The fairy tapped the stones with a staccato tap of her wand to render them magnetic.

丢.equation: beret (89a) + go 去 (86) = mislay 丢
丢.mnemonic: He was about to put his beret on and go out, but found that he had mislaid it. / The two giants (who ran the bakery shop) fashioned a temporary beret out of dough.

宴.equation: house 宀 (91a) + sun 日 (6) + women 女 (15) = banquet 宴
宴.mnemonic: The woman has to stay in the house all day, although the sun is shining, as she has to prepare for the banquet. / The dwarf has always had a yen to be a master chef (although he usually only gets to cook in the canteen) so has been allowed to cook the food.

赢.mnemonic: The final progress diagram: this completes HSK Level a. If you didn’t think you’d ever get to the end of the book — well, neither did we when we were writing it! We reckon we all deserve a break to celebrate.
